46
47/* GUC variable */
48int			bytea_output = BYTEA_OUTPUT_HEX;
49
50typedef struct varlena VarString;
51
52/*
53 * State for text_position_* functions.
54 */
55typedef struct
56{
57	pg_locale_t locale;			/* collation used for substring matching */
58	bool		is_multibyte_char_in_char;	/* need to check char boundaries? */
59	bool		greedy;			/* find longest possible substring? */
60
61	char	   *str1;			/* haystack string */
62	char	   *str2;			/* needle string */
63	int			len1;			/* string lengths in bytes */
64	int			len2;
65
66	/* Skip table for Boyer-Moore-Horspool search algorithm: */
67	int			skiptablemask;	/* mask for ANDing with skiptable subscripts */
68	int			skiptable[256]; /* skip distance for given mismatched char */
69
70	/*
71	 * Note that with nondeterministic collations, the length of the last
72	 * match is not necessarily equal to the length of the "needle" passed in.
73	 */
74	char	   *last_match;		/* pointer to last match in 'str1' */
75	int			last_match_len; /* length of last match */
76	int			last_match_len_tmp; /* same but for internal use */
77
78	/*
79	 * Sometimes we need to convert the byte position of a match to a
80	 * character position.  These store the last position that was converted,
81	 * so that on the next call, we can continue from that point, rather than
82	 * count characters from the very beginning.
83	 */
84	char	   *refpoint;		/* pointer within original haystack string */
85	int			refpos;			/* 0-based character offset of the same point */
86} TextPositionState;
87
88typedef struct
89{
90	char	   *buf1;			/* 1st string, or abbreviation original string
91								 * buf */
92	char	   *buf2;			/* 2nd string, or abbreviation strxfrm() buf */
93	int			buflen1;		/* Allocated length of buf1 */
94	int			buflen2;		/* Allocated length of buf2 */
95	int			last_len1;		/* Length of last buf1 string/strxfrm() input */
96	int			last_len2;		/* Length of last buf2 string/strxfrm() blob */
97	int			last_returned;	/* Last comparison result (cache) */
98	bool		cache_blob;		/* Does buf2 contain strxfrm() blob, etc? */
99	bool		collate_c;
100	Oid			typid;			/* Actual datatype (text/bpchar/bytea/name) */
101	hyperLogLogState abbr_card; /* Abbreviated key cardinality state */
102	hyperLogLogState full_card; /* Full key cardinality state */
103	double		prop_card;		/* Required cardinality proportion */
104	pg_locale_t locale;
105} VarStringSortSupport;
106
107/*
108 * Output data for split_text(): we output either to an array or a table.
109 * tupstore and tupdesc must be set up in advance to output to a table.
110 */
111typedef struct
112{
113	ArrayBuildState *astate;
114	Tuplestorestate *tupstore;
115	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
116} SplitTextOutputData;
117
118/*
119 * This should be large enough that most strings will fit, but small enough
120 * that we feel comfortable putting it on the stack
121 */
122#define TEXTBUFLEN		1024
123
124#define DatumGetVarStringP(X)		((VarString *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM(X))
125#define DatumGetVarStringPP(X)		((VarString *) PG_DETOAST_DATUM_PACKED(X))
126
127static int	varstrfastcmp_c(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup);
128static int	bpcharfastcmp_c(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup);
129static int	namefastcmp_c(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup);
130static int	varlenafastcmp_locale(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup);
131static int	namefastcmp_locale(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup);
132static int	varstrfastcmp_locale(char *a1p, int len1, char *a2p, int len2, SortSupport ssup);
133static Datum varstr_abbrev_convert(Datum original, SortSupport ssup);
134static bool varstr_abbrev_abort(int memtupcount, SortSupport ssup);
135static int32 text_length(Datum str);
136static text *text_catenate(text *t1, text *t2);
137static text *text_substring(Datum str,
138							int32 start,
139							int32 length,
140							bool length_not_specified);
141static int	pg_mbcharcliplen_chars(const char *mbstr, int len, int limit);
142static text *text_overlay(text *t1, text *t2, int sp, int sl);
143static int	text_position(text *t1, text *t2, Oid collid);
144static void text_position_setup(text *t1, text *t2, Oid collid, TextPositionState *state);
145static bool text_position_next(TextPositionState *state);
146static char *text_position_next_internal(char *start_ptr, TextPositionState *state);
147static char *text_position_get_match_ptr(TextPositionState *state);
148static int	text_position_get_match_pos(TextPositionState *state);
149static void text_position_cleanup(TextPositionState *state);
150static void check_collation_set(Oid collid);
151static int	text_cmp(text *arg1, text *arg2, Oid collid);
152static bytea *bytea_catenate(bytea *t1, bytea *t2);
153static bytea *bytea_substring(Datum str,
154							  int S,
155							  int L,
156							  bool length_not_specified);
157static bytea *bytea_overlay(bytea *t1, bytea *t2, int sp, int sl);
158static void appendStringInfoText(StringInfo str, const text *t);
159static bool split_text(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, SplitTextOutputData *tstate);
160static void split_text_accum_result(SplitTextOutputData *tstate,
161									text *field_value,
162									text *null_string,
163									Oid collation);
164static text *array_to_text_internal(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, ArrayType *v,
165									const char *fldsep, const char *null_string);
166static StringInfo makeStringAggState(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo);
167static bool text_format_parse_digits(const char **ptr, const char *end_ptr,
168									 int *value);
169static const char *text_format_parse_format(const char *start_ptr,
170											const char *end_ptr,
171											int *argpos, int *widthpos,
172											int *flags, int *width);
173static void text_format_string_conversion(StringInfo buf, char conversion,
174										  FmgrInfo *typOutputInfo,
175										  Datum value, bool isNull,
176										  int flags, int width);
177static void text_format_append_string(StringInfo buf, const char *str,
178									  int flags, int width);
179
180
181/*****************************************************************************
182 *	 CONVERSION ROUTINES EXPORTED FOR USE BY C CODE							 *
183 *****************************************************************************/
184
185/*
186 * cstring_to_text
187 *
188 * Create a text value from a null-terminated C string.
189 *
190 * The new text value is freshly palloc'd with a full-size VARHDR.
191 */
192text *
193cstring_to_text(const char *s)
194{
195	return cstring_to_text_with_len(s, strlen(s));
196}
197
198/*
199 * cstring_to_text_with_len
200 *
201 * Same as cstring_to_text except the caller specifies the string length;
202 * the string need not be null_terminated.
203 */
204text *
205cstring_to_text_with_len(const char *s, int len)
206{
207	text	   *result = (text *) palloc(len + VARHDRSZ);
208
209	SET_VARSIZE(result, len + VARHDRSZ);
210	memcpy(VARDATA(result), s, len);
211
212	return result;
213}
214
215/*
216 * text_to_cstring
217 *
218 * Create a palloc'd, null-terminated C string from a text value.
219 *
220 * We support being passed a compressed or toasted text value.
221 * This is a bit bogus since such values shouldn't really be referred to as
222 * "text *", but it seems useful for robustness.  If we didn't handle that
223 * case here, we'd need another routine that did, anyway.
224 */
225char *
226text_to_cstring(const text *t)
227{
228	/* must cast away the const, unfortunately */
229	text	   *tunpacked = pg_detoast_datum_packed(unconstify(text *, t));
230	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(tunpacked);
231	char	   *result;
232
233	result = (char *) palloc(len + 1);
234	memcpy(result, VARDATA_ANY(tunpacked), len);
235	result[len] = '\0';
236
237	if (tunpacked != t)
238		pfree(tunpacked);
239
240	return result;
241}
242
243/*
244 * text_to_cstring_buffer
245 *
246 * Copy a text value into a caller-supplied buffer of size dst_len.
247 *
248 * The text string is truncated if necessary to fit.  The result is
249 * guaranteed null-terminated (unless dst_len == 0).
250 *
251 * We support being passed a compressed or toasted text value.
252 * This is a bit bogus since such values shouldn't really be referred to as
253 * "text *", but it seems useful for robustness.  If we didn't handle that
254 * case here, we'd need another routine that did, anyway.
255 */
256void
257text_to_cstring_buffer(const text *src, char *dst, size_t dst_len)
258{
259	/* must cast away the const, unfortunately */
260	text	   *srcunpacked = pg_detoast_datum_packed(unconstify(text *, src));
261	size_t		src_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(srcunpacked);
262
263	if (dst_len > 0)
264	{
265		dst_len--;
266		if (dst_len >= src_len)
267			dst_len = src_len;
268		else					/* ensure truncation is encoding-safe */
269			dst_len = pg_mbcliplen(VARDATA_ANY(srcunpacked), src_len, dst_len);
270		memcpy(dst, VARDATA_ANY(srcunpacked), dst_len);
271		dst[dst_len] = '\0';
272	}
273
274	if (srcunpacked != src)
275		pfree(srcunpacked);
276}
277
278
279/*****************************************************************************
280 *	 USER I/O ROUTINES														 *
281 *****************************************************************************/
282
283
284#define VAL(CH)			((CH) - '0')
285#define DIG(VAL)		((VAL) + '0')
286
287/*
288 *		byteain			- converts from printable representation of byte array
289 *
290 *		Non-printable characters must be passed as '\nnn' (octal) and are
291 *		converted to internal form.  '\' must be passed as '\\'.
292 *		ereport(ERROR, ...) if bad form.
293 *
294 *		BUGS:
295 *				The input is scanned twice.
296 *				The error checking of input is minimal.
297 */
298Datum
299byteain(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
300{
301	char	   *inputText = PG_GETARG_CSTRING(0);
302	Node	   *escontext = fcinfo->context;
303	char	   *tp;
304	char	   *rp;
305	int			bc;
306	bytea	   *result;
307
308	/* Recognize hex input */
309	if (inputText[0] == '\\' && inputText[1] == 'x')
310	{
311		size_t		len = strlen(inputText);
312
313		bc = (len - 2) / 2 + VARHDRSZ;	/* maximum possible length */
314		result = palloc(bc);
315		bc = hex_decode_safe(inputText + 2, len - 2, VARDATA(result),
316							 escontext);
317		SET_VARSIZE(result, bc + VARHDRSZ); /* actual length */
318
319		PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
320	}
321
322	/* Else, it's the traditional escaped style */
323	for (bc = 0, tp = inputText; *tp != '\0'; bc++)
324	{
325		if (tp[0] != '\\')
326			tp++;
327		else if ((tp[0] == '\\') &&
328				 (tp[1] >= '0' && tp[1] <= '3') &&
329				 (tp[2] >= '0' && tp[2] <= '7') &&
330				 (tp[3] >= '0' && tp[3] <= '7'))
331			tp += 4;
332		else if ((tp[0] == '\\') &&
333				 (tp[1] == '\\'))
334			tp += 2;
335		else
336		{
337			/*
338			 * one backslash, not followed by another or ### valid octal
339			 */
340			ereturn(escontext, (Datum) 0,
341					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TEXT_REPRESENTATION),
342					 errmsg("invalid input syntax for type %s", "bytea")));
343		}
344	}
345
346	bc += VARHDRSZ;
347
348	result = (bytea *) palloc(bc);
349	SET_VARSIZE(result, bc);
350
351	tp = inputText;
352	rp = VARDATA(result);
353	while (*tp != '\0')
354	{
355		if (tp[0] != '\\')
356			*rp++ = *tp++;
357		else if ((tp[0] == '\\') &&
358				 (tp[1] >= '0' && tp[1] <= '3') &&
359				 (tp[2] >= '0' && tp[2] <= '7') &&
360				 (tp[3] >= '0' && tp[3] <= '7'))
361		{
362			bc = VAL(tp[1]);
363			bc <<= 3;
364			bc += VAL(tp[2]);
365			bc <<= 3;
366			*rp++ = bc + VAL(tp[3]);
367
368			tp += 4;
369		}
370		else if ((tp[0] == '\\') &&
371				 (tp[1] == '\\'))
372		{
373			*rp++ = '\\';
374			tp += 2;
375		}
376		else
377		{
378			/*
379			 * We should never get here. The first pass should not allow it.
380			 */
381			ereturn(escontext, (Datum) 0,
382					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TEXT_REPRESENTATION),
383					 errmsg("invalid input syntax for type %s", "bytea")));
384		}
385	}
386
387	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
388}
389
390/*
391 *		byteaout		- converts to printable representation of byte array
392 *
393 *		In the traditional escaped format, non-printable characters are
394 *		printed as '\nnn' (octal) and '\' as '\\'.
395 */
396Datum
397byteaout(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
398{
399	bytea	   *vlena = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
400	char	   *result;
401	char	   *rp;
402
403	if (bytea_output == BYTEA_OUTPUT_HEX)
404	{
405		/* Print hex format */
406		rp = result = palloc(VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(vlena) * 2 + 2 + 1);
407		*rp++ = '\\';
408		*rp++ = 'x';
409		rp += hex_encode(VARDATA_ANY(vlena), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(vlena), rp);
410	}
411	else if (bytea_output == BYTEA_OUTPUT_ESCAPE)
412	{
413		/* Print traditional escaped format */
414		char	   *vp;
415		uint64		len;
416		int			i;
417
418		len = 1;				/* empty string has 1 char */
419		vp = VARDATA_ANY(vlena);
420		for (i = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(vlena); i != 0; i--, vp++)
421		{
422			if (*vp == '\\')
423				len += 2;
424			else if ((unsigned char) *vp < 0x20 || (unsigned char) *vp > 0x7e)
425				len += 4;
426			else
427				len++;
428		}
429
430		/*
431		 * In principle len can't overflow uint32 if the input fit in 1GB, but
432		 * for safety let's check rather than relying on palloc's internal
433		 * check.
434		 */
435		if (len > MaxAllocSize)
436			ereport(ERROR,
437					(errcode(ERRCODE_PROGRAM_LIMIT_EXCEEDED),
438					 errmsg_internal("result of bytea output conversion is too large")));
439		rp = result = (char *) palloc(len);
440
441		vp = VARDATA_ANY(vlena);
442		for (i = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(vlena); i != 0; i--, vp++)
443		{
444			if (*vp == '\\')
445			{
446				*rp++ = '\\';
447				*rp++ = '\\';
448			}
449			else if ((unsigned char) *vp < 0x20 || (unsigned char) *vp > 0x7e)
450			{
451				int			val;	/* holds unprintable chars */
452
453				val = *vp;
454				rp[0] = '\\';
455				rp[3] = DIG(val & 07);
456				val >>= 3;
457				rp[2] = DIG(val & 07);
458				val >>= 3;
459				rp[1] = DIG(val & 03);
460				rp += 4;
461			}
462			else
463				*rp++ = *vp;
464		}
465	}
466	else
467	{
468		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized \"bytea_output\" setting: %d",
469			 bytea_output);
470		rp = result = NULL;		/* keep compiler quiet */
471	}
472	*rp = '\0';
473	PG_RETURN_CSTRING(result);
474}
475
476/*
477 *		bytearecv			- converts external binary format to bytea
478 */
479Datum
480bytearecv(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
481{
482	StringInfo	buf = (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
483	bytea	   *result;
484	int			nbytes;
485
486	nbytes = buf->len - buf->cursor;
487	result = (bytea *) palloc(nbytes + VARHDRSZ);
488	SET_VARSIZE(result, nbytes + VARHDRSZ);
489	pq_copymsgbytes(buf, VARDATA(result), nbytes);
490	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
491}
492
493/*
494 *		byteasend			- converts bytea to binary format
495 *
496 * This is a special case: just copy the input...
497 */
498Datum
499byteasend(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
500{
501	bytea	   *vlena = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_P_COPY(0);
502
503	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(vlena);
504}
505
506Datum
507bytea_string_agg_transfn(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
508{
509	StringInfo	state;
510
511	state = PG_ARGISNULL(0) ? NULL : (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
512
513	/* Append the value unless null, preceding it with the delimiter. */
514	if (!PG_ARGISNULL(1))
515	{
516		bytea	   *value = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
517		bool		isfirst = false;
518
519		/*
520		 * You might think we can just throw away the first delimiter, however
521		 * we must keep it as we may be a parallel worker doing partial
522		 * aggregation building a state to send to the main process.  We need
523		 * to keep the delimiter of every aggregation so that the combine
524		 * function can properly join up the strings of two separately
525		 * partially aggregated results.  The first delimiter is only stripped
526		 * off in the final function.  To know how much to strip off the front
527		 * of the string, we store the length of the first delimiter in the
528		 * StringInfo's cursor field, which we don't otherwise need here.
529		 */
530		if (state == NULL)
531		{
532			state = makeStringAggState(fcinfo);
533			isfirst = true;
534		}
535
536		if (!PG_ARGISNULL(2))
537		{
538			bytea	   *delim = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(2);
539
540			appendBinaryStringInfo(state, VARDATA_ANY(delim),
541								   VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(delim));
542			if (isfirst)
543				state->cursor = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(delim);
544		}
545
546		appendBinaryStringInfo(state, VARDATA_ANY(value),
547							   VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(value));
548	}
549
550	/*
551	 * The transition type for string_agg() is declared to be "internal",
552	 * which is a pass-by-value type the same size as a pointer.
553	 */
554	if (state)
555		PG_RETURN_POINTER(state);
556	PG_RETURN_NULL();
557}
558
559Datum
560bytea_string_agg_finalfn(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
561{
562	StringInfo	state;
563
564	/* cannot be called directly because of internal-type argument */
565	Assert(AggCheckCallContext(fcinfo, NULL));
566
567	state = PG_ARGISNULL(0) ? NULL : (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
568
569	if (state != NULL)
570	{
571		/* As per comment in transfn, strip data before the cursor position */
572		bytea	   *result;
573		int			strippedlen = state->len - state->cursor;
574
575		result = (bytea *) palloc(strippedlen + VARHDRSZ);
576		SET_VARSIZE(result, strippedlen + VARHDRSZ);
577		memcpy(VARDATA(result), &state->data[state->cursor], strippedlen);
578		PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
579	}
580	else
581		PG_RETURN_NULL();
582}
583
584/*
585 *		textin			- converts cstring to internal representation
586 */
587Datum
588textin(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
589{
590	char	   *inputText = PG_GETARG_CSTRING(0);
591
592	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(inputText));
593}
594
595/*
596 *		textout			- converts internal representation to cstring
597 */
598Datum
599textout(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
600{
601	Datum		txt = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
602
603	PG_RETURN_CSTRING(TextDatumGetCString(txt));
604}
605
606/*
607 *		textrecv			- converts external binary format to text
608 */
609Datum
610textrecv(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
611{
612	StringInfo	buf = (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
613	text	   *result;
614	char	   *str;
615	int			nbytes;
616
617	str = pq_getmsgtext(buf, buf->len - buf->cursor, &nbytes);
618
619	result = cstring_to_text_with_len(str, nbytes);
620	pfree(str);
621	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
622}
623
624/*
625 *		textsend			- converts text to binary format
626 */
627Datum
628textsend(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
629{
630	text	   *t = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
631	StringInfoData buf;
632
633	pq_begintypsend(&buf);
634	pq_sendtext(&buf, VARDATA_ANY(t), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t));
635	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(pq_endtypsend(&buf));
636}
637
638
639/*
640 *		unknownin			- converts cstring to internal representation
641 */
642Datum
643unknownin(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
644{
645	char	   *str = PG_GETARG_CSTRING(0);
646
647	/* representation is same as cstring */
648	PG_RETURN_CSTRING(pstrdup(str));
649}
650
651/*
652 *		unknownout			- converts internal representation to cstring
653 */
654Datum
655unknownout(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
656{
657	/* representation is same as cstring */
658	char	   *str = PG_GETARG_CSTRING(0);
659
660	PG_RETURN_CSTRING(pstrdup(str));
661}
662
663/*
664 *		unknownrecv			- converts external binary format to unknown
665 */
666Datum
667unknownrecv(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
668{
669	StringInfo	buf = (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
670	char	   *str;
671	int			nbytes;
672
673	str = pq_getmsgtext(buf, buf->len - buf->cursor, &nbytes);
674	/* representation is same as cstring */
675	PG_RETURN_CSTRING(str);
676}
677
678/*
679 *		unknownsend			- converts unknown to binary format
680 */
681Datum
682unknownsend(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
683{
684	/* representation is same as cstring */
685	char	   *str = PG_GETARG_CSTRING(0);
686	StringInfoData buf;
687
688	pq_begintypsend(&buf);
689	pq_sendtext(&buf, str, strlen(str));
690	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(pq_endtypsend(&buf));
691}
692
693
694/* ========== PUBLIC ROUTINES ========== */
695
696/*
697 * textlen -
698 *	  returns the logical length of a text*
699 *	   (which is less than the VARSIZE of the text*)
700 */
701Datum
702textlen(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
703{
704	Datum		str = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
705
706	/* try to avoid decompressing argument */
707	PG_RETURN_INT32(text_length(str));
708}
709
710/*
711 * text_length -
712 *	Does the real work for textlen()
713 *
714 *	This is broken out so it can be called directly by other string processing
715 *	functions.  Note that the argument is passed as a Datum, to indicate that
716 *	it may still be in compressed form.  We can avoid decompressing it at all
717 *	in some cases.
718 */
719static int32
720text_length(Datum str)
721{
722	/* fastpath when max encoding length is one */
723	if (pg_database_encoding_max_length() == 1)
724		PG_RETURN_INT32(toast_raw_datum_size(str) - VARHDRSZ);
725	else
726	{
727		text	   *t = DatumGetTextPP(str);
728
729		PG_RETURN_INT32(pg_mbstrlen_with_len(VARDATA_ANY(t),
730											 VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t)));
731	}
732}
733
734/*
735 * textoctetlen -
736 *	  returns the physical length of a text*
737 *	   (which is less than the VARSIZE of the text*)
738 */
739Datum
740textoctetlen(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
741{
742	Datum		str = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
743
744	/* We need not detoast the input at all */
745	PG_RETURN_INT32(toast_raw_datum_size(str) - VARHDRSZ);
746}
747
748/*
749 * textcat -
750 *	  takes two text* and returns a text* that is the concatenation of
751 *	  the two.
752 *
753 * Rewritten by Sapa, sapa@hq.icb.chel.su. 8-Jul-96.
754 * Updated by Thomas, Thomas.Lockhart@jpl.nasa.gov 1997-07-10.
755 * Allocate space for output in all cases.
756 * XXX - thomas 1997-07-10
757 */
758Datum
759textcat(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
760{
761	text	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
762	text	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
763
764	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(text_catenate(t1, t2));
765}
766
767/*
768 * text_catenate
769 *	Guts of textcat(), broken out so it can be used by other functions
770 *
771 * Arguments can be in short-header form, but not compressed or out-of-line
772 */
773static text *
774text_catenate(text *t1, text *t2)
775{
776	text	   *result;
777	int			len1,
778				len2,
779				len;
780	char	   *ptr;
781
782	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t1);
783	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2);
784
785	/* paranoia ... probably should throw error instead? */
786	if (len1 < 0)
787		len1 = 0;
788	if (len2 < 0)
789		len2 = 0;
790
791	len = len1 + len2 + VARHDRSZ;
792	result = (text *) palloc(len);
793
794	/* Set size of result string... */
795	SET_VARSIZE(result, len);
796
797	/* Fill data field of result string... */
798	ptr = VARDATA(result);
799	if (len1 > 0)
800		memcpy(ptr, VARDATA_ANY(t1), len1);
801	if (len2 > 0)
802		memcpy(ptr + len1, VARDATA_ANY(t2), len2);
803
804	return result;
805}
806
807/*
808 * charlen_to_bytelen()
809 *	Compute the number of bytes occupied by n characters starting at *p
810 *
811 * The caller shall ensure there are n complete characters.  Callers achieve
812 * this by deriving "n" from regmatch_t findings from searching a wchar array.
813 * pg_mb2wchar_with_len() skips any trailing incomplete character, so regex
814 * matches will end no later than the last complete character.  (The string
815 * need not be null-terminated.)
816 */
817static int
818charlen_to_bytelen(const char *p, int n)
819{
820	if (pg_database_encoding_max_length() == 1)
821	{
822		/* Optimization for single-byte encodings */
823		return n;
824	}
825	else
826	{
827		const char *s;
828
829		for (s = p; n > 0; n--)
830			s += pg_mblen_unbounded(s); /* caller verified encoding */
831
832		return s - p;
833	}
834}
835
836/*
837 * text_substr()
838 * Return a substring starting at the specified position.
839 * - thomas 1997-12-31
840 *
841 * Input:
842 *	- string
843 *	- starting position (is one-based)
844 *	- string length
845 *
846 * If the starting position is zero or less, then return from the start of the string
847 *	adjusting the length to be consistent with the "negative start" per SQL.
848 * If the length is less than zero, return the remaining string.
849 *
850 * Added multibyte support.
851 * - Tatsuo Ishii 1998-4-21
852 * Changed behavior if starting position is less than one to conform to SQL behavior.
853 * Formerly returned the entire string; now returns a portion.
854 * - Thomas Lockhart 1998-12-10
855 * Now uses faster TOAST-slicing interface
856 * - John Gray 2002-02-22
857 * Remove "#ifdef MULTIBYTE" and test for encoding_max_length instead. Change
858 * behaviors conflicting with SQL to meet SQL (if E = S + L < S throw
859 * error; if E < 1, return '', not entire string). Fixed MB related bug when
860 * S > LC and < LC + 4 sometimes garbage characters are returned.
861 * - Joe Conway 2002-08-10
862 */
863Datum
864text_substr(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
865{
866	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(text_substring(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0),
867									PG_GETARG_INT32(1),
868									PG_GETARG_INT32(2),
869									false));
870}
871
872/*
873 * text_substr_no_len -
874 *	  Wrapper to avoid opr_sanity failure due to
875 *	  one function accepting a different number of args.
876 */
877Datum
878text_substr_no_len(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
879{
880	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(text_substring(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0),
881									PG_GETARG_INT32(1),
882									-1, true));
883}
884
885/*
886 * text_substring -
887 *	Does the real work for text_substr() and text_substr_no_len()
888 *
889 *	This is broken out so it can be called directly by other string processing
890 *	functions.  Note that the argument is passed as a Datum, to indicate that
891 *	it may still be in compressed/toasted form.  We can avoid detoasting all
892 *	of it in some cases.
893 *
894 *	The result is always a freshly palloc'd datum.
895 */
896static text *
897text_substring(Datum str, int32 start, int32 length, bool length_not_specified)
898{
899	int32		eml = pg_database_encoding_max_length();
900	int32		S = start;		/* start position */
901	int32		S1;				/* adjusted start position */
902	int32		L1;				/* adjusted substring length */
903	int32		E;				/* end position, exclusive */
904
905	/*
906	 * SQL99 says S can be zero or negative (which we don't document), but we
907	 * still must fetch from the start of the string.
908	 * https://www.postgresql.org/message-id/170905442373.643.11536838320909376197%40wrigleys.postgresql.org
909	 */
910	S1 = Max(S, 1);
911
912	/* life is easy if the encoding max length is 1 */
913	if (eml == 1)
914	{
915		if (length_not_specified)	/* special case - get length to end of
916									 * string */
917			L1 = -1;
918		else if (length < 0)
919		{
920			/* SQL99 says to throw an error for E < S, i.e., negative length */
921			ereport(ERROR,
922					(errcode(ERRCODE_SUBSTRING_ERROR),
923					 errmsg("negative substring length not allowed")));
924			L1 = -1;			/* silence stupider compilers */
925		}
926		else if (pg_add_s32_overflow(S, length, &E))
927		{
928			/*
929			 * L could be large enough for S + L to overflow, in which case
930			 * the substring must run to end of string.
931			 */
932			L1 = -1;
933		}
934		else
935		{
936			/*
937			 * A zero or negative value for the end position can happen if the
938			 * start was negative or one. SQL99 says to return a zero-length
939			 * string.
940			 */
941			if (E < 1)
942				return cstring_to_text("");
943
944			L1 = E - S1;
945		}
946
947		/*
948		 * If the start position is past the end of the string, SQL99 says to
949		 * return a zero-length string -- DatumGetTextPSlice() will do that
950		 * for us.  We need only convert S1 to zero-based starting position.
951		 */
952		return DatumGetTextPSlice(str, S1 - 1, L1);
953	}
954	else if (eml > 1)
955	{
956		/*
957		 * When encoding max length is > 1, we can't get LC without
958		 * detoasting, so we'll grab a conservatively large slice now and go
959		 * back later to do the right thing
960		 */
961		int32		slice_start;
962		int32		slice_size;
963		int32		slice_strlen;
964		int32		slice_len;
965		text	   *slice;
966		int32		E1;
967		int32		i;
968		char	   *p;
969		char	   *s;
970		text	   *ret;
971
972		/*
973		 * We need to start at position zero because there is no way to know
974		 * in advance which byte offset corresponds to the supplied start
975		 * position.
976		 */
977		slice_start = 0;
978
979		if (length_not_specified)	/* special case - get length to end of
980									 * string */
981			E = slice_size = L1 = -1;
982		else if (length < 0)
983		{
984			/* SQL99 says to throw an error for E < S, i.e., negative length */
985			ereport(ERROR,
986					(errcode(ERRCODE_SUBSTRING_ERROR),
987					 errmsg("negative substring length not allowed")));
988			E = slice_size = L1 = -1;	/* silence stupider compilers */
989		}
990		else if (pg_add_s32_overflow(S, length, &E))
991		{
992			/*
993			 * L could be large enough for S + L to overflow, in which case
994			 * the substring must run to end of string.
995			 */
996			slice_size = L1 = -1;
997		}
998		else
999		{
1000			/*
1001			 * Ending at position 1, exclusive, obviously yields an empty
1002			 * string.  A zero or negative value can happen if the start was
1003			 * negative or one. SQL99 says to return a zero-length string.
1004			 */
1005			if (E <= 1)
1006				return cstring_to_text("");
1007
1008			/*
1009			 * if E is past the end of the string, the tuple toaster will
1010			 * truncate the length for us
1011			 */
1012			L1 = E - S1;
1013
1014			/*
1015			 * Total slice size in bytes can't be any longer than the
1016			 * inclusive end position times the encoding max length.  If that
1017			 * overflows, we can just use -1.
1018			 */
1019			if (pg_mul_s32_overflow(E - 1, eml, &slice_size))
1020				slice_size = -1;
1021		}
1022
1023		/*
1024		 * If we're working with an untoasted source, no need to do an extra
1025		 * copying step.
1026		 */
1027		if (VARATT_IS_COMPRESSED(DatumGetPointer(str)) ||
1028			VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL(DatumGetPointer(str)))
1029			slice = DatumGetTextPSlice(str, slice_start, slice_size);
1030		else
1031			slice = (text *) DatumGetPointer(str);
1032
1033		/* see if we got back an empty string */
1034		slice_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(slice);
1035		if (slice_len == 0)
1036		{
1037			if (slice != (text *) DatumGetPointer(str))
1038				pfree(slice);
1039			return cstring_to_text("");
1040		}
1041
1042		/*
1043		 * Now we can get the actual length of the slice in MB characters,
1044		 * stopping at the end of the substring.  Continuing beyond the
1045		 * substring end could find an incomplete character attributable
1046		 * solely to DatumGetTextPSlice() chopping in the middle of a
1047		 * character, and it would be superfluous work at best.
1048		 */
1049		slice_strlen =
1050			(slice_size == -1 ?
1051			 pg_mbstrlen_with_len(VARDATA_ANY(slice), slice_len) :
1052			 pg_mbcharcliplen_chars(VARDATA_ANY(slice), slice_len, E - 1));
1053
1054		/*
1055		 * Check that the start position wasn't > slice_strlen. If so, SQL99
1056		 * says to return a zero-length string.
1057		 */
1058		if (S1 > slice_strlen)
1059		{
1060			if (slice != (text *) DatumGetPointer(str))
1061				pfree(slice);
1062			return cstring_to_text("");
1063		}
1064
1065		/*
1066		 * Adjust L1 and E1 now that we know the slice string length. Again
1067		 * remember that S1 is one based, and slice_start is zero based.
1068		 */
1069		if (L1 > -1)
1070			E1 = Min(S1 + L1, slice_start + 1 + slice_strlen);
1071		else
1072			E1 = slice_start + 1 + slice_strlen;
1073
1074		/*
1075		 * Find the start position in the slice; remember S1 is not zero based
1076		 */
1077		p = VARDATA_ANY(slice);
1078		for (i = 0; i < S1 - 1; i++)
1079			p += pg_mblen_unbounded(p);
1080
1081		/* hang onto a pointer to our start position */
1082		s = p;
1083
1084		/*
1085		 * Count the actual bytes used by the substring of the requested
1086		 * length.
1087		 */
1088		for (i = S1; i < E1; i++)
1089			p += pg_mblen_unbounded(p);
1090
1091		ret = (text *) palloc(VARHDRSZ + (p - s));
1092		SET_VARSIZE(ret, VARHDRSZ + (p - s));
1093		memcpy(VARDATA(ret), s, (p - s));
1094
1095		if (slice != (text *) DatumGetPointer(str))
1096			pfree(slice);
1097
1098		return ret;
1099	}
1100	else
1101		elog(ERROR, "invalid backend encoding: encoding max length < 1");
1102
1103	/* not reached: suppress compiler warning */
1104	return NULL;
1105}
1106
1107/*
1108 * pg_mbcharcliplen_chars -
1109 *	Mirror pg_mbcharcliplen(), except return value unit is chars, not bytes.
1110 *
1111 *	This mirrors all the dubious historical behavior, so it's static to
1112 *	discourage proliferation.  The assertions are specific to the one caller.
1113 */
1114static int
1115pg_mbcharcliplen_chars(const char *mbstr, int len, int limit)
1116{
1117	int			nch = 0;
1118	int			l;
1119
1120	Assert(len > 0);
1121	Assert(limit > 0);
1122	Assert(pg_database_encoding_max_length() > 1);
1123
1124	while (len > 0 && *mbstr)
1125	{
1126		l = pg_mblen_with_len(mbstr, len);
1127		nch++;
1128		if (nch == limit)
1129			break;
1130		len -= l;
1131		mbstr += l;
1132	}
1133	return nch;
1134}
1135
1136/*
1137 * textoverlay
1138 *	Replace specified substring of first string with second
1139 *
1140 * The SQL standard defines OVERLAY() in terms of substring and concatenation.
1141 * This code is a direct implementation of what the standard says.
1142 */
1143Datum
1144textoverlay(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1145{
1146	text	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1147	text	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1148	int			sp = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);	/* substring start position */
1149	int			sl = PG_GETARG_INT32(3);	/* substring length */
1150
1151	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(text_overlay(t1, t2, sp, sl));
1152}
1153
1154Datum
1155textoverlay_no_len(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1156{
1157	text	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1158	text	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1159	int			sp = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);	/* substring start position */
1160	int			sl;
1161
1162	sl = text_length(PointerGetDatum(t2));	/* defaults to length(t2) */
1163	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(text_overlay(t1, t2, sp, sl));
1164}
1165
1166static text *
1167text_overlay(text *t1, text *t2, int sp, int sl)
1168{
1169	text	   *result;
1170	text	   *s1;
1171	text	   *s2;
1172	int			sp_pl_sl;
1173
1174	/*
1175	 * Check for possible integer-overflow cases.  For negative sp, throw a
1176	 * "substring length" error because that's what should be expected
1177	 * according to the spec's definition of OVERLAY().
1178	 */
1179	if (sp <= 0)
1180		ereport(ERROR,
1181				(errcode(ERRCODE_SUBSTRING_ERROR),
1182				 errmsg("negative substring length not allowed")));
1183	if (pg_add_s32_overflow(sp, sl, &sp_pl_sl))
1184		ereport(ERROR,
1185				(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
1186				 errmsg("integer out of range")));
1187
1188	s1 = text_substring(PointerGetDatum(t1), 1, sp - 1, false);
1189	s2 = text_substring(PointerGetDatum(t1), sp_pl_sl, -1, true);
1190	result = text_catenate(s1, t2);
1191	result = text_catenate(result, s2);
1192
1193	return result;
1194}
1195
1196/*
1197 * textpos -
1198 *	  Return the position of the specified substring.
1199 *	  Implements the SQL POSITION() function.
1200 *	  Ref: A Guide To The SQL Standard, Date & Darwen, 1997
1201 * - thomas 1997-07-27
1202 */
1203Datum
1204textpos(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1205{
1206	text	   *str = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1207	text	   *search_str = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1208
1209	PG_RETURN_INT32((int32) text_position(str, search_str, PG_GET_COLLATION()));
1210}
1211
1212/*
1213 * text_position -
1214 *	Does the real work for textpos()
1215 *
1216 * Inputs:
1217 *		t1 - string to be searched
1218 *		t2 - pattern to match within t1
1219 * Result:
1220 *		Character index of the first matched char, starting from 1,
1221 *		or 0 if no match.
1222 *
1223 *	This is broken out so it can be called directly by other string processing
1224 *	functions.
1225 */
1226static int
1227text_position(text *t1, text *t2, Oid collid)
1228{
1229	TextPositionState state;
1230	int			result;
1231
1232	check_collation_set(collid);
1233
1234	/* Empty needle always matches at position 1 */
1235	if (VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2) < 1)
1236		return 1;
1237
1238	/* Otherwise, can't match if haystack is shorter than needle */
1239	if (VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t1) < VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2) &&
1240		pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid)->deterministic)
1241		return 0;
1242
1243	text_position_setup(t1, t2, collid, &state);
1244	/* don't need greedy mode here */
1245	state.greedy = false;
1246
1247	if (!text_position_next(&state))
1248		result = 0;
1249	else
1250		result = text_position_get_match_pos(&state);
1251	text_position_cleanup(&state);
1252	return result;
1253}
1254
1255
1256/*
1257 * text_position_setup, text_position_next, text_position_cleanup -
1258 *	Component steps of text_position()
1259 *
1260 * These are broken out so that a string can be efficiently searched for
1261 * multiple occurrences of the same pattern.  text_position_next may be
1262 * called multiple times, and it advances to the next match on each call.
1263 * text_position_get_match_ptr() and text_position_get_match_pos() return
1264 * a pointer or 1-based character position of the last match, respectively.
1265 *
1266 * The "state" variable is normally just a local variable in the caller.
1267 *
1268 * NOTE: text_position_next skips over the matched portion.  For example,
1269 * searching for "xx" in "xxx" returns only one match, not two.
1270 */
1271
1272static void
1273text_position_setup(text *t1, text *t2, Oid collid, TextPositionState *state)
1274{
1275	int			len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t1);
1276	int			len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2);
1277
1278	check_collation_set(collid);
1279
1280	state->locale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
1281
1282	/*
1283	 * Most callers need greedy mode, but some might want to unset this to
1284	 * optimize.
1285	 */
1286	state->greedy = true;
1287
1288	Assert(len2 > 0);
1289
1290	/*
1291	 * Even with a multi-byte encoding, we perform the search using the raw
1292	 * byte sequence, ignoring multibyte issues.  For UTF-8, that works fine,
1293	 * because in UTF-8 the byte sequence of one character cannot contain
1294	 * another character.  For other multi-byte encodings, we do the search
1295	 * initially as a simple byte search, ignoring multibyte issues, but
1296	 * verify afterwards that the match we found is at a character boundary,
1297	 * and continue the search if it was a false match.
1298	 */
1299	if (pg_database_encoding_max_length() == 1)
1300		state->is_multibyte_char_in_char = false;
1301	else if (GetDatabaseEncoding() == PG_UTF8)
1302		state->is_multibyte_char_in_char = false;
1303	else
1304		state->is_multibyte_char_in_char = true;
1305
1306	state->str1 = VARDATA_ANY(t1);
1307	state->str2 = VARDATA_ANY(t2);
1308	state->len1 = len1;
1309	state->len2 = len2;
1310	state->last_match = NULL;
1311	state->refpoint = state->str1;
1312	state->refpos = 0;
1313
1314	/*
1315	 * Prepare the skip table for Boyer-Moore-Horspool searching.  In these
1316	 * notes we use the terminology that the "haystack" is the string to be
1317	 * searched (t1) and the "needle" is the pattern being sought (t2).
1318	 *
1319	 * If the needle is empty or bigger than the haystack then there is no
1320	 * point in wasting cycles initializing the table.  We also choose not to
1321	 * use B-M-H for needles of length 1, since the skip table can't possibly
1322	 * save anything in that case.
1323	 *
1324	 * (With nondeterministic collations, the search is already
1325	 * multibyte-aware, so we don't need this.)
1326	 */
1327	if (len1 >= len2 && len2 > 1 && state->locale->deterministic)
1328	{
1329		int			searchlength = len1 - len2;
1330		int			skiptablemask;
1331		int			last;
1332		int			i;
1333		const char *str2 = state->str2;
1334
1335		/*
1336		 * First we must determine how much of the skip table to use.  The
1337		 * declaration of TextPositionState allows up to 256 elements, but for
1338		 * short search problems we don't really want to have to initialize so
1339		 * many elements --- it would take too long in comparison to the
1340		 * actual search time.  So we choose a useful skip table size based on
1341		 * the haystack length minus the needle length.  The closer the needle
1342		 * length is to the haystack length the less useful skipping becomes.
1343		 *
1344		 * Note: since we use bit-masking to select table elements, the skip
1345		 * table size MUST be a power of 2, and so the mask must be 2^N-1.
1346		 */
1347		if (searchlength < 16)
1348			skiptablemask = 3;
1349		else if (searchlength < 64)
1350			skiptablemask = 7;
1351		else if (searchlength < 128)
1352			skiptablemask = 15;
1353		else if (searchlength < 512)
1354			skiptablemask = 31;
1355		else if (searchlength < 2048)
1356			skiptablemask = 63;
1357		else if (searchlength < 4096)
1358			skiptablemask = 127;
1359		else
1360			skiptablemask = 255;
1361		state->skiptablemask = skiptablemask;
1362
1363		/*
1364		 * Initialize the skip table.  We set all elements to the needle
1365		 * length, since this is the correct skip distance for any character
1366		 * not found in the needle.
1367		 */
1368		for (i = 0; i <= skiptablemask; i++)
1369			state->skiptable[i] = len2;
1370
1371		/*
1372		 * Now examine the needle.  For each character except the last one,
1373		 * set the corresponding table element to the appropriate skip
1374		 * distance.  Note that when two characters share the same skip table
1375		 * entry, the one later in the needle must determine the skip
1376		 * distance.
1377		 */
1378		last = len2 - 1;
1379
1380		for (i = 0; i < last; i++)
1381			state->skiptable[(unsigned char) str2[i] & skiptablemask] = last - i;
1382	}
1383}
1384
1385/*
1386 * Advance to the next match, starting from the end of the previous match
1387 * (or the beginning of the string, on first call).  Returns true if a match
1388 * is found.
1389 *
1390 * Note that this refuses to match an empty-string needle.  Most callers
1391 * will have handled that case specially and we'll never see it here.
1392 */
1393static bool
1394text_position_next(TextPositionState *state)
1395{
1396	int			needle_len = state->len2;
1397	char	   *start_ptr;
1398	char	   *matchptr;
1399
1400	if (needle_len <= 0)
1401		return false;			/* result for empty pattern */
1402
1403	/* Start from the point right after the previous match. */
1404	if (state->last_match)
1405		start_ptr = state->last_match + state->last_match_len;
1406	else
1407		start_ptr = state->str1;
1408
1409retry:
1410	matchptr = text_position_next_internal(start_ptr, state);
1411
1412	if (!matchptr)
1413		return false;
1414
1415	/*
1416	 * Found a match for the byte sequence.  If this is a multibyte encoding,
1417	 * where one character's byte sequence can appear inside a longer
1418	 * multi-byte character, we need to verify that the match was at a
1419	 * character boundary, not in the middle of a multi-byte character.
1420	 */
1421	if (state->is_multibyte_char_in_char && state->locale->deterministic)
1422	{
1423		const char *haystack_end = state->str1 + state->len1;
1424
1425		/* Walk one character at a time, until we reach the match. */
1426
1427		/* the search should never move backwards. */
1428		Assert(state->refpoint <= matchptr);
1429
1430		while (state->refpoint < matchptr)
1431		{
1432			/* step to next character. */
1433			state->refpoint += pg_mblen_range(state->refpoint, haystack_end);
1434			state->refpos++;
1435
1436			/*
1437			 * If we stepped over the match's start position, then it was a
1438			 * false positive, where the byte sequence appeared in the middle
1439			 * of a multi-byte character.  Skip it, and continue the search at
1440			 * the next character boundary.
1441			 */
1442			if (state->refpoint > matchptr)
1443			{
1444				start_ptr = state->refpoint;
1445				goto retry;
1446			}
1447		}
1448	}
1449
1450	state->last_match = matchptr;
1451	state->last_match_len = state->last_match_len_tmp;
1452	return true;
1453}
1454
1455/*
1456 * Subroutine of text_position_next().  This searches for the raw byte
1457 * sequence, ignoring any multi-byte encoding issues.  Returns the first
1458 * match starting at 'start_ptr', or NULL if no match is found.
1459 */
1460static char *
1461text_position_next_internal(char *start_ptr, TextPositionState *state)
1462{
1463	int			haystack_len = state->len1;
1464	int			needle_len = state->len2;
1465	int			skiptablemask = state->skiptablemask;
1466	const char *haystack = state->str1;
1467	const char *needle = state->str2;
1468	const char *haystack_end = &haystack[haystack_len];
1469	const char *hptr;
1470
1471	Assert(start_ptr >= haystack && start_ptr <= haystack_end);
1472	Assert(needle_len > 0);
1473
1474	state->last_match_len_tmp = needle_len;
1475
1476	if (!state->locale->deterministic)
1477	{
1478		/*
1479		 * With a nondeterministic collation, we have to use an unoptimized
1480		 * route.  We walk through the haystack and see if at each position
1481		 * there is a substring of the remaining string that is equal to the
1482		 * needle under the given collation.
1483		 *
1484		 * Note, the found substring could have a different length than the
1485		 * needle.  Callers that want to skip over the found string need to
1486		 * read the length of the found substring from last_match_len rather
1487		 * than just using the length of their needle.
1488		 *
1489		 * Most callers will require "greedy" semantics, meaning that we need
1490		 * to find the longest such substring, not the shortest.  For callers
1491		 * that don't need greedy semantics, we can finish on the first match.
1492		 *
1493		 * This loop depends on the assumption that the needle is nonempty and
1494		 * any matching substring must also be nonempty.  (Even if the
1495		 * collation would accept an empty match, returning one would send
1496		 * callers that search for successive matches into an infinite loop.)
1497		 */
1498		const char *result_hptr = NULL;
1499
1500		hptr = start_ptr;
1501		while (hptr < haystack_end)
1502		{
1503			const char *test_end;
1504
1505			/*
1506			 * First check the common case that there is a match in the
1507			 * haystack of exactly the length of the needle.
1508			 */
1509			if (!state->greedy &&
1510				haystack_end - hptr >= needle_len &&
1511				pg_strncoll(hptr, needle_len, needle, needle_len, state->locale) == 0)
1512				return (char *) hptr;
1513
1514			/*
1515			 * Else check if any of the non-empty substrings starting at hptr
1516			 * compare equal to the needle.
1517			 */
1518			test_end = hptr;
1519			do
1520			{
1521				test_end += pg_mblen_range(test_end, haystack_end);
1522				if (pg_strncoll(hptr, (test_end - hptr), needle, needle_len, state->locale) == 0)
1523				{
1524					state->last_match_len_tmp = (test_end - hptr);
1525					result_hptr = hptr;
1526					if (!state->greedy)
1527						break;
1528				}
1529			} while (test_end < haystack_end);
1530
1531			if (result_hptr)
1532				break;
1533
1534			hptr += pg_mblen_range(hptr, haystack_end);
1535		}
1536
1537		return (char *) result_hptr;
1538	}
1539	else if (needle_len == 1)
1540	{
1541		/* No point in using B-M-H for a one-character needle */
1542		char		nchar = *needle;
1543
1544		hptr = start_ptr;
1545		while (hptr < haystack_end)
1546		{
1547			if (*hptr == nchar)
1548				return (char *) hptr;
1549			hptr++;
1550		}
1551	}
1552	else
1553	{
1554		const char *needle_last = &needle[needle_len - 1];
1555
1556		/* Start at startpos plus the length of the needle */
1557		hptr = start_ptr + needle_len - 1;
1558		while (hptr < haystack_end)
1559		{
1560			/* Match the needle scanning *backward* */
1561			const char *nptr;
1562			const char *p;
1563
1564			nptr = needle_last;
1565			p = hptr;
1566			while (*nptr == *p)
1567			{
1568				/* Matched it all?	If so, return 1-based position */
1569				if (nptr == needle)
1570					return (char *) p;
1571				nptr--, p--;
1572			}
1573
1574			/*
1575			 * No match, so use the haystack char at hptr to decide how far to
1576			 * advance.  If the needle had any occurrence of that character
1577			 * (or more precisely, one sharing the same skiptable entry)
1578			 * before its last character, then we advance far enough to align
1579			 * the last such needle character with that haystack position.
1580			 * Otherwise we can advance by the whole needle length.
1581			 */
1582			hptr += state->skiptable[(unsigned char) *hptr & skiptablemask];
1583		}
1584	}
1585
1586	return 0;					/* not found */
1587}
1588
1589/*
1590 * Return a pointer to the current match.
1591 *
1592 * The returned pointer points into the original haystack string.
1593 */
1594static char *
1595text_position_get_match_ptr(TextPositionState *state)
1596{
1597	return state->last_match;
1598}
1599
1600/*
1601 * Return the offset of the current match.
1602 *
1603 * The offset is in characters, 1-based.
1604 */
1605static int
1606text_position_get_match_pos(TextPositionState *state)
1607{
1608	/* Convert the byte position to char position. */
1609	state->refpos += pg_mbstrlen_with_len(state->refpoint,
1610										  state->last_match - state->refpoint);
1611	state->refpoint = state->last_match;
1612	return state->refpos + 1;
1613}
1614
1615/*
1616 * Reset search state to the initial state installed by text_position_setup.
1617 *
1618 * The next call to text_position_next will search from the beginning
1619 * of the string.
1620 */
1621static void
1622text_position_reset(TextPositionState *state)
1623{
1624	state->last_match = NULL;
1625	state->refpoint = state->str1;
1626	state->refpos = 0;
1627}
1628
1629static void
1630text_position_cleanup(TextPositionState *state)
1631{
1632	/* no cleanup needed */
1633}
1634
1635
1636static void
1637check_collation_set(Oid collid)
1638{
1639	if (!OidIsValid(collid))
1640	{
1641		/*
1642		 * This typically means that the parser could not resolve a conflict
1643		 * of implicit collations, so report it that way.
1644		 */
1645		ereport(ERROR,
1646				(errcode(ERRCODE_INDETERMINATE_COLLATION),
1647				 errmsg("could not determine which collation to use for string comparison"),
1648				 errhint("Use the COLLATE clause to set the collation explicitly.")));
1649	}
1650}
1651
1652/*
1653 * varstr_cmp()
1654 *
1655 * Comparison function for text strings with given lengths, using the
1656 * appropriate locale. Returns an integer less than, equal to, or greater than
1657 * zero, indicating whether arg1 is less than, equal to, or greater than arg2.
1658 *
1659 * Note: many functions that depend on this are marked leakproof; therefore,
1660 * avoid reporting the actual contents of the input when throwing errors.
1661 * All errors herein should be things that can't happen except on corrupt
1662 * data, anyway; otherwise we will have trouble with indexing strings that
1663 * would cause them.
1664 */
1665int
1666varstr_cmp(const char *arg1, int len1, const char *arg2, int len2, Oid collid)
1667{
1668	int			result;
1669	pg_locale_t mylocale;
1670
1671	check_collation_set(collid);
1672
1673	mylocale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
1674
1675	if (mylocale->collate_is_c)
1676	{
1677		result = memcmp(arg1, arg2, Min(len1, len2));
1678		if ((result == 0) && (len1 != len2))
1679			result = (len1 < len2) ? -1 : 1;
1680	}
1681	else
1682	{
1683		/*
1684		 * memcmp() can't tell us which of two unequal strings sorts first,
1685		 * but it's a cheap way to tell if they're equal.  Testing shows that
1686		 * memcmp() followed by strcoll() is only trivially slower than
1687		 * strcoll() by itself, so we don't lose much if this doesn't work out
1688		 * very often, and if it does - for example, because there are many
1689		 * equal strings in the input - then we win big by avoiding expensive
1690		 * collation-aware comparisons.
1691		 */
1692		if (len1 == len2 && memcmp(arg1, arg2, len1) == 0)
1693			return 0;
1694
1695		result = pg_strncoll(arg1, len1, arg2, len2, mylocale);
1696
1697		/* Break tie if necessary. */
1698		if (result == 0 && mylocale->deterministic)
1699		{
1700			result = memcmp(arg1, arg2, Min(len1, len2));
1701			if ((result == 0) && (len1 != len2))
1702				result = (len1 < len2) ? -1 : 1;
1703		}
1704	}
1705
1706	return result;
1707}
1708
1709/* text_cmp()
1710 * Internal comparison function for text strings.
1711 * Returns -1, 0 or 1
1712 */
1713static int
1714text_cmp(text *arg1, text *arg2, Oid collid)
1715{
1716	char	   *a1p,
1717			   *a2p;
1718	int			len1,
1719				len2;
1720
1721	a1p = VARDATA_ANY(arg1);
1722	a2p = VARDATA_ANY(arg2);
1723
1724	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
1725	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
1726
1727	return varstr_cmp(a1p, len1, a2p, len2, collid);
1728}
1729
1730/*
1731 * Comparison functions for text strings.
1732 *
1733 * Note: btree indexes need these routines not to leak memory; therefore,
1734 * be careful to free working copies of toasted datums.  Most places don't
1735 * need to be so careful.
1736 */
1737
1738Datum
1739texteq(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1740{
1741	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
1742	pg_locale_t mylocale = 0;
1743	bool		result;
1744
1745	check_collation_set(collid);
1746
1747	mylocale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
1748
1749	if (mylocale->deterministic)
1750	{
1751		Datum		arg1 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
1752		Datum		arg2 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(1);
1753		Size		len1,
1754					len2;
1755
1756		/*
1757		 * Since we only care about equality or not-equality, we can avoid all
1758		 * the expense of strcoll() here, and just do bitwise comparison.  In
1759		 * fact, we don't even have to do a bitwise comparison if we can show
1760		 * the lengths of the strings are unequal; which might save us from
1761		 * having to detoast one or both values.
1762		 */
1763		len1 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg1);
1764		len2 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg2);
1765		if (len1 != len2)
1766			result = false;
1767		else
1768		{
1769			text	   *targ1 = DatumGetTextPP(arg1);
1770			text	   *targ2 = DatumGetTextPP(arg2);
1771
1772			result = (memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(targ1), VARDATA_ANY(targ2),
1773							 len1 - VARHDRSZ) == 0);
1774
1775			PG_FREE_IF_COPY(targ1, 0);
1776			PG_FREE_IF_COPY(targ2, 1);
1777		}
1778	}
1779	else
1780	{
1781		text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1782		text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1783
1784		result = (text_cmp(arg1, arg2, collid) == 0);
1785
1786		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1787		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1788	}
1789
1790	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1791}
1792
1793Datum
1794textne(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1795{
1796	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
1797	pg_locale_t mylocale;
1798	bool		result;
1799
1800	check_collation_set(collid);
1801
1802	mylocale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
1803
1804	if (mylocale->deterministic)
1805	{
1806		Datum		arg1 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
1807		Datum		arg2 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(1);
1808		Size		len1,
1809					len2;
1810
1811		/* See comment in texteq() */
1812		len1 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg1);
1813		len2 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg2);
1814		if (len1 != len2)
1815			result = true;
1816		else
1817		{
1818			text	   *targ1 = DatumGetTextPP(arg1);
1819			text	   *targ2 = DatumGetTextPP(arg2);
1820
1821			result = (memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(targ1), VARDATA_ANY(targ2),
1822							 len1 - VARHDRSZ) != 0);
1823
1824			PG_FREE_IF_COPY(targ1, 0);
1825			PG_FREE_IF_COPY(targ2, 1);
1826		}
1827	}
1828	else
1829	{
1830		text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1831		text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1832
1833		result = (text_cmp(arg1, arg2, collid) != 0);
1834
1835		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1836		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1837	}
1838
1839	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1840}
1841
1842Datum
1843text_lt(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1844{
1845	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1846	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1847	bool		result;
1848
1849	result = (text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION()) < 0);
1850
1851	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1852	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1853
1854	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1855}
1856
1857Datum
1858text_le(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1859{
1860	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1861	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1862	bool		result;
1863
1864	result = (text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION()) <= 0);
1865
1866	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1867	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1868
1869	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1870}
1871
1872Datum
1873text_gt(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1874{
1875	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1876	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1877	bool		result;
1878
1879	result = (text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION()) > 0);
1880
1881	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1882	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1883
1884	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1885}
1886
1887Datum
1888text_ge(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1889{
1890	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1891	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1892	bool		result;
1893
1894	result = (text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION()) >= 0);
1895
1896	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1897	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1898
1899	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1900}
1901
1902Datum
1903text_starts_with(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1904{
1905	Datum		arg1 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
1906	Datum		arg2 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(1);
1907	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
1908	pg_locale_t mylocale;
1909	bool		result;
1910	Size		len1,
1911				len2;
1912
1913	check_collation_set(collid);
1914
1915	mylocale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
1916
1917	if (!mylocale->deterministic)
1918		ereport(ERROR,
1919				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1920				 errmsg("nondeterministic collations are not supported for substring searches")));
1921
1922	len1 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg1);
1923	len2 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg2);
1924	if (len2 > len1)
1925		result = false;
1926	else
1927	{
1928		text	   *targ1 = text_substring(arg1, 1, len2, false);
1929		text	   *targ2 = DatumGetTextPP(arg2);
1930
1931		result = (memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(targ1), VARDATA_ANY(targ2),
1932						 VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(targ2)) == 0);
1933
1934		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(targ1, 0);
1935		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(targ2, 1);
1936	}
1937
1938	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
1939}
1940
1941Datum
1942bttextcmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1943{
1944	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1945	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
1946	int32		result;
1947
1948	result = text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION());
1949
1950	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
1951	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
1952
1953	PG_RETURN_INT32(result);
1954}
1955
1956Datum
1957bttextsortsupport(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1958{
1959	SortSupport ssup = (SortSupport) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
1960	Oid			collid = ssup->ssup_collation;
1961	MemoryContext oldcontext;
1962
1963	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ssup->ssup_cxt);
1964
1965	/* Use generic string SortSupport */
1966	varstr_sortsupport(ssup, TEXTOID, collid);
1967
1968	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
1969
1970	PG_RETURN_VOID();
1971}
1972
1973/*
1974 * Generic sortsupport interface for character type's operator classes.
1975 * Includes locale support, and support for BpChar semantics (i.e. removing
1976 * trailing spaces before comparison).
1977 *
1978 * Relies on the assumption that text, VarChar, BpChar, and bytea all have the
1979 * same representation.  Callers that always use the C collation (e.g.
1980 * non-collatable type callers like bytea) may have NUL bytes in their strings;
1981 * this will not work with any other collation, though.
1982 */
1983void
1984varstr_sortsupport(SortSupport ssup, Oid typid, Oid collid)
1985{
1986	bool		abbreviate = ssup->abbreviate;
1987	bool		collate_c = false;
1988	VarStringSortSupport *sss;
1989	pg_locale_t locale;
1990
1991	check_collation_set(collid);
1992
1993	locale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
1994
1995	/*
1996	 * If possible, set ssup->comparator to a function which can be used to
1997	 * directly compare two datums.  If we can do this, we'll avoid the
1998	 * overhead of a trip through the fmgr layer for every comparison, which
1999	 * can be substantial.
2000	 *
2001	 * Most typically, we'll set the comparator to varlenafastcmp_locale,
2002	 * which uses strcoll() to perform comparisons.  We use that for the
2003	 * BpChar case too, but type NAME uses namefastcmp_locale. However, if
2004	 * LC_COLLATE = C, we can make things quite a bit faster with
2005	 * varstrfastcmp_c, bpcharfastcmp_c, or namefastcmp_c, all of which use
2006	 * memcmp() rather than strcoll().
2007	 */
2008	if (locale->collate_is_c)
2009	{
2010		if (typid == BPCHAROID)
2011			ssup->comparator = bpcharfastcmp_c;
2012		else if (typid == NAMEOID)
2013		{
2014			ssup->comparator = namefastcmp_c;
2015			/* Not supporting abbreviation with type NAME, for now */
2016			abbreviate = false;
2017		}
2018		else
2019			ssup->comparator = varstrfastcmp_c;
2020
2021		collate_c = true;
2022	}
2023	else
2024	{
2025		/*
2026		 * We use varlenafastcmp_locale except for type NAME.
2027		 */
2028		if (typid == NAMEOID)
2029		{
2030			ssup->comparator = namefastcmp_locale;
2031			/* Not supporting abbreviation with type NAME, for now */
2032			abbreviate = false;
2033		}
2034		else
2035			ssup->comparator = varlenafastcmp_locale;
2036
2037		/*
2038		 * Unfortunately, it seems that abbreviation for non-C collations is
2039		 * broken on many common platforms; see pg_strxfrm_enabled().
2040		 *
2041		 * Even apart from the risk of broken locales, it's possible that
2042		 * there are platforms where the use of abbreviated keys should be
2043		 * disabled at compile time.  Having only 4 byte datums could make
2044		 * worst-case performance drastically more likely, for example.
2045		 * Moreover, macOS's strxfrm() implementation is known to not
2046		 * effectively concentrate a significant amount of entropy from the
2047		 * original string in earlier transformed blobs.  It's possible that
2048		 * other supported platforms are similarly encumbered.  So, if we ever
2049		 * get past disabling this categorically, we may still want or need to
2050		 * disable it for particular platforms.
2051		 */
2052		if (!pg_strxfrm_enabled(locale))
2053			abbreviate = false;
2054	}
2055
2056	/*
2057	 * If we're using abbreviated keys, or if we're using a locale-aware
2058	 * comparison, we need to initialize a VarStringSortSupport object. Both
2059	 * cases will make use of the temporary buffers we initialize here for
2060	 * scratch space (and to detect requirement for BpChar semantics from
2061	 * caller), and the abbreviation case requires additional state.
2062	 */
2063	if (abbreviate || !collate_c)
2064	{
2065		sss = palloc(sizeof(VarStringSortSupport));
2066		sss->buf1 = palloc(TEXTBUFLEN);
2067		sss->buflen1 = TEXTBUFLEN;
2068		sss->buf2 = palloc(TEXTBUFLEN);
2069		sss->buflen2 = TEXTBUFLEN;
2070		/* Start with invalid values */
2071		sss->last_len1 = -1;
2072		sss->last_len2 = -1;
2073		/* Initialize */
2074		sss->last_returned = 0;
2075		if (collate_c)
2076			sss->locale = NULL;
2077		else
2078			sss->locale = locale;
2079
2080		/*
2081		 * To avoid somehow confusing a strxfrm() blob and an original string,
2082		 * constantly keep track of the variety of data that buf1 and buf2
2083		 * currently contain.
2084		 *
2085		 * Comparisons may be interleaved with conversion calls.  Frequently,
2086		 * conversions and comparisons are batched into two distinct phases,
2087		 * but the correctness of caching cannot hinge upon this.  For
2088		 * comparison caching, buffer state is only trusted if cache_blob is
2089		 * found set to false, whereas strxfrm() caching only trusts the state
2090		 * when cache_blob is found set to true.
2091		 *
2092		 * Arbitrarily initialize cache_blob to true.
2093		 */
2094		sss->cache_blob = true;
2095		sss->collate_c = collate_c;
2096		sss->typid = typid;
2097		ssup->ssup_extra = sss;
2098
2099		/*
2100		 * If possible, plan to use the abbreviated keys optimization.  The
2101		 * core code may switch back to authoritative comparator should
2102		 * abbreviation be aborted.
2103		 */
2104		if (abbreviate)
2105		{
2106			sss->prop_card = 0.20;
2107			initHyperLogLog(&sss->abbr_card, 10);
2108			initHyperLogLog(&sss->full_card, 10);
2109			ssup->abbrev_full_comparator = ssup->comparator;
2110			ssup->comparator = ssup_datum_unsigned_cmp;
2111			ssup->abbrev_converter = varstr_abbrev_convert;
2112			ssup->abbrev_abort = varstr_abbrev_abort;
2113		}
2114	}
2115}
2116
2117/*
2118 * sortsupport comparison func (for C locale case)
2119 */
2120static int
2121varstrfastcmp_c(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup)
2122{
2123	VarString  *arg1 = DatumGetVarStringPP(x);
2124	VarString  *arg2 = DatumGetVarStringPP(y);
2125	char	   *a1p,
2126			   *a2p;
2127	int			len1,
2128				len2,
2129				result;
2130
2131	a1p = VARDATA_ANY(arg1);
2132	a2p = VARDATA_ANY(arg2);
2133
2134	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
2135	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
2136
2137	result = memcmp(a1p, a2p, Min(len1, len2));
2138	if ((result == 0) && (len1 != len2))
2139		result = (len1 < len2) ? -1 : 1;
2140
2141	/* We can't afford to leak memory here. */
2142	if (PointerGetDatum(arg1) != x)
2143		pfree(arg1);
2144	if (PointerGetDatum(arg2) != y)
2145		pfree(arg2);
2146
2147	return result;
2148}
2149
2150/*
2151 * sortsupport comparison func (for BpChar C locale case)
2152 *
2153 * BpChar outsources its sortsupport to this module.  Specialization for the
2154 * varstr_sortsupport BpChar case, modeled on
2155 * internal_bpchar_pattern_compare().
2156 */
2157static int
2158bpcharfastcmp_c(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup)
2159{
2160	BpChar	   *arg1 = DatumGetBpCharPP(x);
2161	BpChar	   *arg2 = DatumGetBpCharPP(y);
2162	char	   *a1p,
2163			   *a2p;
2164	int			len1,
2165				len2,
2166				result;
2167
2168	a1p = VARDATA_ANY(arg1);
2169	a2p = VARDATA_ANY(arg2);
2170
2171	len1 = bpchartruelen(a1p, VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1));
2172	len2 = bpchartruelen(a2p, VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2));
2173
2174	result = memcmp(a1p, a2p, Min(len1, len2));
2175	if ((result == 0) && (len1 != len2))
2176		result = (len1 < len2) ? -1 : 1;
2177
2178	/* We can't afford to leak memory here. */
2179	if (PointerGetDatum(arg1) != x)
2180		pfree(arg1);
2181	if (PointerGetDatum(arg2) != y)
2182		pfree(arg2);
2183
2184	return result;
2185}
2186
2187/*
2188 * sortsupport comparison func (for NAME C locale case)
2189 */
2190static int
2191namefastcmp_c(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup)
2192{
2193	Name		arg1 = DatumGetName(x);
2194	Name		arg2 = DatumGetName(y);
2195
2196	return strncmp(NameStr(*arg1), NameStr(*arg2), NAMEDATALEN);
2197}
2198
2199/*
2200 * sortsupport comparison func (for locale case with all varlena types)
2201 */
2202static int
2203varlenafastcmp_locale(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup)
2204{
2205	VarString  *arg1 = DatumGetVarStringPP(x);
2206	VarString  *arg2 = DatumGetVarStringPP(y);
2207	char	   *a1p,
2208			   *a2p;
2209	int			len1,
2210				len2,
2211				result;
2212
2213	a1p = VARDATA_ANY(arg1);
2214	a2p = VARDATA_ANY(arg2);
2215
2216	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
2217	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
2218
2219	result = varstrfastcmp_locale(a1p, len1, a2p, len2, ssup);
2220
2221	/* We can't afford to leak memory here. */
2222	if (PointerGetDatum(arg1) != x)
2223		pfree(arg1);
2224	if (PointerGetDatum(arg2) != y)
2225		pfree(arg2);
2226
2227	return result;
2228}
2229
2230/*
2231 * sortsupport comparison func (for locale case with NAME type)
2232 */
2233static int
2234namefastcmp_locale(Datum x, Datum y, SortSupport ssup)
2235{
2236	Name		arg1 = DatumGetName(x);
2237	Name		arg2 = DatumGetName(y);
2238
2239	return varstrfastcmp_locale(NameStr(*arg1), strlen(NameStr(*arg1)),
2240								NameStr(*arg2), strlen(NameStr(*arg2)),
2241								ssup);
2242}
2243
2244/*
2245 * sortsupport comparison func for locale cases
2246 */
2247static int
2248varstrfastcmp_locale(char *a1p, int len1, char *a2p, int len2, SortSupport ssup)
2249{
2250	VarStringSortSupport *sss = (VarStringSortSupport *) ssup->ssup_extra;
2251	int			result;
2252	bool		arg1_match;
2253
2254	/* Fast pre-check for equality, as discussed in varstr_cmp() */
2255	if (len1 == len2 && memcmp(a1p, a2p, len1) == 0)
2256	{
2257		/*
2258		 * No change in buf1 or buf2 contents, so avoid changing last_len1 or
2259		 * last_len2.  Existing contents of buffers might still be used by
2260		 * next call.
2261		 *
2262		 * It's fine to allow the comparison of BpChar padding bytes here,
2263		 * even though that implies that the memcmp() will usually be
2264		 * performed for BpChar callers (though multibyte characters could
2265		 * still prevent that from occurring).  The memcmp() is still very
2266		 * cheap, and BpChar's funny semantics have us remove trailing spaces
2267		 * (not limited to padding), so we need make no distinction between
2268		 * padding space characters and "real" space characters.
2269		 */
2270		return 0;
2271	}
2272
2273	if (sss->typid == BPCHAROID)
2274	{
2275		/* Get true number of bytes, ignoring trailing spaces */
2276		len1 = bpchartruelen(a1p, len1);
2277		len2 = bpchartruelen(a2p, len2);
2278	}
2279
2280	if (len1 >= sss->buflen1)
2281	{
2282		sss->buflen1 = Max(len1 + 1, Min(sss->buflen1 * 2, MaxAllocSize));
2283		sss->buf1 = repalloc(sss->buf1, sss->buflen1);
2284	}
2285	if (len2 >= sss->buflen2)
2286	{
2287		sss->buflen2 = Max(len2 + 1, Min(sss->buflen2 * 2, MaxAllocSize));
2288		sss->buf2 = repalloc(sss->buf2, sss->buflen2);
2289	}
2290
2291	/*
2292	 * We're likely to be asked to compare the same strings repeatedly, and
2293	 * memcmp() is so much cheaper than strcoll() that it pays to try to cache
2294	 * comparisons, even though in general there is no reason to think that
2295	 * that will work out (every string datum may be unique).  Caching does
2296	 * not slow things down measurably when it doesn't work out, and can speed
2297	 * things up by rather a lot when it does.  In part, this is because the
2298	 * memcmp() compares data from cachelines that are needed in L1 cache even
2299	 * when the last comparison's result cannot be reused.
2300	 */
2301	arg1_match = true;
2302	if (len1 != sss->last_len1 || memcmp(sss->buf1, a1p, len1) != 0)
2303	{
2304		arg1_match = false;
2305		memcpy(sss->buf1, a1p, len1);
2306		sss->buf1[len1] = '\0';
2307		sss->last_len1 = len1;
2308	}
2309
2310	/*
2311	 * If we're comparing the same two strings as last time, we can return the
2312	 * same answer without calling strcoll() again.  This is more likely than
2313	 * it seems (at least with moderate to low cardinality sets), because
2314	 * quicksort compares the same pivot against many values.
2315	 */
2316	if (len2 != sss->last_len2 || memcmp(sss->buf2, a2p, len2) != 0)
2317	{
2318		memcpy(sss->buf2, a2p, len2);
2319		sss->buf2[len2] = '\0';
2320		sss->last_len2 = len2;
2321	}
2322	else if (arg1_match && !sss->cache_blob)
2323	{
2324		/* Use result cached following last actual strcoll() call */
2325		return sss->last_returned;
2326	}
2327
2328	result = pg_strcoll(sss->buf1, sss->buf2, sss->locale);
2329
2330	/* Break tie if necessary. */
2331	if (result == 0 && sss->locale->deterministic)
2332		result = strcmp(sss->buf1, sss->buf2);
2333
2334	/* Cache result, perhaps saving an expensive strcoll() call next time */
2335	sss->cache_blob = false;
2336	sss->last_returned = result;
2337	return result;
2338}
2339
2340/*
2341 * Conversion routine for sortsupport.  Converts original to abbreviated key
2342 * representation.  Our encoding strategy is simple -- pack the first 8 bytes
2343 * of a strxfrm() blob into a Datum (on little-endian machines, the 8 bytes are
2344 * stored in reverse order), and treat it as an unsigned integer.  When the "C"
2345 * locale is used, or in case of bytea, just memcpy() from original instead.
2346 */
2347static Datum
2348varstr_abbrev_convert(Datum original, SortSupport ssup)
2349{
2350	const size_t max_prefix_bytes = sizeof(Datum);
2351	VarStringSortSupport *sss = (VarStringSortSupport *) ssup->ssup_extra;
2352	VarString  *authoritative = DatumGetVarStringPP(original);
2353	char	   *authoritative_data = VARDATA_ANY(authoritative);
2354
2355	/* working state */
2356	Datum		res;
2357	char	   *pres;
2358	int			len;
2359	uint32		hash;
2360
2361	pres = (char *) &res;
2362	/* memset(), so any non-overwritten bytes are NUL */
2363	memset(pres, 0, max_prefix_bytes);
2364	len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(authoritative);
2365
2366	/* Get number of bytes, ignoring trailing spaces */
2367	if (sss->typid == BPCHAROID)
2368		len = bpchartruelen(authoritative_data, len);
2369
2370	/*
2371	 * If we're using the C collation, use memcpy(), rather than strxfrm(), to
2372	 * abbreviate keys.  The full comparator for the C locale is always
2373	 * memcmp().  It would be incorrect to allow bytea callers (callers that
2374	 * always force the C collation -- bytea isn't a collatable type, but this
2375	 * approach is convenient) to use strxfrm().  This is because bytea
2376	 * strings may contain NUL bytes.  Besides, this should be faster, too.
2377	 *
2378	 * More generally, it's okay that bytea callers can have NUL bytes in
2379	 * strings because abbreviated cmp need not make a distinction between
2380	 * terminating NUL bytes, and NUL bytes representing actual NULs in the
2381	 * authoritative representation.  Hopefully a comparison at or past one
2382	 * abbreviated key's terminating NUL byte will resolve the comparison
2383	 * without consulting the authoritative representation; specifically, some
2384	 * later non-NUL byte in the longer string can resolve the comparison
2385	 * against a subsequent terminating NUL in the shorter string.  There will
2386	 * usually be what is effectively a "length-wise" resolution there and
2387	 * then.
2388	 *
2389	 * If that doesn't work out -- if all bytes in the longer string
2390	 * positioned at or past the offset of the smaller string's (first)
2391	 * terminating NUL are actually representative of NUL bytes in the
2392	 * authoritative binary string (perhaps with some *terminating* NUL bytes
2393	 * towards the end of the longer string iff it happens to still be small)
2394	 * -- then an authoritative tie-breaker will happen, and do the right
2395	 * thing: explicitly consider string length.
2396	 */
2397	if (sss->collate_c)
2398		memcpy(pres, authoritative_data, Min(len, max_prefix_bytes));
2399	else
2400	{
2401		Size		bsize;
2402
2403		/*
2404		 * We're not using the C collation, so fall back on strxfrm or ICU
2405		 * analogs.
2406		 */
2407
2408		/* By convention, we use buffer 1 to store and NUL-terminate */
2409		if (len >= sss->buflen1)
2410		{
2411			sss->buflen1 = Max(len + 1, Min(sss->buflen1 * 2, MaxAllocSize));
2412			sss->buf1 = repalloc(sss->buf1, sss->buflen1);
2413		}
2414
2415		/* Might be able to reuse strxfrm() blob from last call */
2416		if (sss->last_len1 == len && sss->cache_blob &&
2417			memcmp(sss->buf1, authoritative_data, len) == 0)
2418		{
2419			memcpy(pres, sss->buf2, Min(max_prefix_bytes, sss->last_len2));
2420			/* No change affecting cardinality, so no hashing required */
2421			goto done;
2422		}
2423
2424		memcpy(sss->buf1, authoritative_data, len);
2425
2426		/*
2427		 * pg_strxfrm() and pg_strxfrm_prefix expect NUL-terminated strings.
2428		 */
2429		sss->buf1[len] = '\0';
2430		sss->last_len1 = len;
2431
2432		if (pg_strxfrm_prefix_enabled(sss->locale))
2433		{
2434			if (sss->buflen2 < max_prefix_bytes)
2435			{
2436				sss->buflen2 = Max(max_prefix_bytes,
2437								   Min(sss->buflen2 * 2, MaxAllocSize));
2438				sss->buf2 = repalloc(sss->buf2, sss->buflen2);
2439			}
2440
2441			bsize = pg_strxfrm_prefix(sss->buf2, sss->buf1,
2442									  max_prefix_bytes, sss->locale);
2443			sss->last_len2 = bsize;
2444		}
2445		else
2446		{
2447			/*
2448			 * Loop: Call pg_strxfrm(), possibly enlarge buffer, and try
2449			 * again.  The pg_strxfrm() function leaves the result buffer
2450			 * content undefined if the result did not fit, so we need to
2451			 * retry until everything fits, even though we only need the first
2452			 * few bytes in the end.
2453			 */
2454			for (;;)
2455			{
2456				bsize = pg_strxfrm(sss->buf2, sss->buf1, sss->buflen2,
2457								   sss->locale);
2458
2459				sss->last_len2 = bsize;
2460				if (bsize < sss->buflen2)
2461					break;
2462
2463				/*
2464				 * Grow buffer and retry.
2465				 */
2466				sss->buflen2 = Max(bsize + 1,
2467								   Min(sss->buflen2 * 2, MaxAllocSize));
2468				sss->buf2 = repalloc(sss->buf2, sss->buflen2);
2469			}
2470		}
2471
2472		/*
2473		 * Every Datum byte is always compared.  This is safe because the
2474		 * strxfrm() blob is itself NUL terminated, leaving no danger of
2475		 * misinterpreting any NUL bytes not intended to be interpreted as
2476		 * logically representing termination.
2477		 *
2478		 * (Actually, even if there were NUL bytes in the blob it would be
2479		 * okay.  See remarks on bytea case above.)
2480		 */
2481		memcpy(pres, sss->buf2, Min(max_prefix_bytes, bsize));
2482	}
2483
2484	/*
2485	 * Maintain approximate cardinality of both abbreviated keys and original,
2486	 * authoritative keys using HyperLogLog.  Used as cheap insurance against
2487	 * the worst case, where we do many string transformations for no saving
2488	 * in full strcoll()-based comparisons.  These statistics are used by
2489	 * varstr_abbrev_abort().
2490	 *
2491	 * First, Hash key proper, or a significant fraction of it.  Mix in length
2492	 * in order to compensate for cases where differences are past
2493	 * PG_CACHE_LINE_SIZE bytes, so as to limit the overhead of hashing.
2494	 */
2495	hash = DatumGetUInt32(hash_any((unsigned char *) authoritative_data,
2496								   Min(len, PG_CACHE_LINE_SIZE)));
2497
2498	if (len > PG_CACHE_LINE_SIZE)
2499		hash ^= DatumGetUInt32(hash_uint32((uint32) len));
2500
2501	addHyperLogLog(&sss->full_card, hash);
2502
2503	/* Hash abbreviated key */
2504#if SIZEOF_DATUM == 8
2505	{
2506		uint32		lohalf,
2507					hihalf;
2508
2509		lohalf = (uint32) res;
2510		hihalf = (uint32) (res >> 32);
2511		hash = DatumGetUInt32(hash_uint32(lohalf ^ hihalf));
2512	}
2513#else							/* SIZEOF_DATUM != 8 */
2514	hash = DatumGetUInt32(hash_uint32((uint32) res));
2515#endif
2516
2517	addHyperLogLog(&sss->abbr_card, hash);
2518
2519	/* Cache result, perhaps saving an expensive strxfrm() call next time */
2520	sss->cache_blob = true;
2521done:
2522
2523	/*
2524	 * Byteswap on little-endian machines.
2525	 *
2526	 * This is needed so that ssup_datum_unsigned_cmp() (an unsigned integer
2527	 * 3-way comparator) works correctly on all platforms.  If we didn't do
2528	 * this, the comparator would have to call memcmp() with a pair of
2529	 * pointers to the first byte of each abbreviated key, which is slower.
2530	 */
2531	res = DatumBigEndianToNative(res);
2532
2533	/* Don't leak memory here */
2534	if (PointerGetDatum(authoritative) != original)
2535		pfree(authoritative);
2536
2537	return res;
2538}
2539
2540/*
2541 * Callback for estimating effectiveness of abbreviated key optimization, using
2542 * heuristic rules.  Returns value indicating if the abbreviation optimization
2543 * should be aborted, based on its projected effectiveness.
2544 */
2545static bool
2546varstr_abbrev_abort(int memtupcount, SortSupport ssup)
2547{
2548	VarStringSortSupport *sss = (VarStringSortSupport *) ssup->ssup_extra;
2549	double		abbrev_distinct,
2550				key_distinct;
2551
2552	Assert(ssup->abbreviate);
2553
2554	/* Have a little patience */
2555	if (memtupcount < 100)
2556		return false;
2557
2558	abbrev_distinct = estimateHyperLogLog(&sss->abbr_card);
2559	key_distinct = estimateHyperLogLog(&sss->full_card);
2560
2561	/*
2562	 * Clamp cardinality estimates to at least one distinct value.  While
2563	 * NULLs are generally disregarded, if only NULL values were seen so far,
2564	 * that might misrepresent costs if we failed to clamp.
2565	 */
2566	if (abbrev_distinct <= 1.0)
2567		abbrev_distinct = 1.0;
2568
2569	if (key_distinct <= 1.0)
2570		key_distinct = 1.0;
2571
2572	/*
2573	 * In the worst case all abbreviated keys are identical, while at the same
2574	 * time there are differences within full key strings not captured in
2575	 * abbreviations.
2576	 */
2577	if (trace_sort)
2578	{
2579		double		norm_abbrev_card = abbrev_distinct / (double) memtupcount;
2580
2581		elog(LOG, "varstr_abbrev: abbrev_distinct after %d: %f "
2582			 "(key_distinct: %f, norm_abbrev_card: %f, prop_card: %f)",
2583			 memtupcount, abbrev_distinct, key_distinct, norm_abbrev_card,
2584			 sss->prop_card);
2585	}
2586
2587	/*
2588	 * If the number of distinct abbreviated keys approximately matches the
2589	 * number of distinct authoritative original keys, that's reason enough to
2590	 * proceed.  We can win even with a very low cardinality set if most
2591	 * tie-breakers only memcmp().  This is by far the most important
2592	 * consideration.
2593	 *
