2594	 * While comparisons that are resolved at the abbreviated key level are
2595	 * considerably cheaper than tie-breakers resolved with memcmp(), both of
2596	 * those two outcomes are so much cheaper than a full strcoll() once
2597	 * sorting is underway that it doesn't seem worth it to weigh abbreviated
2598	 * cardinality against the overall size of the set in order to more
2599	 * accurately model costs.  Assume that an abbreviated comparison, and an
2600	 * abbreviated comparison with a cheap memcmp()-based authoritative
2601	 * resolution are equivalent.
2602	 */
2603	if (abbrev_distinct > key_distinct * sss->prop_card)
2604	{
2605		/*
2606		 * When we have exceeded 10,000 tuples, decay required cardinality
2607		 * aggressively for next call.
2608		 *
2609		 * This is useful because the number of comparisons required on
2610		 * average increases at a linearithmic rate, and at roughly 10,000
2611		 * tuples that factor will start to dominate over the linear costs of
2612		 * string transformation (this is a conservative estimate).  The decay
2613		 * rate is chosen to be a little less aggressive than halving -- which
2614		 * (since we're called at points at which memtupcount has doubled)
2615		 * would never see the cost model actually abort past the first call
2616		 * following a decay.  This decay rate is mostly a precaution against
2617		 * a sudden, violent swing in how well abbreviated cardinality tracks
2618		 * full key cardinality.  The decay also serves to prevent a marginal
2619		 * case from being aborted too late, when too much has already been
2620		 * invested in string transformation.
2621		 *
2622		 * It's possible for sets of several million distinct strings with
2623		 * mere tens of thousands of distinct abbreviated keys to still
2624		 * benefit very significantly.  This will generally occur provided
2625		 * each abbreviated key is a proxy for a roughly uniform number of the
2626		 * set's full keys. If it isn't so, we hope to catch that early and
2627		 * abort.  If it isn't caught early, by the time the problem is
2628		 * apparent it's probably not worth aborting.
2629		 */
2630		if (memtupcount > 10000)
2631			sss->prop_card *= 0.65;
2632
2633		return false;
2634	}
2635
2636	/*
2637	 * Abort abbreviation strategy.
2638	 *
2639	 * The worst case, where all abbreviated keys are identical while all
2640	 * original strings differ will typically only see a regression of about
2641	 * 10% in execution time for small to medium sized lists of strings.
2642	 * Whereas on modern CPUs where cache stalls are the dominant cost, we can
2643	 * often expect very large improvements, particularly with sets of strings
2644	 * of moderately high to high abbreviated cardinality.  There is little to
2645	 * lose but much to gain, which our strategy reflects.
2646	 */
2647	if (trace_sort)
2648		elog(LOG, "varstr_abbrev: aborted abbreviation at %d "
2649			 "(abbrev_distinct: %f, key_distinct: %f, prop_card: %f)",
2650			 memtupcount, abbrev_distinct, key_distinct, sss->prop_card);
2651
2652	return true;
2653}
2654
2655/*
2656 * Generic equalimage support function for character type's operator classes.
2657 * Disables the use of deduplication with nondeterministic collations.
2658 */
2659Datum
2660btvarstrequalimage(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2661{
2662	/* Oid		opcintype = PG_GETARG_OID(0); */
2663	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
2664	pg_locale_t locale;
2665
2666	check_collation_set(collid);
2667
2668	locale = pg_newlocale_from_collation(collid);
2669
2670	PG_RETURN_BOOL(locale->deterministic);
2671}
2672
2673Datum
2674text_larger(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2675{
2676	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2677	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2678	text	   *result;
2679
2680	result = ((text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION()) > 0) ? arg1 : arg2);
2681
2682	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
2683}
2684
2685Datum
2686text_smaller(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2687{
2688	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2689	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2690	text	   *result;
2691
2692	result = ((text_cmp(arg1, arg2, PG_GET_COLLATION()) < 0) ? arg1 : arg2);
2693
2694	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
2695}
2696
2697
2698/*
2699 * Cross-type comparison functions for types text and name.
2700 */
2701
2702Datum
2703nameeqtext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2704{
2705	Name		arg1 = PG_GETARG_NAME(0);
2706	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2707	size_t		len1 = strlen(NameStr(*arg1));
2708	size_t		len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
2709	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
2710	bool		result;
2711
2712	check_collation_set(collid);
2713
2714	if (collid == C_COLLATION_OID)
2715		result = (len1 == len2 &&
2716				  memcmp(NameStr(*arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), len1) == 0);
2717	else
2718		result = (varstr_cmp(NameStr(*arg1), len1,
2719							 VARDATA_ANY(arg2), len2,
2720							 collid) == 0);
2721
2722	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2723
2724	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
2725}
2726
2727Datum
2728texteqname(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2729{
2730	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2731	Name		arg2 = PG_GETARG_NAME(1);
2732	size_t		len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
2733	size_t		len2 = strlen(NameStr(*arg2));
2734	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
2735	bool		result;
2736
2737	check_collation_set(collid);
2738
2739	if (collid == C_COLLATION_OID)
2740		result = (len1 == len2 &&
2741				  memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), NameStr(*arg2), len1) == 0);
2742	else
2743		result = (varstr_cmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), len1,
2744							 NameStr(*arg2), len2,
2745							 collid) == 0);
2746
2747	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2748
2749	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
2750}
2751
2752Datum
2753namenetext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2754{
2755	Name		arg1 = PG_GETARG_NAME(0);
2756	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2757	size_t		len1 = strlen(NameStr(*arg1));
2758	size_t		len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
2759	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
2760	bool		result;
2761
2762	check_collation_set(collid);
2763
2764	if (collid == C_COLLATION_OID)
2765		result = !(len1 == len2 &&
2766				   memcmp(NameStr(*arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), len1) == 0);
2767	else
2768		result = !(varstr_cmp(NameStr(*arg1), len1,
2769							  VARDATA_ANY(arg2), len2,
2770							  collid) == 0);
2771
2772	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2773
2774	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
2775}
2776
2777Datum
2778textnename(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2779{
2780	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2781	Name		arg2 = PG_GETARG_NAME(1);
2782	size_t		len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
2783	size_t		len2 = strlen(NameStr(*arg2));
2784	Oid			collid = PG_GET_COLLATION();
2785	bool		result;
2786
2787	check_collation_set(collid);
2788
2789	if (collid == C_COLLATION_OID)
2790		result = !(len1 == len2 &&
2791				   memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), NameStr(*arg2), len1) == 0);
2792	else
2793		result = !(varstr_cmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), len1,
2794							  NameStr(*arg2), len2,
2795							  collid) == 0);
2796
2797	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2798
2799	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
2800}
2801
2802Datum
2803btnametextcmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2804{
2805	Name		arg1 = PG_GETARG_NAME(0);
2806	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2807	int32		result;
2808
2809	result = varstr_cmp(NameStr(*arg1), strlen(NameStr(*arg1)),
2810						VARDATA_ANY(arg2), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2),
2811						PG_GET_COLLATION());
2812
2813	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2814
2815	PG_RETURN_INT32(result);
2816}
2817
2818Datum
2819bttextnamecmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2820{
2821	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2822	Name		arg2 = PG_GETARG_NAME(1);
2823	int32		result;
2824
2825	result = varstr_cmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1),
2826						NameStr(*arg2), strlen(NameStr(*arg2)),
2827						PG_GET_COLLATION());
2828
2829	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2830
2831	PG_RETURN_INT32(result);
2832}
2833
2834#define CmpCall(cmpfunc) \
2835	DatumGetInt32(DirectFunctionCall2Coll(cmpfunc, \
2836										  PG_GET_COLLATION(), \
2837										  PG_GETARG_DATUM(0), \
2838										  PG_GETARG_DATUM(1)))
2839
2840Datum
2841namelttext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2842{
2843	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(btnametextcmp) < 0);
2844}
2845
2846Datum
2847nameletext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2848{
2849	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(btnametextcmp) <= 0);
2850}
2851
2852Datum
2853namegttext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2854{
2855	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(btnametextcmp) > 0);
2856}
2857
2858Datum
2859namegetext(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2860{
2861	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(btnametextcmp) >= 0);
2862}
2863
2864Datum
2865textltname(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2866{
2867	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(bttextnamecmp) < 0);
2868}
2869
2870Datum
2871textlename(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2872{
2873	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(bttextnamecmp) <= 0);
2874}
2875
2876Datum
2877textgtname(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2878{
2879	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(bttextnamecmp) > 0);
2880}
2881
2882Datum
2883textgename(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2884{
2885	PG_RETURN_BOOL(CmpCall(bttextnamecmp) >= 0);
2886}
2887
2888#undef CmpCall
2889
2890
2891/*
2892 * The following operators support character-by-character comparison
2893 * of text datums, to allow building indexes suitable for LIKE clauses.
2894 * Note that the regular texteq/textne comparison operators, and regular
2895 * support functions 1 and 2 with "C" collation are assumed to be
2896 * compatible with these!
2897 */
2898
2899static int
2900internal_text_pattern_compare(text *arg1, text *arg2)
2901{
2902	int			result;
2903	int			len1,
2904				len2;
2905
2906	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
2907	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
2908
2909	result = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
2910	if (result != 0)
2911		return result;
2912	else if (len1 < len2)
2913		return -1;
2914	else if (len1 > len2)
2915		return 1;
2916	else
2917		return 0;
2918}
2919
2920
2921Datum
2922text_pattern_lt(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2923{
2924	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2925	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2926	int			result;
2927
2928	result = internal_text_pattern_compare(arg1, arg2);
2929
2930	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2931	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2932
2933	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result < 0);
2934}
2935
2936
2937Datum
2938text_pattern_le(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2939{
2940	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2941	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2942	int			result;
2943
2944	result = internal_text_pattern_compare(arg1, arg2);
2945
2946	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2947	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2948
2949	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result <= 0);
2950}
2951
2952
2953Datum
2954text_pattern_ge(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2955{
2956	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2957	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2958	int			result;
2959
2960	result = internal_text_pattern_compare(arg1, arg2);
2961
2962	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2963	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2964
2965	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result >= 0);
2966}
2967
2968
2969Datum
2970text_pattern_gt(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2971{
2972	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2973	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2974	int			result;
2975
2976	result = internal_text_pattern_compare(arg1, arg2);
2977
2978	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2979	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2980
2981	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result > 0);
2982}
2983
2984
2985Datum
2986bttext_pattern_cmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2987{
2988	text	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
2989	text	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
2990	int			result;
2991
2992	result = internal_text_pattern_compare(arg1, arg2);
2993
2994	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
2995	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
2996
2997	PG_RETURN_INT32(result);
2998}
2999
3000
3001Datum
3002bttext_pattern_sortsupport(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3003{
3004	SortSupport ssup = (SortSupport) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
3005	MemoryContext oldcontext;
3006
3007	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ssup->ssup_cxt);
3008
3009	/* Use generic string SortSupport, forcing "C" collation */
3010	varstr_sortsupport(ssup, TEXTOID, C_COLLATION_OID);
3011
3012	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
3013
3014	PG_RETURN_VOID();
3015}
3016
3017
3018/*-------------------------------------------------------------
3019 * byteaoctetlen
3020 *
3021 * get the number of bytes contained in an instance of type 'bytea'
3022 *-------------------------------------------------------------
3023 */
3024Datum
3025byteaoctetlen(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3026{
3027	Datum		str = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
3028
3029	/* We need not detoast the input at all */
3030	PG_RETURN_INT32(toast_raw_datum_size(str) - VARHDRSZ);
3031}
3032
3033/*
3034 * byteacat -
3035 *	  takes two bytea* and returns a bytea* that is the concatenation of
3036 *	  the two.
3037 *
3038 * Cloned from textcat and modified as required.
3039 */
3040Datum
3041byteacat(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3042{
3043	bytea	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3044	bytea	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
3045
3046	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(bytea_catenate(t1, t2));
3047}
3048
3049/*
3050 * bytea_catenate
3051 *	Guts of byteacat(), broken out so it can be used by other functions
3052 *
3053 * Arguments can be in short-header form, but not compressed or out-of-line
3054 */
3055static bytea *
3056bytea_catenate(bytea *t1, bytea *t2)
3057{
3058	bytea	   *result;
3059	int			len1,
3060				len2,
3061				len;
3062	char	   *ptr;
3063
3064	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t1);
3065	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2);
3066
3067	/* paranoia ... probably should throw error instead? */
3068	if (len1 < 0)
3069		len1 = 0;
3070	if (len2 < 0)
3071		len2 = 0;
3072
3073	len = len1 + len2 + VARHDRSZ;
3074	result = (bytea *) palloc(len);
3075
3076	/* Set size of result string... */
3077	SET_VARSIZE(result, len);
3078
3079	/* Fill data field of result string... */
3080	ptr = VARDATA(result);
3081	if (len1 > 0)
3082		memcpy(ptr, VARDATA_ANY(t1), len1);
3083	if (len2 > 0)
3084		memcpy(ptr + len1, VARDATA_ANY(t2), len2);
3085
3086	return result;
3087}
3088
3089#define PG_STR_GET_BYTEA(str_) \
3090	DatumGetByteaPP(DirectFunctionCall1(byteain, CStringGetDatum(str_)))
3091
3092/*
3093 * bytea_substr()
3094 * Return a substring starting at the specified position.
3095 * Cloned from text_substr and modified as required.
3096 *
3097 * Input:
3098 *	- string
3099 *	- starting position (is one-based)
3100 *	- string length (optional)
3101 *
3102 * If the starting position is zero or less, then return from the start of the string
3103 * adjusting the length to be consistent with the "negative start" per SQL.
3104 * If the length is less than zero, an ERROR is thrown. If no third argument
3105 * (length) is provided, the length to the end of the string is assumed.
3106 */
3107Datum
3108bytea_substr(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3109{
3110	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(bytea_substring(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0),
3111									  PG_GETARG_INT32(1),
3112									  PG_GETARG_INT32(2),
3113									  false));
3114}
3115
3116/*
3117 * bytea_substr_no_len -
3118 *	  Wrapper to avoid opr_sanity failure due to
3119 *	  one function accepting a different number of args.
3120 */
3121Datum
3122bytea_substr_no_len(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3123{
3124	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(bytea_substring(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0),
3125									  PG_GETARG_INT32(1),
3126									  -1,
3127									  true));
3128}
3129
3130static bytea *
3131bytea_substring(Datum str,
3132				int S,
3133				int L,
3134				bool length_not_specified)
3135{
3136	int32		S1;				/* adjusted start position */
3137	int32		L1;				/* adjusted substring length */
3138	int32		E;				/* end position */
3139
3140	/*
3141	 * The logic here should generally match text_substring().
3142	 */
3143	S1 = Max(S, 1);
3144
3145	if (length_not_specified)
3146	{
3147		/*
3148		 * Not passed a length - DatumGetByteaPSlice() grabs everything to the
3149		 * end of the string if we pass it a negative value for length.
3150		 */
3151		L1 = -1;
3152	}
3153	else if (L < 0)
3154	{
3155		/* SQL99 says to throw an error for E < S, i.e., negative length */
3156		ereport(ERROR,
3157				(errcode(ERRCODE_SUBSTRING_ERROR),
3158				 errmsg("negative substring length not allowed")));
3159		L1 = -1;				/* silence stupider compilers */
3160	}
3161	else if (pg_add_s32_overflow(S, L, &E))
3162	{
3163		/*
3164		 * L could be large enough for S + L to overflow, in which case the
3165		 * substring must run to end of string.
3166		 */
3167		L1 = -1;
3168	}
3169	else
3170	{
3171		/*
3172		 * A zero or negative value for the end position can happen if the
3173		 * start was negative or one. SQL99 says to return a zero-length
3174		 * string.
3175		 */
3176		if (E < 1)
3177			return PG_STR_GET_BYTEA("");
3178
3179		L1 = E - S1;
3180	}
3181
3182	/*
3183	 * If the start position is past the end of the string, SQL99 says to
3184	 * return a zero-length string -- DatumGetByteaPSlice() will do that for
3185	 * us.  We need only convert S1 to zero-based starting position.
3186	 */
3187	return DatumGetByteaPSlice(str, S1 - 1, L1);
3188}
3189
3190/*
3191 * byteaoverlay
3192 *	Replace specified substring of first string with second
3193 *
3194 * The SQL standard defines OVERLAY() in terms of substring and concatenation.
3195 * This code is a direct implementation of what the standard says.
3196 */
3197Datum
3198byteaoverlay(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3199{
3200	bytea	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3201	bytea	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
3202	int			sp = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);	/* substring start position */
3203	int			sl = PG_GETARG_INT32(3);	/* substring length */
3204
3205	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(bytea_overlay(t1, t2, sp, sl));
3206}
3207
3208Datum
3209byteaoverlay_no_len(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3210{
3211	bytea	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3212	bytea	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
3213	int			sp = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);	/* substring start position */
3214	int			sl;
3215
3216	sl = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2); /* defaults to length(t2) */
3217	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(bytea_overlay(t1, t2, sp, sl));
3218}
3219
3220static bytea *
3221bytea_overlay(bytea *t1, bytea *t2, int sp, int sl)
3222{
3223	bytea	   *result;
3224	bytea	   *s1;
3225	bytea	   *s2;
3226	int			sp_pl_sl;
3227
3228	/*
3229	 * Check for possible integer-overflow cases.  For negative sp, throw a
3230	 * "substring length" error because that's what should be expected
3231	 * according to the spec's definition of OVERLAY().
3232	 */
3233	if (sp <= 0)
3234		ereport(ERROR,
3235				(errcode(ERRCODE_SUBSTRING_ERROR),
3236				 errmsg("negative substring length not allowed")));
3237	if (pg_add_s32_overflow(sp, sl, &sp_pl_sl))
3238		ereport(ERROR,
3239				(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
3240				 errmsg("integer out of range")));
3241
3242	s1 = bytea_substring(PointerGetDatum(t1), 1, sp - 1, false);
3243	s2 = bytea_substring(PointerGetDatum(t1), sp_pl_sl, -1, true);
3244	result = bytea_catenate(s1, t2);
3245	result = bytea_catenate(result, s2);
3246
3247	return result;
3248}
3249
3250/*
3251 * bit_count
3252 */
3253Datum
3254bytea_bit_count(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3255{
3256	bytea	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3257
3258	PG_RETURN_INT64(pg_popcount(VARDATA_ANY(t1), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t1)));
3259}
3260
3261/*
3262 * byteapos -
3263 *	  Return the position of the specified substring.
3264 *	  Implements the SQL POSITION() function.
3265 * Cloned from textpos and modified as required.
3266 */
3267Datum
3268byteapos(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3269{
3270	bytea	   *t1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3271	bytea	   *t2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
3272	int			pos;
3273	int			px,
3274				p;
3275	int			len1,
3276				len2;
3277	char	   *p1,
3278			   *p2;
3279
3280	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t1);
3281	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t2);
3282
3283	if (len2 <= 0)
3284		PG_RETURN_INT32(1);		/* result for empty pattern */
3285
3286	p1 = VARDATA_ANY(t1);
3287	p2 = VARDATA_ANY(t2);
3288
3289	pos = 0;
3290	px = (len1 - len2);
3291	for (p = 0; p <= px; p++)
3292	{
3293		if ((*p2 == *p1) && (memcmp(p1, p2, len2) == 0))
3294		{
3295			pos = p + 1;
3296			break;
3297		};
3298		p1++;
3299	};
3300
3301	PG_RETURN_INT32(pos);
3302}
3303
3304/*-------------------------------------------------------------
3305 * byteaGetByte
3306 *
3307 * this routine treats "bytea" as an array of bytes.
3308 * It returns the Nth byte (a number between 0 and 255).
3309 *-------------------------------------------------------------
3310 */
3311Datum
3312byteaGetByte(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3313{
3314	bytea	   *v = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3315	int32		n = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
3316	int			len;
3317	int			byte;
3318
3319	len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(v);
3320
3321	if (n < 0 || n >= len)
3322		ereport(ERROR,
3323				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
3324				 errmsg("index %d out of valid range, 0..%d",
3325						n, len - 1)));
3326
3327	byte = ((unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(v))[n];
3328
3329	PG_RETURN_INT32(byte);
3330}
3331
3332/*-------------------------------------------------------------
3333 * byteaGetBit
3334 *
3335 * This routine treats a "bytea" type like an array of bits.
3336 * It returns the value of the Nth bit (0 or 1).
3337 *
3338 *-------------------------------------------------------------
3339 */
3340Datum
3341byteaGetBit(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3342{
3343	bytea	   *v = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3344	int64		n = PG_GETARG_INT64(1);
3345	int			byteNo,
3346				bitNo;
3347	int			len;
3348	int			byte;
3349
3350	len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(v);
3351
3352	if (n < 0 || n >= (int64) len * 8)
3353		ereport(ERROR,
3354				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
3355				 errmsg("index %" PRId64 " out of valid range, 0..%" PRId64,
3356						n, (int64) len * 8 - 1)));
3357
3358	/* n/8 is now known < len, so safe to cast to int */
3359	byteNo = (int) (n / 8);
3360	bitNo = (int) (n % 8);
3361
3362	byte = ((unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(v))[byteNo];
3363
3364	if (byte & (1 << bitNo))
3365		PG_RETURN_INT32(1);
3366	else
3367		PG_RETURN_INT32(0);
3368}
3369
3370/*-------------------------------------------------------------
3371 * byteaSetByte
3372 *
3373 * Given an instance of type 'bytea' creates a new one with
3374 * the Nth byte set to the given value.
3375 *
3376 *-------------------------------------------------------------
3377 */
3378Datum
3379byteaSetByte(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3380{
3381	bytea	   *res = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_P_COPY(0);
3382	int32		n = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
3383	int32		newByte = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
3384	int			len;
3385
3386	len = VARSIZE(res) - VARHDRSZ;
3387
3388	if (n < 0 || n >= len)
3389		ereport(ERROR,
3390				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
3391				 errmsg("index %d out of valid range, 0..%d",
3392						n, len - 1)));
3393
3394	/*
3395	 * Now set the byte.
3396	 */
3397	((unsigned char *) VARDATA(res))[n] = newByte;
3398
3399	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(res);
3400}
3401
3402/*-------------------------------------------------------------
3403 * byteaSetBit
3404 *
3405 * Given an instance of type 'bytea' creates a new one with
3406 * the Nth bit set to the given value.
3407 *
3408 *-------------------------------------------------------------
3409 */
3410Datum
3411byteaSetBit(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3412{
3413	bytea	   *res = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_P_COPY(0);
3414	int64		n = PG_GETARG_INT64(1);
3415	int32		newBit = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
3416	int			len;
3417	int			oldByte,
3418				newByte;
3419	int			byteNo,
3420				bitNo;
3421
3422	len = VARSIZE(res) - VARHDRSZ;
3423
3424	if (n < 0 || n >= (int64) len * 8)
3425		ereport(ERROR,
3426				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
3427				 errmsg("index %" PRId64 " out of valid range, 0..%" PRId64,
3428						n, (int64) len * 8 - 1)));
3429
3430	/* n/8 is now known < len, so safe to cast to int */
3431	byteNo = (int) (n / 8);
3432	bitNo = (int) (n % 8);
3433
3434	/*
3435	 * sanity check!
3436	 */
3437	if (newBit != 0 && newBit != 1)
3438		ereport(ERROR,
3439				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
3440				 errmsg("new bit must be 0 or 1")));
3441
3442	/*
3443	 * Update the byte.
3444	 */
3445	oldByte = ((unsigned char *) VARDATA(res))[byteNo];
3446
3447	if (newBit == 0)
3448		newByte = oldByte & (~(1 << bitNo));
3449	else
3450		newByte = oldByte | (1 << bitNo);
3451
3452	((unsigned char *) VARDATA(res))[byteNo] = newByte;
3453
3454	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(res);
3455}
3456
3457/*
3458 * Return reversed bytea
3459 */
3460Datum
3461bytea_reverse(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3462{
3463	bytea	   *v = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3464	const char *p = VARDATA_ANY(v);
3465	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(v);
3466	const char *endp = p + len;
3467	bytea	   *result = palloc(len + VARHDRSZ);
3468	char	   *dst = (char *) VARDATA(result) + len;
3469
3470	SET_VARSIZE(result, len + VARHDRSZ);
3471
3472	while (p < endp)
3473		*(--dst) = *p++;
3474
3475	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
3476}
3477
3478
3479/* text_name()
3480 * Converts a text type to a Name type.
3481 */
3482Datum
3483text_name(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3484{
3485	text	   *s = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
3486	Name		result;
3487	int			len;
3488
3489	len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(s);
3490
3491	/* Truncate oversize input */
3492	if (len >= NAMEDATALEN)
3493		len = pg_mbcliplen(VARDATA_ANY(s), len, NAMEDATALEN - 1);
3494
3495	/* We use palloc0 here to ensure result is zero-padded */
3496	result = (Name) palloc0(NAMEDATALEN);
3497	memcpy(NameStr(*result), VARDATA_ANY(s), len);
3498
3499	PG_RETURN_NAME(result);
3500}
3501
3502/* name_text()
3503 * Converts a Name type to a text type.
3504 */
3505Datum
3506name_text(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3507{
3508	Name		s = PG_GETARG_NAME(0);
3509
3510	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(NameStr(*s)));
3511}
3512
3513
3514/*
3515 * textToQualifiedNameList - convert a text object to list of names
3516 *
3517 * This implements the input parsing needed by nextval() and other
3518 * functions that take a text parameter representing a qualified name.
3519 * We split the name at dots, downcase if not double-quoted, and
3520 * truncate names if they're too long.
3521 */
3522List *
3523textToQualifiedNameList(text *textval)
3524{
3525	char	   *rawname;
3526	List	   *result = NIL;
3527	List	   *namelist;
3528	ListCell   *l;
3529
3530	/* Convert to C string (handles possible detoasting). */
3531	/* Note we rely on being able to modify rawname below. */
3532	rawname = text_to_cstring(textval);
3533
3534	if (!SplitIdentifierString(rawname, '.', &namelist))
3535		ereport(ERROR,
3536				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_NAME),
3537				 errmsg("invalid name syntax")));
3538
3539	if (namelist == NIL)
3540		ereport(ERROR,
3541				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_NAME),
3542				 errmsg("invalid name syntax")));
3543
3544	foreach(l, namelist)
3545	{
3546		char	   *curname = (char *) lfirst(l);
3547
3548		result = lappend(result, makeString(pstrdup(curname)));
3549	}
3550
3551	pfree(rawname);
3552	list_free(namelist);
3553
3554	return result;
3555}
3556
3557/*
3558 * SplitIdentifierString --- parse a string containing identifiers
3559 *
3560 * This is the guts of textToQualifiedNameList, and is exported for use in
3561 * other situations such as parsing GUC variables.  In the GUC case, it's
3562 * important to avoid memory leaks, so the API is designed to minimize the
3563 * amount of stuff that needs to be allocated and freed.
3564 *
3565 * Inputs:
3566 *	rawstring: the input string; must be overwritable!	On return, it's
3567 *			   been modified to contain the separated identifiers.
3568 *	separator: the separator punctuation expected between identifiers
3569 *			   (typically '.' or ',').  Whitespace may also appear around
3570 *			   identifiers.
3571 * Outputs:
3572 *	namelist: filled with a palloc'd list of pointers to identifiers within
3573 *			  rawstring.  Caller should list_free() this even on error return.
3574 *
3575 * Returns true if okay, false if there is a syntax error in the string.
3576 *
3577 * Note that an empty string is considered okay here, though not in
3578 * textToQualifiedNameList.
3579 */
3580bool
3581SplitIdentifierString(char *rawstring, char separator,
3582					  List **namelist)
3583{
3584	char	   *nextp = rawstring;
3585	bool		done = false;
3586
3587	*namelist = NIL;
3588
3589	while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3590		nextp++;				/* skip leading whitespace */
3591
3592	if (*nextp == '\0')
3593		return true;			/* allow empty string */
3594
3595	/* At the top of the loop, we are at start of a new identifier. */
3596	do
3597	{
3598		char	   *curname;
3599		char	   *endp;
3600
3601		if (*nextp == '"')
3602		{
3603			/* Quoted name --- collapse quote-quote pairs, no downcasing */
3604			curname = nextp + 1;
3605			for (;;)
3606			{
3607				endp = strchr(nextp + 1, '"');
3608				if (endp == NULL)
3609					return false;	/* mismatched quotes */
3610				if (endp[1] != '"')
3611					break;		/* found end of quoted name */
3612				/* Collapse adjacent quotes into one quote, and look again */
3613				memmove(endp, endp + 1, strlen(endp));
3614				nextp = endp;
3615			}
3616			/* endp now points at the terminating quote */
3617			nextp = endp + 1;
3618		}
3619		else
3620		{
3621			/* Unquoted name --- extends to separator or whitespace */
3622			char	   *downname;
3623			int			len;
3624
3625			curname = nextp;
3626			while (*nextp && *nextp != separator &&
3627				   !scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3628				nextp++;
3629			endp = nextp;
3630			if (curname == nextp)
3631				return false;	/* empty unquoted name not allowed */
3632
3633			/*
3634			 * Downcase the identifier, using same code as main lexer does.
3635			 *
3636			 * XXX because we want to overwrite the input in-place, we cannot
3637			 * support a downcasing transformation that increases the string
3638			 * length.  This is not a problem given the current implementation
3639			 * of downcase_truncate_identifier, but we'll probably have to do
3640			 * something about this someday.
3641			 */
3642			len = endp - curname;
3643			downname = downcase_truncate_identifier(curname, len, false);
3644			Assert(strlen(downname) <= len);
3645			strncpy(curname, downname, len);	/* strncpy is required here */
3646			pfree(downname);
3647		}
3648
3649		while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3650			nextp++;			/* skip trailing whitespace */
3651
3652		if (*nextp == separator)
3653		{
3654			nextp++;
3655			while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3656				nextp++;		/* skip leading whitespace for next */
3657			/* we expect another name, so done remains false */
3658		}
3659		else if (*nextp == '\0')
3660			done = true;
3661		else
3662			return false;		/* invalid syntax */
3663
3664		/* Now safe to overwrite separator with a null */
3665		*endp = '\0';
3666
3667		/* Truncate name if it's overlength */
3668		truncate_identifier(curname, strlen(curname), false);
3669
3670		/*
3671		 * Finished isolating current name --- add it to list
3672		 */
3673		*namelist = lappend(*namelist, curname);
3674
3675		/* Loop back if we didn't reach end of string */
3676	} while (!done);
3677
3678	return true;
3679}
3680
3681
3682/*
3683 * SplitDirectoriesString --- parse a string containing file/directory names
3684 *
3685 * This works fine on file names too; the function name is historical.
3686 *
3687 * This is similar to SplitIdentifierString, except that the parsing
3688 * rules are meant to handle pathnames instead of identifiers: there is
3689 * no downcasing, embedded spaces are allowed, the max length is MAXPGPATH-1,
3690 * and we apply canonicalize_path() to each extracted string.  Because of the
3691 * last, the returned strings are separately palloc'd rather than being
3692 * pointers into rawstring --- but we still scribble on rawstring.
3693 *
3694 * Inputs:
3695 *	rawstring: the input string; must be modifiable!
3696 *	separator: the separator punctuation expected between directories
3697 *			   (typically ',' or ';').  Whitespace may also appear around
3698 *			   directories.
3699 * Outputs:
3700 *	namelist: filled with a palloc'd list of directory names.
3701 *			  Caller should list_free_deep() this even on error return.
3702 *
3703 * Returns true if okay, false if there is a syntax error in the string.
3704 *
3705 * Note that an empty string is considered okay here.
3706 */
3707bool
3708SplitDirectoriesString(char *rawstring, char separator,
3709					   List **namelist)
3710{
3711	char	   *nextp = rawstring;
3712	bool		done = false;
3713
3714	*namelist = NIL;
3715
3716	while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3717		nextp++;				/* skip leading whitespace */
3718
3719	if (*nextp == '\0')
3720		return true;			/* allow empty string */
3721
3722	/* At the top of the loop, we are at start of a new directory. */
3723	do
3724	{
3725		char	   *curname;
3726		char	   *endp;
3727
3728		if (*nextp == '"')
3729		{
3730			/* Quoted name --- collapse quote-quote pairs */
3731			curname = nextp + 1;
3732			for (;;)
3733			{
3734				endp = strchr(nextp + 1, '"');
3735				if (endp == NULL)
3736					return false;	/* mismatched quotes */
3737				if (endp[1] != '"')
3738					break;		/* found end of quoted name */
3739				/* Collapse adjacent quotes into one quote, and look again */
3740				memmove(endp, endp + 1, strlen(endp));
3741				nextp = endp;
3742			}
3743			/* endp now points at the terminating quote */
3744			nextp = endp + 1;
3745		}
3746		else
3747		{
3748			/* Unquoted name --- extends to separator or end of string */
3749			curname = endp = nextp;
3750			while (*nextp && *nextp != separator)
3751			{
3752				/* trailing whitespace should not be included in name */
3753				if (!scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3754					endp = nextp + 1;
3755				nextp++;
3756			}
3757			if (curname == endp)
3758				return false;	/* empty unquoted name not allowed */
3759		}
3760
3761		while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3762			nextp++;			/* skip trailing whitespace */
3763
3764		if (*nextp == separator)
3765		{
3766			nextp++;
3767			while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3768				nextp++;		/* skip leading whitespace for next */
3769			/* we expect another name, so done remains false */
3770		}
3771		else if (*nextp == '\0')
3772			done = true;
3773		else
3774			return false;		/* invalid syntax */
3775
3776		/* Now safe to overwrite separator with a null */
3777		*endp = '\0';
3778
3779		/* Truncate path if it's overlength */
3780		if (strlen(curname) >= MAXPGPATH)
3781			curname[MAXPGPATH - 1] = '\0';
3782
3783		/*
3784		 * Finished isolating current name --- add it to list
3785		 */
3786		curname = pstrdup(curname);
3787		canonicalize_path(curname);
3788		*namelist = lappend(*namelist, curname);
3789
3790		/* Loop back if we didn't reach end of string */
3791	} while (!done);
3792
3793	return true;
3794}
3795
3796
3797/*
3798 * SplitGUCList --- parse a string containing identifiers or file names
3799 *
3800 * This is used to split the value of a GUC_LIST_QUOTE GUC variable, without
3801 * presuming whether the elements will be taken as identifiers or file names.
3802 * We assume the input has already been through flatten_set_variable_args(),
3803 * so that we need never downcase (if appropriate, that was done already).
3804 * Nor do we ever truncate, since we don't know the correct max length.
3805 * We disallow embedded whitespace for simplicity (it shouldn't matter,
3806 * because any embedded whitespace should have led to double-quoting).
3807 * Otherwise the API is identical to SplitIdentifierString.
3808 *
3809 * XXX it's annoying to have so many copies of this string-splitting logic.
3810 * However, it's not clear that having one function with a bunch of option
3811 * flags would be much better.
3812 *
3813 * XXX there is a version of this function in src/bin/pg_dump/dumputils.c.
3814 * Be sure to update that if you have to change this.
3815 *
3816 * Inputs:
3817 *	rawstring: the input string; must be overwritable!	On return, it's
3818 *			   been modified to contain the separated identifiers.
3819 *	separator: the separator punctuation expected between identifiers
3820 *			   (typically '.' or ',').  Whitespace may also appear around
3821 *			   identifiers.
3822 * Outputs:
3823 *	namelist: filled with a palloc'd list of pointers to identifiers within
3824 *			  rawstring.  Caller should list_free() this even on error return.
3825 *
3826 * Returns true if okay, false if there is a syntax error in the string.
3827 */
3828bool
3829SplitGUCList(char *rawstring, char separator,
3830			 List **namelist)
3831{
3832	char	   *nextp = rawstring;
3833	bool		done = false;
3834
3835	*namelist = NIL;
3836
3837	while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3838		nextp++;				/* skip leading whitespace */
3839
3840	if (*nextp == '\0')
3841		return true;			/* allow empty string */
3842
3843	/* At the top of the loop, we are at start of a new identifier. */
3844	do
3845	{
3846		char	   *curname;
3847		char	   *endp;
3848
3849		if (*nextp == '"')
3850		{
3851			/* Quoted name --- collapse quote-quote pairs */
3852			curname = nextp + 1;
3853			for (;;)
3854			{
3855				endp = strchr(nextp + 1, '"');
3856				if (endp == NULL)
3857					return false;	/* mismatched quotes */
3858				if (endp[1] != '"')
3859					break;		/* found end of quoted name */
3860				/* Collapse adjacent quotes into one quote, and look again */
3861				memmove(endp, endp + 1, strlen(endp));
3862				nextp = endp;
3863			}
3864			/* endp now points at the terminating quote */
3865			nextp = endp + 1;
3866		}
3867		else
3868		{
3869			/* Unquoted name --- extends to separator or whitespace */
3870			curname = nextp;
3871			while (*nextp && *nextp != separator &&
3872				   !scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3873				nextp++;
3874			endp = nextp;
3875			if (curname == nextp)
3876				return false;	/* empty unquoted name not allowed */
3877		}
3878
3879		while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3880			nextp++;			/* skip trailing whitespace */
3881
3882		if (*nextp == separator)
3883		{
3884			nextp++;
3885			while (scanner_isspace(*nextp))
3886				nextp++;		/* skip leading whitespace for next */
3887			/* we expect another name, so done remains false */
3888		}
3889		else if (*nextp == '\0')
3890			done = true;
3891		else
3892			return false;		/* invalid syntax */
3893
3894		/* Now safe to overwrite separator with a null */
3895		*endp = '\0';
3896
3897		/*
3898		 * Finished isolating current name --- add it to list
3899		 */
3900		*namelist = lappend(*namelist, curname);
3901
3902		/* Loop back if we didn't reach end of string */
3903	} while (!done);
3904
3905	return true;
3906}
3907
3908
3909/*****************************************************************************
3910 *	Comparison Functions used for bytea
3911 *
3912 * Note: btree indexes need these routines not to leak memory; therefore,
3913 * be careful to free working copies of toasted datums.  Most places don't
3914 * need to be so careful.
3915 *****************************************************************************/
3916
3917Datum
3918byteaeq(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3919{
3920	Datum		arg1 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
3921	Datum		arg2 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(1);
3922	bool		result;
3923	Size		len1,
3924				len2;
3925
3926	/*
3927	 * We can use a fast path for unequal lengths, which might save us from
3928	 * having to detoast one or both values.
3929	 */
3930	len1 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg1);
3931	len2 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg2);
3932	if (len1 != len2)
3933		result = false;
3934	else
3935	{
3936		bytea	   *barg1 = DatumGetByteaPP(arg1);
3937		bytea	   *barg2 = DatumGetByteaPP(arg2);
3938
3939		result = (memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(barg1), VARDATA_ANY(barg2),
3940						 len1 - VARHDRSZ) == 0);
3941
3942		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(barg1, 0);
3943		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(barg2, 1);
3944	}
3945
3946	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
3947}
3948
3949Datum
3950byteane(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3951{
3952	Datum		arg1 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
3953	Datum		arg2 = PG_GETARG_DATUM(1);
3954	bool		result;
3955	Size		len1,
3956				len2;
3957
3958	/*
3959	 * We can use a fast path for unequal lengths, which might save us from
3960	 * having to detoast one or both values.
3961	 */
3962	len1 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg1);
3963	len2 = toast_raw_datum_size(arg2);
3964	if (len1 != len2)
3965		result = true;
3966	else
3967	{
3968		bytea	   *barg1 = DatumGetByteaPP(arg1);
3969		bytea	   *barg2 = DatumGetByteaPP(arg2);
3970
3971		result = (memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(barg1), VARDATA_ANY(barg2),
3972						 len1 - VARHDRSZ) != 0);
3973
3974		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(barg1, 0);
3975		PG_FREE_IF_COPY(barg2, 1);
3976	}
3977
3978	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
3979}
3980
3981Datum
3982bytealt(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
3983{
3984	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
3985	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
3986	int			len1,
3987				len2;
3988	int			cmp;
3989
3990	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
3991	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
3992
3993	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
3994
3995	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
3996	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
3997
3998	PG_RETURN_BOOL((cmp < 0) || ((cmp == 0) && (len1 < len2)));
3999}
4000
4001Datum
4002byteale(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4003{
4004	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4005	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
4006	int			len1,
4007				len2;
4008	int			cmp;
4009
4010	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
4011	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
4012
4013	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
4014
4015	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
4016	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
4017
4018	PG_RETURN_BOOL((cmp < 0) || ((cmp == 0) && (len1 <= len2)));
4019}
4020
4021Datum
4022byteagt(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4023{
4024	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4025	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
4026	int			len1,
4027				len2;
4028	int			cmp;
4029
4030	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
4031	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
4032
4033	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
4034
4035	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
4036	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
4037
4038	PG_RETURN_BOOL((cmp > 0) || ((cmp == 0) && (len1 > len2)));
4039}
4040
4041Datum
4042byteage(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4043{
4044	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4045	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
4046	int			len1,
4047				len2;
4048	int			cmp;
4049
4050	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
4051	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
4052
4053	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
4054
4055	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
4056	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
4057
4058	PG_RETURN_BOOL((cmp > 0) || ((cmp == 0) && (len1 >= len2)));
4059}
4060
4061Datum
4062byteacmp(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4063{
4064	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4065	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
4066	int			len1,
4067				len2;
4068	int			cmp;
4069
4070	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
4071	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
4072
4073	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
4074	if ((cmp == 0) && (len1 != len2))
4075		cmp = (len1 < len2) ? -1 : 1;
4076
4077	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg1, 0);
4078	PG_FREE_IF_COPY(arg2, 1);
4079
4080	PG_RETURN_INT32(cmp);
4081}
4082
4083Datum
4084bytea_larger(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4085{
4086	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4087	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
4088	bytea	   *result;
4089	int			len1,
4090				len2;
4091	int			cmp;
4092
4093	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
4094	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
4095
4096	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
4097	result = ((cmp > 0) || ((cmp == 0) && (len1 > len2)) ? arg1 : arg2);
4098
4099	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
4100}
4101
4102Datum
4103bytea_smaller(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4104{
4105	bytea	   *arg1 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4106	bytea	   *arg2 = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(1);
4107	bytea	   *result;
4108	int			len1,
4109				len2;
4110	int			cmp;
4111
4112	len1 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg1);
4113	len2 = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(arg2);
4114
4115	cmp = memcmp(VARDATA_ANY(arg1), VARDATA_ANY(arg2), Min(len1, len2));
4116	result = ((cmp < 0) || ((cmp == 0) && (len1 < len2)) ? arg1 : arg2);
4117
4118	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
4119}
4120
4121Datum
4122bytea_sortsupport(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4123{
4124	SortSupport ssup = (SortSupport) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
4125	MemoryContext oldcontext;
4126
4127	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(ssup->ssup_cxt);
4128
4129	/* Use generic string SortSupport, forcing "C" collation */
4130	varstr_sortsupport(ssup, BYTEAOID, C_COLLATION_OID);
4131
4132	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
4133
4134	PG_RETURN_VOID();
4135}
4136
4137/* Cast bytea -> int2 */
4138Datum
4139bytea_int2(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4140{
4141	bytea	   *v = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4142	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(v);
4143	uint16		result;
4144
4145	/* Check that the byte array is not too long */
4146	if (len > sizeof(result))
4147		ereport(ERROR,
4148				errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
4149				errmsg("smallint out of range"));
4150
4151	/* Convert it to an integer; most significant bytes come first */
4152	result = 0;
4153	for (int i = 0; i < len; i++)
4154	{
4155		result <<= BITS_PER_BYTE;
4156		result |= ((unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(v))[i];
4157	}
4158
4159	PG_RETURN_INT16(result);
4160}
4161
4162/* Cast bytea -> int4 */
4163Datum
4164bytea_int4(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4165{
4166	bytea	   *v = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4167	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(v);
4168	uint32		result;
4169
4170	/* Check that the byte array is not too long */
4171	if (len > sizeof(result))
4172		ereport(ERROR,
4173				errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
4174				errmsg("integer out of range"));
4175
4176	/* Convert it to an integer; most significant bytes come first */
4177	result = 0;
4178	for (int i = 0; i < len; i++)
4179	{
4180		result <<= BITS_PER_BYTE;
4181		result |= ((unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(v))[i];
4182	}
4183
4184	PG_RETURN_INT32(result);
4185}
4186
4187/* Cast bytea -> int8 */
4188Datum
4189bytea_int8(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4190{
4191	bytea	   *v = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
4192	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(v);
4193	uint64		result;
4194
4195	/* Check that the byte array is not too long */
4196	if (len > sizeof(result))
4197		ereport(ERROR,
4198				errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
4199				errmsg("bigint out of range"));
4200
4201	/* Convert it to an integer; most significant bytes come first */
4202	result = 0;
4203	for (int i = 0; i < len; i++)
4204	{
4205		result <<= BITS_PER_BYTE;
4206		result |= ((unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(v))[i];
4207	}
4208
4209	PG_RETURN_INT64(result);
4210}
4211
4212/* Cast int2 -> bytea; can just use int2send() */
4213Datum
4214int2_bytea(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4215{
4216	return int2send(fcinfo);
4217}
4218
4219/* Cast int4 -> bytea; can just use int4send() */
4220Datum
4221int4_bytea(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4222{
4223	return int4send(fcinfo);
4224}
4225
4226/* Cast int8 -> bytea; can just use int8send() */
4227Datum
4228int8_bytea(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4229{
4230	return int8send(fcinfo);
4231}
4232
4233/*
4234 * appendStringInfoText
4235 *
4236 * Append a text to str.
4237 * Like appendStringInfoString(str, text_to_cstring(t)) but faster.
4238 */
4239static void
4240appendStringInfoText(StringInfo str, const text *t)
4241{
4242	appendBinaryStringInfo(str, VARDATA_ANY(t), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(t));
4243}
4244
4245/*
4246 * replace_text
4247 * replace all occurrences of 'old_sub_str' in 'orig_str'
4248 * with 'new_sub_str' to form 'new_str'
4249 *
4250 * returns 'orig_str' if 'old_sub_str' == '' or 'orig_str' == ''
4251 * otherwise returns 'new_str'
4252 */
4253Datum
4254replace_text(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4255{
4256	text	   *src_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
4257	text	   *from_sub_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
4258	text	   *to_sub_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(2);
4259	int			src_text_len;
4260	int			from_sub_text_len;
4261	TextPositionState state;
4262	text	   *ret_text;
4263	int			chunk_len;
4264	char	   *curr_ptr;
4265	char	   *start_ptr;
4266	StringInfoData str;
4267	bool		found;
4268
4269	src_text_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(src_text);
4270	from_sub_text_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(from_sub_text);
4271
4272	/* Return unmodified source string if empty source or pattern */
4273	if (src_text_len < 1 || from_sub_text_len < 1)
4274	{
4275		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(src_text);
4276	}
4277
4278	text_position_setup(src_text, from_sub_text, PG_GET_COLLATION(), &state);
4279
4280	found = text_position_next(&state);
4281
4282	/* When the from_sub_text is not found, there is nothing to do. */
4283	if (!found)
4284	{
4285		text_position_cleanup(&state);
4286		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(src_text);
4287	}
4288	curr_ptr = text_position_get_match_ptr(&state);
4289	start_ptr = VARDATA_ANY(src_text);
4290
4291	initStringInfo(&str);
4292
4293	do
4294	{
4295		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
4296
4297		/* copy the data skipped over by last text_position_next() */
4298		chunk_len = curr_ptr - start_ptr;
4299		appendBinaryStringInfo(&str, start_ptr, chunk_len);
4300
4301		appendStringInfoText(&str, to_sub_text);
4302
4303		start_ptr = curr_ptr + state.last_match_len;
4304
4305		found = text_position_next(&state);
4306		if (found)
4307			curr_ptr = text_position_get_match_ptr(&state);
4308	}
4309	while (found);
4310
4311	/* copy trailing data */
4312	chunk_len = ((char *) src_text + VARSIZE_ANY(src_text)) - start_ptr;
4313	appendBinaryStringInfo(&str, start_ptr, chunk_len);
4314
4315	text_position_cleanup(&state);
4316
4317	ret_text = cstring_to_text_with_len(str.data, str.len);
4318	pfree(str.data);
4319
4320	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(ret_text);
4321}
4322
4323/*
4324 * check_replace_text_has_escape
4325 *
4326 * Returns 0 if text contains no backslashes that need processing.
4327 * Returns 1 if text contains backslashes, but not regexp submatch specifiers.
4328 * Returns 2 if text contains regexp submatch specifiers (\1 .. \9).
4329 */
4330static int
4331check_replace_text_has_escape(const text *replace_text)
4332{
4333	int			result = 0;
4334	const char *p = VARDATA_ANY(replace_text);
4335	const char *p_end = p + VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(replace_text);
4336
4337	while (p < p_end)
4338	{
4339		/* Find next escape char, if any. */
4340		p = memchr(p, '\\', p_end - p);
4341		if (p == NULL)
4342			break;
4343		p++;
4344		/* Note: a backslash at the end doesn't require extra processing. */
4345		if (p < p_end)
4346		{
4347			if (*p >= '1' && *p <= '9')
4348				return 2;		/* Found a submatch specifier, so done */
4349			result = 1;			/* Found some other sequence, keep looking */
4350			p++;
4351		}
4352	}
4353	return result;
4354}
4355
4356/*
4357 * appendStringInfoRegexpSubstr
4358 *
4359 * Append replace_text to str, substituting regexp back references for
4360 * \n escapes.  start_ptr is the start of the match in the source string,
4361 * at logical character position data_pos.
4362 */
4363static void
4364appendStringInfoRegexpSubstr(StringInfo str, text *replace_text,
4365							 regmatch_t *pmatch,
4366							 char *start_ptr, int data_pos)
4367{
4368	const char *p = VARDATA_ANY(replace_text);
4369	const char *p_end = p + VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(replace_text);
4370
4371	while (p < p_end)
4372	{
4373		const char *chunk_start = p;
4374		int			so;
4375		int			eo;
4376
4377		/* Find next escape char, if any. */
4378		p = memchr(p, '\\', p_end - p);
4379		if (p == NULL)
4380			p = p_end;
4381
4382		/* Copy the text we just scanned over, if any. */
4383		if (p > chunk_start)
4384			appendBinaryStringInfo(str, chunk_start, p - chunk_start);
4385
4386		/* Done if at end of string, else advance over escape char. */
4387		if (p >= p_end)
4388			break;
4389		p++;
4390
4391		if (p >= p_end)
4392		{
4393			/* Escape at very end of input.  Treat same as unexpected char */
4394			appendStringInfoChar(str, '\\');
4395			break;
4396		}
4397
4398		if (*p >= '1' && *p <= '9')
4399		{
4400			/* Use the back reference of regexp. */
4401			int			idx = *p - '0';
4402
4403			so = pmatch[idx].rm_so;
4404			eo = pmatch[idx].rm_eo;
4405			p++;
4406		}
4407		else if (*p == '&')
4408		{
4409			/* Use the entire matched string. */
4410			so = pmatch[0].rm_so;
4411			eo = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
4412			p++;
4413		}
4414		else if (*p == '\\')
4415		{
4416			/* \\ means transfer one \ to output. */
4417			appendStringInfoChar(str, '\\');
4418			p++;
4419			continue;
4420		}
4421		else
4422		{
4423			/*
4424			 * If escape char is not followed by any expected char, just treat
4425			 * it as ordinary data to copy.  (XXX would it be better to throw
4426			 * an error?)
4427			 */
4428			appendStringInfoChar(str, '\\');
4429			continue;
4430		}
4431
4432		if (so >= 0 && eo >= 0)
4433		{
4434			/*
4435			 * Copy the text that is back reference of regexp.  Note so and eo
4436			 * are counted in characters not bytes.
4437			 */
4438			char	   *chunk_start;
4439			int			chunk_len;
4440
4441			Assert(so >= data_pos);
4442			chunk_start = start_ptr;
4443			chunk_start += charlen_to_bytelen(chunk_start, so - data_pos);
4444			chunk_len = charlen_to_bytelen(chunk_start, eo - so);
4445			appendBinaryStringInfo(str, chunk_start, chunk_len);
4446		}
4447	}
4448}
4449
4450/*
4451 * replace_text_regexp
4452 *
4453 * replace substring(s) in src_text that match pattern with replace_text.
4454 * The replace_text can contain backslash markers to substitute
4455 * (parts of) the matched text.
4456 *
4457 * cflags: regexp compile flags.
4458 * collation: collation to use.
4459 * search_start: the character (not byte) offset in src_text at which to
4460 * begin searching.
4461 * n: if 0, replace all matches; if > 0, replace only the N'th match.
4462 */
4463text *
4464replace_text_regexp(text *src_text, text *pattern_text,
4465					text *replace_text,
4466					int cflags, Oid collation,
4467					int search_start, int n)
4468{
4469	text	   *ret_text;
4470	regex_t    *re;
4471	int			src_text_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(src_text);
4472	int			nmatches = 0;
4473	StringInfoData buf;
4474	regmatch_t	pmatch[10];		/* main match, plus \1 to \9 */
4475	int			nmatch = lengthof(pmatch);
4476	pg_wchar   *data;
4477	size_t		data_len;
4478	int			data_pos;
4479	char	   *start_ptr;
4480	int			escape_status;
4481
4482	initStringInfo(&buf);
4483
4484	/* Convert data string to wide characters. */
4485	data = (pg_wchar *) palloc((src_text_len + 1) * sizeof(pg_wchar));
4486	data_len = pg_mb2wchar_with_len(VARDATA_ANY(src_text), data, src_text_len);
4487
4488	/* Check whether replace_text has escapes, especially regexp submatches. */
4489	escape_status = check_replace_text_has_escape(replace_text);
4490
4491	/* If no regexp submatches, we can use REG_NOSUB. */
4492	if (escape_status < 2)
4493	{
4494		cflags |= REG_NOSUB;
4495		/* Also tell pg_regexec we only want the whole-match location. */
4496		nmatch = 1;
4497	}
4498
4499	/* Prepare the regexp. */
4500	re = RE_compile_and_cache(pattern_text, cflags, collation);
4501
4502	/* start_ptr points to the data_pos'th character of src_text */
4503	start_ptr = (char *) VARDATA_ANY(src_text);
4504	data_pos = 0;
4505
4506	while (search_start <= data_len)
4507	{
4508		int			regexec_result;
4509
4510		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
4511
4512		regexec_result = pg_regexec(re,
4513									data,
4514									data_len,
4515									search_start,
4516									NULL,	/* no details */
4517									nmatch,
4518									pmatch,
4519									0);
4520
4521		if (regexec_result == REG_NOMATCH)
4522			break;
4523
4524		if (regexec_result != REG_OKAY)
4525		{
4526			char		errMsg[100];
4527
4528			pg_regerror(regexec_result, re, errMsg, sizeof(errMsg));
4529			ereport(ERROR,
4530					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_REGULAR_EXPRESSION),
4531					 errmsg("regular expression failed: %s", errMsg)));
4532		}
4533
4534		/*
4535		 * Count matches, and decide whether to replace this match.
4536		 */
4537		nmatches++;
4538		if (n > 0 && nmatches != n)
4539		{
4540			/*
4541			 * No, so advance search_start, but not start_ptr/data_pos. (Thus,
4542			 * we treat the matched text as if it weren't matched, and copy it
4543			 * to the output later.)
4544			 */
4545			search_start = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
4546			if (pmatch[0].rm_so == pmatch[0].rm_eo)
4547				search_start++;
4548			continue;
4549		}
4550
4551		/*
4552		 * Copy the text to the left of the match position.  Note we are given
4553		 * character not byte indexes.
4554		 */
4555		if (pmatch[0].rm_so - data_pos > 0)
4556		{
4557			int			chunk_len;
4558
4559			chunk_len = charlen_to_bytelen(start_ptr,
4560										   pmatch[0].rm_so - data_pos);
4561			appendBinaryStringInfo(&buf, start_ptr, chunk_len);
4562
4563			/*
4564			 * Advance start_ptr over that text, to avoid multiple rescans of
4565			 * it if the replace_text contains multiple back-references.
4566			 */
4567			start_ptr += chunk_len;
4568			data_pos = pmatch[0].rm_so;
4569		}
4570
4571		/*
4572		 * Copy the replace_text, processing escapes if any are present.
4573		 */
4574		if (escape_status > 0)
4575			appendStringInfoRegexpSubstr(&buf, replace_text, pmatch,
4576										 start_ptr, data_pos);
4577		else
4578			appendStringInfoText(&buf, replace_text);
4579
4580		/* Advance start_ptr and data_pos over the matched text. */
4581		start_ptr += charlen_to_bytelen(start_ptr,
4582										pmatch[0].rm_eo - data_pos);
4583		data_pos = pmatch[0].rm_eo;
4584
4585		/*
4586		 * If we only want to replace one occurrence, we're done.
4587		 */
4588		if (n > 0)
4589			break;
4590
4591		/*
4592		 * Advance search position.  Normally we start the next search at the
4593		 * end of the previous match; but if the match was of zero length, we
4594		 * have to advance by one character, or we'd just find the same match
4595		 * again.
4596		 */
4597		search_start = data_pos;
4598		if (pmatch[0].rm_so == pmatch[0].rm_eo)
4599			search_start++;
4600	}
4601
4602	/*
4603	 * Copy the text to the right of the last match.
4604	 */
4605	if (data_pos < data_len)
4606	{
4607		int			chunk_len;
4608
4609		chunk_len = ((char *) src_text + VARSIZE_ANY(src_text)) - start_ptr;
4610		appendBinaryStringInfo(&buf, start_ptr, chunk_len);
4611	}
4612
4613	ret_text = cstring_to_text_with_len(buf.data, buf.len);
4614	pfree(buf.data);
4615	pfree(data);
4616
4617	return ret_text;
4618}
4619
4620/*
4621 * split_part
4622 * parse input string based on provided field separator
4623 * return N'th item (1 based, negative counts from end)
4624 */
4625Datum
4626split_part(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4627{
4628	text	   *inputstring = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
4629	text	   *fldsep = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
4630	int			fldnum = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
4631	int			inputstring_len;
4632	int			fldsep_len;
4633	TextPositionState state;
4634	char	   *start_ptr;
4635	char	   *end_ptr;
4636	text	   *result_text;
4637	bool		found;
4638
4639	/* field number is 1 based */
4640	if (fldnum == 0)
4641		ereport(ERROR,
4642				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4643				 errmsg("field position must not be zero")));
4644
4645	inputstring_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(inputstring);
4646	fldsep_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(fldsep);
4647
4648	/* return empty string for empty input string */
4649	if (inputstring_len < 1)
4650		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(""));
4651
4652	/* handle empty field separator */
4653	if (fldsep_len < 1)
4654	{
4655		/* if first or last field, return input string, else empty string */
4656		if (fldnum == 1 || fldnum == -1)
4657			PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(inputstring);
4658		else
4659			PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(""));
4660	}
4661
4662	/* find the first field separator */
4663	text_position_setup(inputstring, fldsep, PG_GET_COLLATION(), &state);
4664
4665	found = text_position_next(&state);
4666
4667	/* special case if fldsep not found at all */
4668	if (!found)
4669	{
4670		text_position_cleanup(&state);
4671		/* if first or last field, return input string, else empty string */
4672		if (fldnum == 1 || fldnum == -1)
4673			PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(inputstring);
4674		else
4675			PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(""));
4676	}
4677
4678	/*
4679	 * take care of a negative field number (i.e. count from the right) by
4680	 * converting to a positive field number; we need total number of fields
4681	 */
4682	if (fldnum < 0)
4683	{
4684		/* we found a fldsep, so there are at least two fields */
4685		int			numfields = 2;
4686
4687		while (text_position_next(&state))
4688			numfields++;
4689
4690		/* special case of last field does not require an extra pass */
4691		if (fldnum == -1)
4692		{
4693			start_ptr = text_position_get_match_ptr(&state) + state.last_match_len;
4694			end_ptr = VARDATA_ANY(inputstring) + inputstring_len;
4695			text_position_cleanup(&state);
4696			PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text_with_len(start_ptr,
4697													  end_ptr - start_ptr));
4698		}
4699
4700		/* else, convert fldnum to positive notation */
4701		fldnum += numfields + 1;
4702
4703		/* if nonexistent field, return empty string */
4704		if (fldnum <= 0)
4705		{
4706			text_position_cleanup(&state);
4707			PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(""));
4708		}
4709
4710		/* reset to pointing at first match, but now with positive fldnum */
4711		text_position_reset(&state);
4712		found = text_position_next(&state);
4713		Assert(found);
4714	}
4715
4716	/* identify bounds of first field */
4717	start_ptr = VARDATA_ANY(inputstring);
4718	end_ptr = text_position_get_match_ptr(&state);
4719
4720	while (found && --fldnum > 0)
4721	{
4722		/* identify bounds of next field */
4723		start_ptr = end_ptr + state.last_match_len;
4724		found = text_position_next(&state);
4725		if (found)
4726			end_ptr = text_position_get_match_ptr(&state);
4727	}
4728
4729	text_position_cleanup(&state);
4730
4731	if (fldnum > 0)
4732	{
4733		/* N'th field separator not found */
4734		/* if last field requested, return it, else empty string */
4735		if (fldnum == 1)
4736		{
4737			int			last_len = start_ptr - VARDATA_ANY(inputstring);
4738
4739			result_text = cstring_to_text_with_len(start_ptr,
4740												   inputstring_len - last_len);
4741		}
4742		else
4743			result_text = cstring_to_text("");
4744	}
4745	else
4746	{
4747		/* non-last field requested */
4748		result_text = cstring_to_text_with_len(start_ptr, end_ptr - start_ptr);
4749	}
4750
4751	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result_text);
4752}
4753
4754/*
4755 * Convenience function to return true when two text params are equal.
4756 */
4757static bool
4758text_isequal(text *txt1, text *txt2, Oid collid)
4759{
4760	return DatumGetBool(DirectFunctionCall2Coll(texteq,
4761												collid,
4762												PointerGetDatum(txt1),
4763												PointerGetDatum(txt2)));
4764}
4765
4766/*
4767 * text_to_array
4768 * parse input string and return text array of elements,
4769 * based on provided field separator
4770 */
4771Datum
4772text_to_array(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4773{
4774	SplitTextOutputData tstate;
4775
4776	/* For array output, tstate should start as all zeroes */
4777	memset(&tstate, 0, sizeof(tstate));
4778
4779	if (!split_text(fcinfo, &tstate))
4780		PG_RETURN_NULL();
4781
4782	if (tstate.astate == NULL)
4783		PG_RETURN_ARRAYTYPE_P(construct_empty_array(TEXTOID));
4784
4785	PG_RETURN_DATUM(makeArrayResult(tstate.astate,
4786									CurrentMemoryContext));
4787}
4788
4789/*
4790 * text_to_array_null
4791 * parse input string and return text array of elements,
4792 * based on provided field separator and null string
4793 *
4794 * This is a separate entry point only to prevent the regression tests from
4795 * complaining about different argument sets for the same internal function.
4796 */
4797Datum
4798text_to_array_null(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4799{
4800	return text_to_array(fcinfo);
4801}
4802
4803/*
4804 * text_to_table
4805 * parse input string and return table of elements,
4806 * based on provided field separator
4807 */
4808Datum
4809text_to_table(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4810{
4811	ReturnSetInfo *rsi = (ReturnSetInfo *) fcinfo->resultinfo;
4812	SplitTextOutputData tstate;
4813
4814	tstate.astate = NULL;
4815	InitMaterializedSRF(fcinfo, MAT_SRF_USE_EXPECTED_DESC);
4816	tstate.tupstore = rsi->setResult;
4817	tstate.tupdesc = rsi->setDesc;
4818
4819	(void) split_text(fcinfo, &tstate);
4820
4821	return (Datum) 0;
4822}
4823
4824/*
4825 * text_to_table_null
4826 * parse input string and return table of elements,
4827 * based on provided field separator and null string
4828 *
4829 * This is a separate entry point only to prevent the regression tests from
4830 * complaining about different argument sets for the same internal function.
4831 */
4832Datum
4833text_to_table_null(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4834{
4835	return text_to_table(fcinfo);
4836}
4837
4838/*
4839 * Common code for text_to_array, text_to_array_null, text_to_table
4840 * and text_to_table_null functions.
4841 *
4842 * These are not strict so we have to test for null inputs explicitly.
4843 * Returns false if result is to be null, else returns true.
4844 *
4845 * Note that if the result is valid but empty (zero elements), we return
4846 * without changing *tstate --- caller must handle that case, too.
4847 */
4848static bool
4849split_text(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, SplitTextOutputData *tstate)
4850{
4851	text	   *inputstring;
4852	text	   *fldsep;
4853	text	   *null_string;
4854	Oid			collation = PG_GET_COLLATION();
4855	int			inputstring_len;
4856	int			fldsep_len;
4857	char	   *start_ptr;
4858	text	   *result_text;
4859
4860	/* when input string is NULL, then result is NULL too */
4861	if (PG_ARGISNULL(0))
4862		return false;
4863
4864	inputstring = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
4865
4866	/* fldsep can be NULL */
4867	if (!PG_ARGISNULL(1))
4868		fldsep = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
4869	else
4870		fldsep = NULL;
4871
4872	/* null_string can be NULL or omitted */
4873	if (PG_NARGS() > 2 && !PG_ARGISNULL(2))
4874		null_string = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(2);
4875	else
4876		null_string = NULL;
4877
4878	if (fldsep != NULL)
4879	{
4880		/*
4881		 * Normal case with non-null fldsep.  Use the text_position machinery
4882		 * to search for occurrences of fldsep.
4883		 */
4884		TextPositionState state;
4885
4886		inputstring_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(inputstring);
4887		fldsep_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(fldsep);
4888
4889		/* return empty set for empty input string */
4890		if (inputstring_len < 1)
4891			return true;
4892
4893		/* empty field separator: return input string as a one-element set */
4894		if (fldsep_len < 1)
4895		{
4896			split_text_accum_result(tstate, inputstring,
4897									null_string, collation);
4898			return true;
4899		}
4900
4901		text_position_setup(inputstring, fldsep, collation, &state);
4902
4903		start_ptr = VARDATA_ANY(inputstring);
4904
4905		for (;;)
4906		{
4907			bool		found;
4908			char	   *end_ptr;
4909			int			chunk_len;
4910
4911			CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
4912
4913			found = text_position_next(&state);
4914			if (!found)
4915			{
4916				/* fetch last field */
4917				chunk_len = ((char *) inputstring + VARSIZE_ANY(inputstring)) - start_ptr;
4918				end_ptr = NULL; /* not used, but some compilers complain */
4919			}
4920			else
4921			{
4922				/* fetch non-last field */
4923				end_ptr = text_position_get_match_ptr(&state);
4924				chunk_len = end_ptr - start_ptr;
4925			}
4926
4927			/* build a temp text datum to pass to split_text_accum_result */
4928			result_text = cstring_to_text_with_len(start_ptr, chunk_len);
4929
4930			/* stash away this field */
4931			split_text_accum_result(tstate, result_text,
4932									null_string, collation);
4933
4934			pfree(result_text);
4935
4936			if (!found)
4937				break;
4938
4939			start_ptr = end_ptr + state.last_match_len;
4940		}
4941
4942		text_position_cleanup(&state);
4943	}
4944	else
4945	{
4946		const char *end_ptr;
4947
4948		/*
4949		 * When fldsep is NULL, each character in the input string becomes a
4950		 * separate element in the result set.  The separator is effectively
4951		 * the space between characters.
4952		 */
4953		inputstring_len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(inputstring);
4954
4955		start_ptr = VARDATA_ANY(inputstring);
4956		end_ptr = start_ptr + inputstring_len;
4957
4958		while (inputstring_len > 0)
4959		{
4960			int			chunk_len = pg_mblen_range(start_ptr, end_ptr);
4961
4962			CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
4963
4964			/* build a temp text datum to pass to split_text_accum_result */
4965			result_text = cstring_to_text_with_len(start_ptr, chunk_len);
4966
4967			/* stash away this field */
4968			split_text_accum_result(tstate, result_text,
4969									null_string, collation);
4970
4971			pfree(result_text);
4972
4973			start_ptr += chunk_len;
4974			inputstring_len -= chunk_len;
4975		}
4976	}
4977
4978	return true;
4979}
4980
4981/*
4982 * Add text item to result set (table or array).
4983 *
4984 * This is also responsible for checking to see if the item matches
4985 * the null_string, in which case we should emit NULL instead.
4986 */
4987static void
4988split_text_accum_result(SplitTextOutputData *tstate,
4989						text *field_value,
4990						text *null_string,
4991						Oid collation)
4992{
4993	bool		is_null = false;
4994
4995	if (null_string && text_isequal(field_value, null_string, collation))
4996		is_null = true;
4997
4998	if (tstate->tupstore)
4999	{
5000		Datum		values[1];
5001		bool		nulls[1];
5002
5003		values[0] = PointerGetDatum(field_value);
5004		nulls[0] = is_null;
5005
5006		tuplestore_putvalues(tstate->tupstore,
5007							 tstate->tupdesc,
5008							 values,
5009							 nulls);
5010	}
5011	else
5012	{
5013		tstate->astate = accumArrayResult(tstate->astate,
5014										  PointerGetDatum(field_value),
5015										  is_null,
5016										  TEXTOID,
5017										  CurrentMemoryContext);
5018	}
5019}
5020
5021/*
5022 * array_to_text
5023 * concatenate Cstring representation of input array elements
5024 * using provided field separator
5025 */
5026Datum
5027array_to_text(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5028{
5029	ArrayType  *v = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(0);
5030	char	   *fldsep = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
5031
5032	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(array_to_text_internal(fcinfo, v, fldsep, NULL));
5033}
5034
5035/*
5036 * array_to_text_null
5037 * concatenate Cstring representation of input array elements
5038 * using provided field separator and null string
5039 *
5040 * This version is not strict so we have to test for null inputs explicitly.
5041 */
5042Datum
5043array_to_text_null(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5044{
5045	ArrayType  *v;
5046	char	   *fldsep;
5047	char	   *null_string;
5048
5049	/* returns NULL when first or second parameter is NULL */
5050	if (PG_ARGISNULL(0) || PG_ARGISNULL(1))
5051		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5052
5053	v = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(0);
5054	fldsep = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
5055
5056	/* NULL null string is passed through as a null pointer */
5057	if (!PG_ARGISNULL(2))
5058		null_string = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(2));
5059	else
5060		null_string = NULL;
5061
5062	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(array_to_text_internal(fcinfo, v, fldsep, null_string));
5063}
5064
5065/*
5066 * common code for array_to_text and array_to_text_null functions
5067 */
5068static text *
5069array_to_text_internal(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, ArrayType *v,
5070					   const char *fldsep, const char *null_string)
5071{
5072	text	   *result;
5073	int			nitems,
5074			   *dims,
5075				ndims;
5076	Oid			element_type;
5077	int			typlen;
5078	bool		typbyval;
5079	char		typalign;
5080	StringInfoData buf;
5081	bool		printed = false;
5082	char	   *p;
5083	bits8	   *bitmap;
5084	int			bitmask;
5085	int			i;
5086	ArrayMetaState *my_extra;
5087
5088	ndims = ARR_NDIM(v);
5089	dims = ARR_DIMS(v);
5090	nitems = ArrayGetNItems(ndims, dims);
5091
5092	/* if there are no elements, return an empty string */
5093	if (nitems == 0)
5094		return cstring_to_text_with_len("", 0);
5095
5096	element_type = ARR_ELEMTYPE(v);
5097	initStringInfo(&buf);
5098
5099	/*
5100	 * We arrange to look up info about element type, including its output
5101	 * conversion proc, only once per series of calls, assuming the element
5102	 * type doesn't change underneath us.
5103	 */
5104	my_extra = (ArrayMetaState *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra;
5105	if (my_extra == NULL)
5106	{
5107		fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra = MemoryContextAlloc(fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt,
5108													  sizeof(ArrayMetaState));
5109		my_extra = (ArrayMetaState *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra;
5110		my_extra->element_type = ~element_type;
5111	}
5112
5113	if (my_extra->element_type != element_type)
5114	{
5115		/*
5116		 * Get info about element type, including its output conversion proc
5117		 */
5118		get_type_io_data(element_type, IOFunc_output,
5119						 &my_extra->typlen, &my_extra->typbyval,
5120						 &my_extra->typalign, &my_extra->typdelim,
5121						 &my_extra->typioparam, &my_extra->typiofunc);
5122		fmgr_info_cxt(my_extra->typiofunc, &my_extra->proc,
5123					  fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt);
5124		my_extra->element_type = element_type;
5125	}
5126	typlen = my_extra->typlen;
5127	typbyval = my_extra->typbyval;
5128	typalign = my_extra->typalign;
5129
5130	p = ARR_DATA_PTR(v);
5131	bitmap = ARR_NULLBITMAP(v);
5132	bitmask = 1;
5133
5134	for (i = 0; i < nitems; i++)
5135	{
5136		Datum		itemvalue;
5137		char	   *value;
5138
5139		/* Get source element, checking for NULL */
5140		if (bitmap && (*bitmap & bitmask) == 0)
5141		{
5142			/* if null_string is NULL, we just ignore null elements */
5143			if (null_string != NULL)
5144			{
5145				if (printed)
5146					appendStringInfo(&buf, "%s%s", fldsep, null_string);
5147				else
5148					appendStringInfoString(&buf, null_string);
5149				printed = true;
5150			}
5151		}
5152		else
5153		{
5154			itemvalue = fetch_att(p, typbyval, typlen);
5155
5156			value = OutputFunctionCall(&my_extra->proc, itemvalue);
5157
5158			if (printed)
5159				appendStringInfo(&buf, "%s%s", fldsep, value);
5160			else
5161				appendStringInfoString(&buf, value);
5162			printed = true;
5163
5164			p = att_addlength_pointer(p, typlen, p);
5165			p = (char *) att_align_nominal(p, typalign);
5166		}
5167
5168		/* advance bitmap pointer if any */
5169		if (bitmap)
5170		{
5171			bitmask <<= 1;
5172			if (bitmask == 0x100)
5173			{
5174				bitmap++;
5175				bitmask = 1;
5176			}
5177		}
5178	}
5179
5180	result = cstring_to_text_with_len(buf.data, buf.len);
5181	pfree(buf.data);
5182
5183	return result;
5184}
5185
5186/*
5187 * Workhorse for to_bin, to_oct, and to_hex.  Note that base must be > 1 and <=
5188 * 16.
5189 */
5190static inline text *
5191convert_to_base(uint64 value, int base)
5192{
5193	const char *digits = "0123456789abcdef";
5194
5195	/* We size the buffer for to_bin's longest possible return value. */
5196	char		buf[sizeof(uint64) * BITS_PER_BYTE];
5197	char	   *const end = buf + sizeof(buf);
5198	char	   *ptr = end;
5199
5200	Assert(base > 1);
5201	Assert(base <= 16);
5202
5203	do
5204	{
5205		*--ptr = digits[value % base];
5206		value /= base;
5207	} while (ptr > buf && value);
5208
5209	return cstring_to_text_with_len(ptr, end - ptr);
5210}
5211
5212/*
5213 * Convert an integer to a string containing a base-2 (binary) representation
5214 * of the number.
5215 */
5216Datum
5217to_bin32(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5218{
5219	uint64		value = (uint32) PG_GETARG_INT32(0);
5220
5221	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(convert_to_base(value, 2));
5222}
5223Datum
5224to_bin64(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5225{
5226	uint64		value = (uint64) PG_GETARG_INT64(0);
5227
5228	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(convert_to_base(value, 2));
5229}
5230
5231/*
5232 * Convert an integer to a string containing a base-8 (oct) representation of
5233 * the number.
5234 */
5235Datum
5236to_oct32(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5237{
5238	uint64		value = (uint32) PG_GETARG_INT32(0);
5239
5240	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(convert_to_base(value, 8));
5241}
5242Datum
5243to_oct64(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5244{
5245	uint64		value = (uint64) PG_GETARG_INT64(0);
5246
5247	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(convert_to_base(value, 8));
5248}
5249
5250/*
5251 * Convert an integer to a string containing a base-16 (hex) representation of
5252 * the number.
5253 */
5254Datum
5255to_hex32(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5256{
5257	uint64		value = (uint32) PG_GETARG_INT32(0);
5258
5259	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(convert_to_base(value, 16));
5260}
5261Datum
5262to_hex64(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5263{
5264	uint64		value = (uint64) PG_GETARG_INT64(0);
5265
5266	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(convert_to_base(value, 16));
5267}
5268
5269/*
5270 * Return the size of a datum, possibly compressed
5271 *
5272 * Works on any data type
5273 */
5274Datum
5275pg_column_size(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5276{
5277	Datum		value = PG_GETARG_DATUM(0);
5278	int32		result;
5279	int			typlen;
5280
5281	/* On first call, get the input type's typlen, and save at *fn_extra */
5282	if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra == NULL)
5283	{
5284		/* Lookup the datatype of the supplied argument */
5285		Oid			argtypeid = get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 0);
5286
5287		typlen = get_typlen(argtypeid);
5288		if (typlen == 0)		/* should not happen */
5289			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", argtypeid);
5290
5291		fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra = MemoryContextAlloc(fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt,
5292													  sizeof(int));
5293		*((int *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra) = typlen;
5294	}
5295	else
5296		typlen = *((int *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra);
5297
5298	if (typlen == -1)
5299	{
5300		/* varlena type, possibly toasted */
5301		result = toast_datum_size(value);
5302	}
5303	else if (typlen == -2)
5304	{
5305		/* cstring */
5306		result = strlen(DatumGetCString(value)) + 1;
5307	}
5308	else
5309	{
5310		/* ordinary fixed-width type */
5311		result = typlen;
5312	}
5313
5314	PG_RETURN_INT32(result);
5315}
5316
5317/*
5318 * Return the compression method stored in the compressed attribute.  Return
5319 * NULL for non varlena type or uncompressed data.
5320 */
5321Datum
5322pg_column_compression(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5323{
5324	int			typlen;
5325	char	   *result;
5326	ToastCompressionId cmid;
5327
5328	/* On first call, get the input type's typlen, and save at *fn_extra */
5329	if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra == NULL)
5330	{
5331		/* Lookup the datatype of the supplied argument */
5332		Oid			argtypeid = get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 0);
5333
5334		typlen = get_typlen(argtypeid);
5335		if (typlen == 0)		/* should not happen */
5336			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", argtypeid);
5337
5338		fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra = MemoryContextAlloc(fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt,
5339													  sizeof(int));
5340		*((int *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra) = typlen;
5341	}
5342	else
5343		typlen = *((int *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra);
5344
5345	if (typlen != -1)
5346		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5347
5348	/* get the compression method id stored in the compressed varlena */
5349	cmid = toast_get_compression_id((struct varlena *)
5350									DatumGetPointer(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0)));
5351	if (cmid == TOAST_INVALID_COMPRESSION_ID)
5352		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5353
5354	/* convert compression method id to compression method name */
5355	switch (cmid)
5356	{
5357		case TOAST_PGLZ_COMPRESSION_ID:
5358			result = "pglz";
5359			break;
5360		case TOAST_LZ4_COMPRESSION_ID:
5361			result = "lz4";
5362			break;
5363		default:
5364			elog(ERROR, "invalid compression method id %d", cmid);
5365	}
5366
5367	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(result));
5368}
5369
5370/*
5371 * Return the chunk_id of the on-disk TOASTed value.  Return NULL if the value
5372 * is un-TOASTed or not on-disk.
5373 */
5374Datum
5375pg_column_toast_chunk_id(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5376{
5377	int			typlen;
5378	struct varlena *attr;
5379	struct varatt_external toast_pointer;
5380
5381	/* On first call, get the input type's typlen, and save at *fn_extra */
5382	if (fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra == NULL)
5383	{
5384		/* Lookup the datatype of the supplied argument */
5385		Oid			argtypeid = get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 0);
5386
5387		typlen = get_typlen(argtypeid);
5388		if (typlen == 0)		/* should not happen */
5389			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", argtypeid);
5390
5391		fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra = MemoryContextAlloc(fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt,
5392													  sizeof(int));
5393		*((int *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra) = typlen;
5394	}
5395	else
5396		typlen = *((int *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra);
5397
5398	if (typlen != -1)
5399		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5400
5401	attr = (struct varlena *) DatumGetPointer(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0));
5402
5403	if (!VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL_ONDISK(attr))
5404		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5405
5406	VARATT_EXTERNAL_GET_POINTER(toast_pointer, attr);
5407
5408	PG_RETURN_OID(toast_pointer.va_valueid);
5409}
5410
5411/*
5412 * string_agg - Concatenates values and returns string.
5413 *
5414 * Syntax: string_agg(value text, delimiter text) RETURNS text
5415 *
5416 * Note: Any NULL values are ignored. The first-call delimiter isn't
5417 * actually used at all, and on subsequent calls the delimiter precedes
5418 * the associated value.
5419 */
5420
5421/* subroutine to initialize state */
5422static StringInfo
5423makeStringAggState(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo)
5424{
5425	StringInfo	state;
5426	MemoryContext aggcontext;
5427	MemoryContext oldcontext;
5428
5429	if (!AggCheckCallContext(fcinfo, &aggcontext))
5430	{
5431		/* cannot be called directly because of internal-type argument */
5432		elog(ERROR, "string_agg_transfn called in non-aggregate context");
5433	}
5434
5435	/*
5436	 * Create state in aggregate context.  It'll stay there across subsequent
5437	 * calls.
5438	 */
5439	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(aggcontext);
