5440	state = makeStringInfo();
5441	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
5442
5443	return state;
5444}
5445
5446Datum
5447string_agg_transfn(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5448{
5449	StringInfo	state;
5450
5451	state = PG_ARGISNULL(0) ? NULL : (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
5452
5453	/* Append the value unless null, preceding it with the delimiter. */
5454	if (!PG_ARGISNULL(1))
5455	{
5456		text	   *value = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1);
5457		bool		isfirst = false;
5458
5459		/*
5460		 * You might think we can just throw away the first delimiter, however
5461		 * we must keep it as we may be a parallel worker doing partial
5462		 * aggregation building a state to send to the main process.  We need
5463		 * to keep the delimiter of every aggregation so that the combine
5464		 * function can properly join up the strings of two separately
5465		 * partially aggregated results.  The first delimiter is only stripped
5466		 * off in the final function.  To know how much to strip off the front
5467		 * of the string, we store the length of the first delimiter in the
5468		 * StringInfo's cursor field, which we don't otherwise need here.
5469		 */
5470		if (state == NULL)
5471		{
5472			state = makeStringAggState(fcinfo);
5473			isfirst = true;
5474		}
5475
5476		if (!PG_ARGISNULL(2))
5477		{
5478			text	   *delim = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(2);
5479
5480			appendStringInfoText(state, delim);
5481			if (isfirst)
5482				state->cursor = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(delim);
5483		}
5484
5485		appendStringInfoText(state, value);
5486	}
5487
5488	/*
5489	 * The transition type for string_agg() is declared to be "internal",
5490	 * which is a pass-by-value type the same size as a pointer.
5491	 */
5492	if (state)
5493		PG_RETURN_POINTER(state);
5494	PG_RETURN_NULL();
5495}
5496
5497/*
5498 * string_agg_combine
5499 *		Aggregate combine function for string_agg(text) and string_agg(bytea)
5500 */
5501Datum
5502string_agg_combine(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5503{
5504	StringInfo	state1;
5505	StringInfo	state2;
5506	MemoryContext agg_context;
5507
5508	if (!AggCheckCallContext(fcinfo, &agg_context))
5509		elog(ERROR, "aggregate function called in non-aggregate context");
5510
5511	state1 = PG_ARGISNULL(0) ? NULL : (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
5512	state2 = PG_ARGISNULL(1) ? NULL : (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(1);
5513
5514	if (state2 == NULL)
5515	{
5516		/*
5517		 * NULL state2 is easy, just return state1, which we know is already
5518		 * in the agg_context
5519		 */
5520		if (state1 == NULL)
5521			PG_RETURN_NULL();
5522		PG_RETURN_POINTER(state1);
5523	}
5524
5525	if (state1 == NULL)
5526	{
5527		/* We must copy state2's data into the agg_context */
5528		MemoryContext old_context;
5529
5530		old_context = MemoryContextSwitchTo(agg_context);
5531		state1 = makeStringAggState(fcinfo);
5532		appendBinaryStringInfo(state1, state2->data, state2->len);
5533		state1->cursor = state2->cursor;
5534		MemoryContextSwitchTo(old_context);
5535	}
5536	else if (state2->len > 0)
5537	{
5538		/* Combine ... state1->cursor does not change in this case */
5539		appendBinaryStringInfo(state1, state2->data, state2->len);
5540	}
5541
5542	PG_RETURN_POINTER(state1);
5543}
5544
5545/*
5546 * string_agg_serialize
5547 *		Aggregate serialize function for string_agg(text) and string_agg(bytea)
5548 *
5549 * This is strict, so we need not handle NULL input
5550 */
5551Datum
5552string_agg_serialize(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5553{
5554	StringInfo	state;
5555	StringInfoData buf;
5556	bytea	   *result;
5557
5558	/* cannot be called directly because of internal-type argument */
5559	Assert(AggCheckCallContext(fcinfo, NULL));
5560
5561	state = (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
5562
5563	pq_begintypsend(&buf);
5564
5565	/* cursor */
5566	pq_sendint(&buf, state->cursor, 4);
5567
5568	/* data */
5569	pq_sendbytes(&buf, state->data, state->len);
5570
5571	result = pq_endtypsend(&buf);
5572
5573	PG_RETURN_BYTEA_P(result);
5574}
5575
5576/*
5577 * string_agg_deserialize
5578 *		Aggregate deserial function for string_agg(text) and string_agg(bytea)
5579 *
5580 * This is strict, so we need not handle NULL input
5581 */
5582Datum
5583string_agg_deserialize(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5584{
5585	bytea	   *sstate;
5586	StringInfo	result;
5587	StringInfoData buf;
5588	char	   *data;
5589	int			datalen;
5590
5591	/* cannot be called directly because of internal-type argument */
5592	Assert(AggCheckCallContext(fcinfo, NULL));
5593
5594	sstate = PG_GETARG_BYTEA_PP(0);
5595
5596	/*
5597	 * Initialize a StringInfo so that we can "receive" it using the standard
5598	 * recv-function infrastructure.
5599	 */
5600	initReadOnlyStringInfo(&buf, VARDATA_ANY(sstate),
5601						   VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(sstate));
5602
5603	result = makeStringAggState(fcinfo);
5604
5605	/* cursor */
5606	result->cursor = pq_getmsgint(&buf, 4);
5607
5608	/* data */
5609	datalen = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(sstate) - 4;
5610	data = (char *) pq_getmsgbytes(&buf, datalen);
5611	appendBinaryStringInfo(result, data, datalen);
5612
5613	pq_getmsgend(&buf);
5614
5615	PG_RETURN_POINTER(result);
5616}
5617
5618Datum
5619string_agg_finalfn(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5620{
5621	StringInfo	state;
5622
5623	/* cannot be called directly because of internal-type argument */
5624	Assert(AggCheckCallContext(fcinfo, NULL));
5625
5626	state = PG_ARGISNULL(0) ? NULL : (StringInfo) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
5627
5628	if (state != NULL)
5629	{
5630		/* As per comment in transfn, strip data before the cursor position */
5631		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text_with_len(&state->data[state->cursor],
5632												  state->len - state->cursor));
5633	}
5634	else
5635		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5636}
5637
5638/*
5639 * Prepare cache with fmgr info for the output functions of the datatypes of
5640 * the arguments of a concat-like function, beginning with argument "argidx".
5641 * (Arguments before that will have corresponding slots in the resulting
5642 * FmgrInfo array, but we don't fill those slots.)
5643 */
5644static FmgrInfo *
5645build_concat_foutcache(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, int argidx)
5646{
5647	FmgrInfo   *foutcache;
5648	int			i;
5649
5650	/* We keep the info in fn_mcxt so it survives across calls */
5651	foutcache = (FmgrInfo *) MemoryContextAlloc(fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt,
5652												PG_NARGS() * sizeof(FmgrInfo));
5653
5654	for (i = argidx; i < PG_NARGS(); i++)
5655	{
5656		Oid			valtype;
5657		Oid			typOutput;
5658		bool		typIsVarlena;
5659
5660		valtype = get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, i);
5661		if (!OidIsValid(valtype))
5662			elog(ERROR, "could not determine data type of concat() input");
5663
5664		getTypeOutputInfo(valtype, &typOutput, &typIsVarlena);
5665		fmgr_info_cxt(typOutput, &foutcache[i], fcinfo->flinfo->fn_mcxt);
5666	}
5667
5668	fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra = foutcache;
5669
5670	return foutcache;
5671}
5672
5673/*
5674 * Implementation of both concat() and concat_ws().
5675 *
5676 * sepstr is the separator string to place between values.
5677 * argidx identifies the first argument to concatenate (counting from zero);
5678 * note that this must be constant across any one series of calls.
5679 *
5680 * Returns NULL if result should be NULL, else text value.
5681 */
5682static text *
5683concat_internal(const char *sepstr, int argidx,
5684				FunctionCallInfo fcinfo)
5685{
5686	text	   *result;
5687	StringInfoData str;
5688	FmgrInfo   *foutcache;
5689	bool		first_arg = true;
5690	int			i;
5691
5692	/*
5693	 * concat(VARIADIC some-array) is essentially equivalent to
5694	 * array_to_text(), ie concat the array elements with the given separator.
5695	 * So we just pass the case off to that code.
5696	 */
5697	if (get_fn_expr_variadic(fcinfo->flinfo))
5698	{
5699		ArrayType  *arr;
5700
5701		/* Should have just the one argument */
5702		Assert(argidx == PG_NARGS() - 1);
5703
5704		/* concat(VARIADIC NULL) is defined as NULL */
5705		if (PG_ARGISNULL(argidx))
5706			return NULL;
5707
5708		/*
5709		 * Non-null argument had better be an array.  We assume that any call
5710		 * context that could let get_fn_expr_variadic return true will have
5711		 * checked that a VARIADIC-labeled parameter actually is an array.  So
5712		 * it should be okay to just Assert that it's an array rather than
5713		 * doing a full-fledged error check.
5714		 */
5715		Assert(OidIsValid(get_base_element_type(get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, argidx))));
5716
5717		/* OK, safe to fetch the array value */
5718		arr = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(argidx);
5719
5720		/*
5721		 * And serialize the array.  We tell array_to_text to ignore null
5722		 * elements, which matches the behavior of the loop below.
5723		 */
5724		return array_to_text_internal(fcinfo, arr, sepstr, NULL);
5725	}
5726
5727	/* Normal case without explicit VARIADIC marker */
5728	initStringInfo(&str);
5729
5730	/* Get output function info, building it if first time through */
5731	foutcache = (FmgrInfo *) fcinfo->flinfo->fn_extra;
5732	if (foutcache == NULL)
5733		foutcache = build_concat_foutcache(fcinfo, argidx);
5734
5735	for (i = argidx; i < PG_NARGS(); i++)
5736	{
5737		if (!PG_ARGISNULL(i))
5738		{
5739			Datum		value = PG_GETARG_DATUM(i);
5740
5741			/* add separator if appropriate */
5742			if (first_arg)
5743				first_arg = false;
5744			else
5745				appendStringInfoString(&str, sepstr);
5746
5747			/* call the appropriate type output function, append the result */
5748			appendStringInfoString(&str,
5749								   OutputFunctionCall(&foutcache[i], value));
5750		}
5751	}
5752
5753	result = cstring_to_text_with_len(str.data, str.len);
5754	pfree(str.data);
5755
5756	return result;
5757}
5758
5759/*
5760 * Concatenate all arguments. NULL arguments are ignored.
5761 */
5762Datum
5763text_concat(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5764{
5765	text	   *result;
5766
5767	result = concat_internal("", 0, fcinfo);
5768	if (result == NULL)
5769		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5770	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
5771}
5772
5773/*
5774 * Concatenate all but first argument value with separators. The first
5775 * parameter is used as the separator. NULL arguments are ignored.
5776 */
5777Datum
5778text_concat_ws(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5779{
5780	char	   *sep;
5781	text	   *result;
5782
5783	/* return NULL when separator is NULL */
5784	if (PG_ARGISNULL(0))
5785		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5786	sep = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
5787
5788	result = concat_internal(sep, 1, fcinfo);
5789	if (result == NULL)
5790		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5791	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
5792}
5793
5794/*
5795 * Return first n characters in the string. When n is negative,
5796 * return all but last |n| characters.
5797 */
5798Datum
5799text_left(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5800{
5801	int			n = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
5802
5803	if (n < 0)
5804	{
5805		text	   *str = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
5806		const char *p = VARDATA_ANY(str);
5807		int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(str);
5808		int			rlen;
5809
5810		n = pg_mbstrlen_with_len(p, len) + n;
5811		rlen = pg_mbcharcliplen(p, len, n);
5812		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text_with_len(p, rlen));
5813	}
5814	else
5815		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(text_substring(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0), 1, n, false));
5816}
5817
5818/*
5819 * Return last n characters in the string. When n is negative,
5820 * return all but first |n| characters.
5821 */
5822Datum
5823text_right(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5824{
5825	text	   *str = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
5826	const char *p = VARDATA_ANY(str);
5827	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(str);
5828	int			n = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
5829	int			off;
5830
5831	if (n < 0)
5832		n = -n;
5833	else
5834		n = pg_mbstrlen_with_len(p, len) - n;
5835	off = pg_mbcharcliplen(p, len, n);
5836
5837	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text_with_len(p + off, len - off));
5838}
5839
5840/*
5841 * Return reversed string
5842 */
5843Datum
5844text_reverse(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5845{
5846	text	   *str = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
5847	const char *p = VARDATA_ANY(str);
5848	int			len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(str);
5849	const char *endp = p + len;
5850	text	   *result;
5851	char	   *dst;
5852
5853	result = palloc(len + VARHDRSZ);
5854	dst = (char *) VARDATA(result) + len;
5855	SET_VARSIZE(result, len + VARHDRSZ);
5856
5857	if (pg_database_encoding_max_length() > 1)
5858	{
5859		/* multibyte version */
5860		while (p < endp)
5861		{
5862			int			sz;
5863
5864			sz = pg_mblen_range(p, endp);
5865			dst -= sz;
5866			memcpy(dst, p, sz);
5867			p += sz;
5868		}
5869	}
5870	else
5871	{
5872		/* single byte version */
5873		while (p < endp)
5874			*(--dst) = *p++;
5875	}
5876
5877	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
5878}
5879
5880
5881/*
5882 * Support macros for text_format()
5883 */
5884#define TEXT_FORMAT_FLAG_MINUS	0x0001	/* is minus flag present? */
5885
5886#define ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(ptr,end_ptr) \
5887	do { \
5888		if (++(ptr) >= (end_ptr)) \
5889			ereport(ERROR, \
5890					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE), \
5891					 errmsg("unterminated format() type specifier"), \
5892					 errhint("For a single \"%%\" use \"%%%%\"."))); \
5893	} while (0)
5894
5895/*
5896 * Returns a formatted string
5897 */
5898Datum
5899text_format(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5900{
5901	text	   *fmt;
5902	StringInfoData str;
5903	const char *cp;
5904	const char *start_ptr;
5905	const char *end_ptr;
5906	text	   *result;
5907	int			arg;
5908	bool		funcvariadic;
5909	int			nargs;
5910	Datum	   *elements = NULL;
5911	bool	   *nulls = NULL;
5912	Oid			element_type = InvalidOid;
5913	Oid			prev_type = InvalidOid;
5914	Oid			prev_width_type = InvalidOid;
5915	FmgrInfo	typoutputfinfo;
5916	FmgrInfo	typoutputinfo_width;
5917
5918	/* When format string is null, immediately return null */
5919	if (PG_ARGISNULL(0))
5920		PG_RETURN_NULL();
5921
5922	/* If argument is marked VARIADIC, expand array into elements */
5923	if (get_fn_expr_variadic(fcinfo->flinfo))
5924	{
5925		ArrayType  *arr;
5926		int16		elmlen;
5927		bool		elmbyval;
5928		char		elmalign;
5929		int			nitems;
5930
5931		/* Should have just the one argument */
5932		Assert(PG_NARGS() == 2);
5933
5934		/* If argument is NULL, we treat it as zero-length array */
5935		if (PG_ARGISNULL(1))
5936			nitems = 0;
5937		else
5938		{
5939			/*
5940			 * Non-null argument had better be an array.  We assume that any
5941			 * call context that could let get_fn_expr_variadic return true
5942			 * will have checked that a VARIADIC-labeled parameter actually is
5943			 * an array.  So it should be okay to just Assert that it's an
5944			 * array rather than doing a full-fledged error check.
5945			 */
5946			Assert(OidIsValid(get_base_element_type(get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 1))));
5947
5948			/* OK, safe to fetch the array value */
5949			arr = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(1);
5950
5951			/* Get info about array element type */
5952			element_type = ARR_ELEMTYPE(arr);
5953			get_typlenbyvalalign(element_type,
5954								 &elmlen, &elmbyval, &elmalign);
5955
5956			/* Extract all array elements */
5957			deconstruct_array(arr, element_type, elmlen, elmbyval, elmalign,
5958							  &elements, &nulls, &nitems);
5959		}
5960
5961		nargs = nitems + 1;
5962		funcvariadic = true;
5963	}
5964	else
5965	{
5966		/* Non-variadic case, we'll process the arguments individually */
5967		nargs = PG_NARGS();
5968		funcvariadic = false;
5969	}
5970
5971	/* Setup for main loop. */
5972	fmt = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
5973	start_ptr = VARDATA_ANY(fmt);
5974	end_ptr = start_ptr + VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(fmt);
5975	initStringInfo(&str);
5976	arg = 1;					/* next argument position to print */
5977
5978	/* Scan format string, looking for conversion specifiers. */
5979	for (cp = start_ptr; cp < end_ptr; cp++)
5980	{
5981		int			argpos;
5982		int			widthpos;
5983		int			flags;
5984		int			width;
5985		Datum		value;
5986		bool		isNull;
5987		Oid			typid;
5988
5989		/*
5990		 * If it's not the start of a conversion specifier, just copy it to
5991		 * the output buffer.
5992		 */
5993		if (*cp != '%')
5994		{
5995			appendStringInfoCharMacro(&str, *cp);
5996			continue;
5997		}
5998
5999		ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(cp, end_ptr);
6000
6001		/* Easy case: %% outputs a single % */
6002		if (*cp == '%')
6003		{
6004			appendStringInfoCharMacro(&str, *cp);
6005			continue;
6006		}
6007
6008		/* Parse the optional portions of the format specifier */
6009		cp = text_format_parse_format(cp, end_ptr,
6010									  &argpos, &widthpos,
6011									  &flags, &width);
6012
6013		/*
6014		 * Next we should see the main conversion specifier.  Whether or not
6015		 * an argument position was present, it's known that at least one
6016		 * character remains in the string at this point.  Experience suggests
6017		 * that it's worth checking that that character is one of the expected
6018		 * ones before we try to fetch arguments, so as to produce the least
6019		 * confusing response to a mis-formatted specifier.
6020		 */
6021		if (strchr("sIL", *cp) == NULL)
6022			ereport(ERROR,
6023					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6024					 errmsg("unrecognized format() type specifier \"%.*s\"",
6025							pg_mblen_range(cp, end_ptr), cp),
6026					 errhint("For a single \"%%\" use \"%%%%\".")));
6027
6028		/* If indirect width was specified, get its value */
6029		if (widthpos >= 0)
6030		{
6031			/* Collect the specified or next argument position */
6032			if (widthpos > 0)
6033				arg = widthpos;
6034			if (arg >= nargs)
6035				ereport(ERROR,
6036						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6037						 errmsg("too few arguments for format()")));
6038
6039			/* Get the value and type of the selected argument */
6040			if (!funcvariadic)
6041			{
6042				value = PG_GETARG_DATUM(arg);
6043				isNull = PG_ARGISNULL(arg);
6044				typid = get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, arg);
6045			}
6046			else
6047			{
6048				value = elements[arg - 1];
6049				isNull = nulls[arg - 1];
6050				typid = element_type;
6051			}
6052			if (!OidIsValid(typid))
6053				elog(ERROR, "could not determine data type of format() input");
6054
6055			arg++;
6056
6057			/* We can treat NULL width the same as zero */
6058			if (isNull)
6059				width = 0;
6060			else if (typid == INT4OID)
6061				width = DatumGetInt32(value);
6062			else if (typid == INT2OID)
6063				width = DatumGetInt16(value);
6064			else
6065			{
6066				/* For less-usual datatypes, convert to text then to int */
6067				char	   *str;
6068
6069				if (typid != prev_width_type)
6070				{
6071					Oid			typoutputfunc;
6072					bool		typIsVarlena;
6073
6074					getTypeOutputInfo(typid, &typoutputfunc, &typIsVarlena);
6075					fmgr_info(typoutputfunc, &typoutputinfo_width);
6076					prev_width_type = typid;
6077				}
6078
6079				str = OutputFunctionCall(&typoutputinfo_width, value);
6080
6081				/* pg_strtoint32 will complain about bad data or overflow */
6082				width = pg_strtoint32(str);
6083
6084				pfree(str);
6085			}
6086		}
6087
6088		/* Collect the specified or next argument position */
6089		if (argpos > 0)
6090			arg = argpos;
6091		if (arg >= nargs)
6092			ereport(ERROR,
6093					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6094					 errmsg("too few arguments for format()")));
6095
6096		/* Get the value and type of the selected argument */
6097		if (!funcvariadic)
6098		{
6099			value = PG_GETARG_DATUM(arg);
6100			isNull = PG_ARGISNULL(arg);
6101			typid = get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, arg);
6102		}
6103		else
6104		{
6105			value = elements[arg - 1];
6106			isNull = nulls[arg - 1];
6107			typid = element_type;
6108		}
6109		if (!OidIsValid(typid))
6110			elog(ERROR, "could not determine data type of format() input");
6111
6112		arg++;
6113
6114		/*
6115		 * Get the appropriate typOutput function, reusing previous one if
6116		 * same type as previous argument.  That's particularly useful in the
6117		 * variadic-array case, but often saves work even for ordinary calls.
6118		 */
6119		if (typid != prev_type)
6120		{
6121			Oid			typoutputfunc;
6122			bool		typIsVarlena;
6123
6124			getTypeOutputInfo(typid, &typoutputfunc, &typIsVarlena);
6125			fmgr_info(typoutputfunc, &typoutputfinfo);
6126			prev_type = typid;
6127		}
6128
6129		/*
6130		 * And now we can format the value.
6131		 */
6132		switch (*cp)
6133		{
6134			case 's':
6135			case 'I':
6136			case 'L':
6137				text_format_string_conversion(&str, *cp, &typoutputfinfo,
6138											  value, isNull,
6139											  flags, width);
6140				break;
6141			default:
6142				/* should not get here, because of previous check */
6143				ereport(ERROR,
6144						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6145						 errmsg("unrecognized format() type specifier \"%.*s\"",
6146								pg_mblen_range(cp, end_ptr), cp),
6147						 errhint("For a single \"%%\" use \"%%%%\".")));
6148				break;
6149		}
6150	}
6151
6152	/* Don't need deconstruct_array results anymore. */
6153	if (elements != NULL)
6154		pfree(elements);
6155	if (nulls != NULL)
6156		pfree(nulls);
6157
6158	/* Generate results. */
6159	result = cstring_to_text_with_len(str.data, str.len);
6160	pfree(str.data);
6161
6162	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
6163}
6164
6165/*
6166 * Parse contiguous digits as a decimal number.
6167 *
6168 * Returns true if some digits could be parsed.
6169 * The value is returned into *value, and *ptr is advanced to the next
6170 * character to be parsed.
6171 *
6172 * Note parsing invariant: at least one character is known available before
6173 * string end (end_ptr) at entry, and this is still true at exit.
6174 */
6175static bool
6176text_format_parse_digits(const char **ptr, const char *end_ptr, int *value)
6177{
6178	bool		found = false;
6179	const char *cp = *ptr;
6180	int			val = 0;
6181
6182	while (*cp >= '0' && *cp <= '9')
6183	{
6184		int8		digit = (*cp - '0');
6185
6186		if (unlikely(pg_mul_s32_overflow(val, 10, &val)) ||
6187			unlikely(pg_add_s32_overflow(val, digit, &val)))
6188			ereport(ERROR,
6189					(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
6190					 errmsg("number is out of range")));
6191		ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(cp, end_ptr);
6192		found = true;
6193	}
6194
6195	*ptr = cp;
6196	*value = val;
6197
6198	return found;
6199}
6200
6201/*
6202 * Parse a format specifier (generally following the SUS printf spec).
6203 *
6204 * We have already advanced over the initial '%', and we are looking for
6205 * [argpos][flags][width]type (but the type character is not consumed here).
6206 *
6207 * Inputs are start_ptr (the position after '%') and end_ptr (string end + 1).
6208 * Output parameters:
6209 *	argpos: argument position for value to be printed.  -1 means unspecified.
6210 *	widthpos: argument position for width.  Zero means the argument position
6211 *			was unspecified (ie, take the next arg) and -1 means no width
6212 *			argument (width was omitted or specified as a constant).
6213 *	flags: bitmask of flags.
6214 *	width: directly-specified width value.  Zero means the width was omitted
6215 *			(note it's not necessary to distinguish this case from an explicit
6216 *			zero width value).
6217 *
6218 * The function result is the next character position to be parsed, ie, the
6219 * location where the type character is/should be.
6220 *
6221 * Note parsing invariant: at least one character is known available before
6222 * string end (end_ptr) at entry, and this is still true at exit.
6223 */
6224static const char *
6225text_format_parse_format(const char *start_ptr, const char *end_ptr,
6226						 int *argpos, int *widthpos,
6227						 int *flags, int *width)
6228{
6229	const char *cp = start_ptr;
6230	int			n;
6231
6232	/* set defaults for output parameters */
6233	*argpos = -1;
6234	*widthpos = -1;
6235	*flags = 0;
6236	*width = 0;
6237
6238	/* try to identify first number */
6239	if (text_format_parse_digits(&cp, end_ptr, &n))
6240	{
6241		if (*cp != '$')
6242		{
6243			/* Must be just a width and a type, so we're done */
6244			*width = n;
6245			return cp;
6246		}
6247		/* The number was argument position */
6248		*argpos = n;
6249		/* Explicit 0 for argument index is immediately refused */
6250		if (n == 0)
6251			ereport(ERROR,
6252					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6253					 errmsg("format specifies argument 0, but arguments are numbered from 1")));
6254		ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(cp, end_ptr);
6255	}
6256
6257	/* Handle flags (only minus is supported now) */
6258	while (*cp == '-')
6259	{
6260		*flags |= TEXT_FORMAT_FLAG_MINUS;
6261		ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(cp, end_ptr);
6262	}
6263
6264	if (*cp == '*')
6265	{
6266		/* Handle indirect width */
6267		ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(cp, end_ptr);
6268		if (text_format_parse_digits(&cp, end_ptr, &n))
6269		{
6270			/* number in this position must be closed by $ */
6271			if (*cp != '$')
6272				ereport(ERROR,
6273						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6274						 errmsg("width argument position must be ended by \"$\"")));
6275			/* The number was width argument position */
6276			*widthpos = n;
6277			/* Explicit 0 for argument index is immediately refused */
6278			if (n == 0)
6279				ereport(ERROR,
6280						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6281						 errmsg("format specifies argument 0, but arguments are numbered from 1")));
6282			ADVANCE_PARSE_POINTER(cp, end_ptr);
6283		}
6284		else
6285			*widthpos = 0;		/* width's argument position is unspecified */
6286	}
6287	else
6288	{
6289		/* Check for direct width specification */
6290		if (text_format_parse_digits(&cp, end_ptr, &n))
6291			*width = n;
6292	}
6293
6294	/* cp should now be pointing at type character */
6295	return cp;
6296}
6297
6298/*
6299 * Format a %s, %I, or %L conversion
6300 */
6301static void
6302text_format_string_conversion(StringInfo buf, char conversion,
6303							  FmgrInfo *typOutputInfo,
6304							  Datum value, bool isNull,
6305							  int flags, int width)
6306{
6307	char	   *str;
6308
6309	/* Handle NULL arguments before trying to stringify the value. */
6310	if (isNull)
6311	{
6312		if (conversion == 's')
6313			text_format_append_string(buf, "", flags, width);
6314		else if (conversion == 'L')
6315			text_format_append_string(buf, "NULL", flags, width);
6316		else if (conversion == 'I')
6317			ereport(ERROR,
6318					(errcode(ERRCODE_NULL_VALUE_NOT_ALLOWED),
6319					 errmsg("null values cannot be formatted as an SQL identifier")));
6320		return;
6321	}
6322
6323	/* Stringify. */
6324	str = OutputFunctionCall(typOutputInfo, value);
6325
6326	/* Escape. */
6327	if (conversion == 'I')
6328	{
6329		/* quote_identifier may or may not allocate a new string. */
6330		text_format_append_string(buf, quote_identifier(str), flags, width);
6331	}
6332	else if (conversion == 'L')
6333	{
6334		char	   *qstr = quote_literal_cstr(str);
6335
6336		text_format_append_string(buf, qstr, flags, width);
6337		/* quote_literal_cstr() always allocates a new string */
6338		pfree(qstr);
6339	}
6340	else
6341		text_format_append_string(buf, str, flags, width);
6342
6343	/* Cleanup. */
6344	pfree(str);
6345}
6346
6347/*
6348 * Append str to buf, padding as directed by flags/width
6349 */
6350static void
6351text_format_append_string(StringInfo buf, const char *str,
6352						  int flags, int width)
6353{
6354	bool		align_to_left = false;
6355	int			len;
6356
6357	/* fast path for typical easy case */
6358	if (width == 0)
6359	{
6360		appendStringInfoString(buf, str);
6361		return;
6362	}
6363
6364	if (width < 0)
6365	{
6366		/* Negative width: implicit '-' flag, then take absolute value */
6367		align_to_left = true;
6368		/* -INT_MIN is undefined */
6369		if (width <= INT_MIN)
6370			ereport(ERROR,
6371					(errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
6372					 errmsg("number is out of range")));
6373		width = -width;
6374	}
6375	else if (flags & TEXT_FORMAT_FLAG_MINUS)
6376		align_to_left = true;
6377
6378	len = pg_mbstrlen(str);
6379	if (align_to_left)
6380	{
6381		/* left justify */
6382		appendStringInfoString(buf, str);
6383		if (len < width)
6384			appendStringInfoSpaces(buf, width - len);
6385	}
6386	else
6387	{
6388		/* right justify */
6389		if (len < width)
6390			appendStringInfoSpaces(buf, width - len);
6391		appendStringInfoString(buf, str);
6392	}
6393}
6394
6395/*
6396 * text_format_nv - nonvariadic wrapper for text_format function.
6397 *
6398 * note: this wrapper is necessary to pass the sanity check in opr_sanity,
6399 * which checks that all built-in functions that share the implementing C
6400 * function take the same number of arguments.
6401 */
6402Datum
6403text_format_nv(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6404{
6405	return text_format(fcinfo);
6406}
6407
6408/*
6409 * Helper function for Levenshtein distance functions. Faster than memcmp(),
6410 * for this use case.
6411 */
6412static inline bool
6413rest_of_char_same(const char *s1, const char *s2, int len)
6414{
6415	while (len > 0)
6416	{
6417		len--;
6418		if (s1[len] != s2[len])
6419			return false;
6420	}
6421	return true;
6422}
6423
6424/* Expand each Levenshtein distance variant */
6425#include "levenshtein.c"
6426#define LEVENSHTEIN_LESS_EQUAL
6427#include "levenshtein.c"
6428
6429
6430/*
6431 * The following *ClosestMatch() functions can be used to determine whether a
6432 * user-provided string resembles any known valid values, which is useful for
6433 * providing hints in log messages, among other things.  Use these functions
6434 * like so:
6435 *
6436 *		initClosestMatch(&state, source_string, max_distance);
6437 *
6438 *		for (int i = 0; i < num_valid_strings; i++)
6439 *			updateClosestMatch(&state, valid_strings[i]);
6440 *
6441 *		closestMatch = getClosestMatch(&state);
6442 */
6443
6444/*
6445 * Initialize the given state with the source string and maximum Levenshtein
6446 * distance to consider.
6447 */
6448void
6449initClosestMatch(ClosestMatchState *state, const char *source, int max_d)
6450{
6451	Assert(state);
6452	Assert(max_d >= 0);
6453
6454	state->source = source;
6455	state->min_d = -1;
6456	state->max_d = max_d;
6457	state->match = NULL;
6458}
6459
6460/*
6461 * If the candidate string is a closer match than the current one saved (or
6462 * there is no match saved), save it as the closest match.
6463 *
6464 * If the source or candidate string is NULL, empty, or too long, this function
6465 * takes no action.  Likewise, if the Levenshtein distance exceeds the maximum
6466 * allowed or more than half the characters are different, no action is taken.
6467 */
6468void
6469updateClosestMatch(ClosestMatchState *state, const char *candidate)
6470{
6471	int			dist;
6472
6473	Assert(state);
6474
6475	if (state->source == NULL || state->source[0] == '\0' ||
6476		candidate == NULL || candidate[0] == '\0')
6477		return;
6478
6479	/*
6480	 * To avoid ERROR-ing, we check the lengths here instead of setting
6481	 * 'trusted' to false in the call to varstr_levenshtein_less_equal().
6482	 */
6483	if (strlen(state->source) > MAX_LEVENSHTEIN_STRLEN ||
6484		strlen(candidate) > MAX_LEVENSHTEIN_STRLEN)
6485		return;
6486
6487	dist = varstr_levenshtein_less_equal(state->source, strlen(state->source),
6488										 candidate, strlen(candidate), 1, 1, 1,
6489										 state->max_d, true);
6490	if (dist <= state->max_d &&
6491		dist <= strlen(state->source) / 2 &&
6492		(state->min_d == -1 || dist < state->min_d))
6493	{
6494		state->min_d = dist;
6495		state->match = candidate;
6496	}
6497}
6498
6499/*
6500 * Return the closest match.  If no suitable candidates were provided via
6501 * updateClosestMatch(), return NULL.
6502 */
6503const char *
6504getClosestMatch(ClosestMatchState *state)
6505{
6506	Assert(state);
6507
6508	return state->match;
6509}
6510
6511
6512/*
6513 * Unicode support
6514 */
6515
6516static UnicodeNormalizationForm
6517unicode_norm_form_from_string(const char *formstr)
6518{
6519	UnicodeNormalizationForm form = -1;
6520
6521	/*
6522	 * Might as well check this while we're here.
6523	 */
6524	if (GetDatabaseEncoding() != PG_UTF8)
6525		ereport(ERROR,
6526				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
6527				 errmsg("Unicode normalization can only be performed if server encoding is UTF8")));
6528
6529	if (pg_strcasecmp(formstr, "NFC") == 0)
6530		form = UNICODE_NFC;
6531	else if (pg_strcasecmp(formstr, "NFD") == 0)
6532		form = UNICODE_NFD;
6533	else if (pg_strcasecmp(formstr, "NFKC") == 0)
6534		form = UNICODE_NFKC;
6535	else if (pg_strcasecmp(formstr, "NFKD") == 0)
6536		form = UNICODE_NFKD;
6537	else
6538		ereport(ERROR,
6539				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6540				 errmsg("invalid normalization form: %s", formstr)));
6541
6542	return form;
6543}
6544
6545/*
6546 * Returns version of Unicode used by Postgres in "major.minor" format (the
6547 * same format as the Unicode version reported by ICU). The third component
6548 * ("update version") never involves additions to the character repertoire and
6549 * is unimportant for most purposes.
6550 *
6551 * See: https://unicode.org/versions/
6552 */
6553Datum
6554unicode_version(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6555{
6556	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(PG_UNICODE_VERSION));
6557}
6558
6559/*
6560 * Returns version of Unicode used by ICU, if enabled; otherwise NULL.
6561 */
6562Datum
6563icu_unicode_version(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6564{
6565#ifdef USE_ICU
6566	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(U_UNICODE_VERSION));
6567#else
6568	PG_RETURN_NULL();
6569#endif
6570}
6571
6572/*
6573 * Check whether the string contains only assigned Unicode code
6574 * points. Requires that the database encoding is UTF-8.
6575 */
6576Datum
6577unicode_assigned(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6578{
6579	text	   *input = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
6580	unsigned char *p;
6581	int			size;
6582
6583	if (GetDatabaseEncoding() != PG_UTF8)
6584		ereport(ERROR,
6585				(errmsg("Unicode categorization can only be performed if server encoding is UTF8")));
6586
6587	/* convert to pg_wchar */
6588	size = pg_mbstrlen_with_len(VARDATA_ANY(input), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(input));
6589	p = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(input);
6590	for (int i = 0; i < size; i++)
6591	{
6592		pg_wchar	uchar = utf8_to_unicode(p);
6593		int			category = unicode_category(uchar);
6594
6595		if (category == PG_U_UNASSIGNED)
6596			PG_RETURN_BOOL(false);
6597
6598		p += pg_utf_mblen(p);
6599	}
6600
6601	PG_RETURN_BOOL(true);
6602}
6603
6604Datum
6605unicode_normalize_func(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6606{
6607	text	   *input = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
6608	char	   *formstr = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
6609	UnicodeNormalizationForm form;
6610	size_t		size;
6611	pg_wchar   *input_chars;
6612	pg_wchar   *output_chars;
6613	unsigned char *p;
6614	text	   *result;
6615	size_t		i;
6616
6617	form = unicode_norm_form_from_string(formstr);
6618
6619	/* convert to pg_wchar */
6620	size = pg_mbstrlen_with_len(VARDATA_ANY(input), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(input));
6621	input_chars = palloc_array(pg_wchar, size + 1);
6622	p = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(input);
6623	for (i = 0; i < size; i++)
6624	{
6625		input_chars[i] = utf8_to_unicode(p);
6626		p += pg_utf_mblen(p);
6627	}
6628	input_chars[i] = (pg_wchar) '\0';
6629	Assert((char *) p == VARDATA_ANY(input) + VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(input));
6630
6631	/* action */
6632	output_chars = unicode_normalize(form, input_chars);
6633
6634	/* convert back to UTF-8 string */
6635	size = 0;
6636	for (pg_wchar *wp = output_chars; *wp; wp++)
6637	{
6638		unsigned char buf[4];
6639
6640		unicode_to_utf8(*wp, buf);
6641		size += pg_utf_mblen(buf);
6642	}
6643
6644	result = palloc(size + VARHDRSZ);
6645	SET_VARSIZE(result, size + VARHDRSZ);
6646
6647	p = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(result);
6648	for (pg_wchar *wp = output_chars; *wp; wp++)
6649	{
6650		unicode_to_utf8(*wp, p);
6651		p += pg_utf_mblen(p);
6652	}
6653	Assert((char *) p == (char *) result + size + VARHDRSZ);
6654
6655	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
6656}
6657
6658/*
6659 * Check whether the string is in the specified Unicode normalization form.
6660 *
6661 * This is done by converting the string to the specified normal form and then
6662 * comparing that to the original string.  To speed that up, we also apply the
6663 * "quick check" algorithm specified in UAX #15, which can give a yes or no
6664 * answer for many strings by just scanning the string once.
6665 *
6666 * This function should generally be optimized for the case where the string
6667 * is in fact normalized.  In that case, we'll end up looking at the entire
6668 * string, so it's probably not worth doing any incremental conversion etc.
6669 */
6670Datum
6671unicode_is_normalized(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6672{
6673	text	   *input = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
6674	char	   *formstr = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
6675	UnicodeNormalizationForm form;
6676	size_t		size;
6677	pg_wchar   *input_chars;
6678	pg_wchar   *output_chars;
6679	unsigned char *p;
6680	size_t		i;
6681	UnicodeNormalizationQC quickcheck;
6682	size_t		output_size;
6683	bool		result;
6684
6685	form = unicode_norm_form_from_string(formstr);
6686
6687	/* convert to pg_wchar */
6688	size = pg_mbstrlen_with_len(VARDATA_ANY(input), VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(input));
6689	input_chars = palloc_array(pg_wchar, size + 1);
6690	p = (unsigned char *) VARDATA_ANY(input);
6691	for (i = 0; i < size; i++)
6692	{
6693		input_chars[i] = utf8_to_unicode(p);
6694		p += pg_utf_mblen(p);
6695	}
6696	input_chars[i] = (pg_wchar) '\0';
6697	Assert((char *) p == VARDATA_ANY(input) + VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(input));
6698
6699	/* quick check (see UAX #15) */
6700	quickcheck = unicode_is_normalized_quickcheck(form, input_chars);
6701	if (quickcheck == UNICODE_NORM_QC_YES)
6702		PG_RETURN_BOOL(true);
6703	else if (quickcheck == UNICODE_NORM_QC_NO)
6704		PG_RETURN_BOOL(false);
6705
6706	/* normalize and compare with original */
6707	output_chars = unicode_normalize(form, input_chars);
6708
6709	output_size = 0;
6710	for (pg_wchar *wp = output_chars; *wp; wp++)
6711		output_size++;
6712
6713	result = (size == output_size) &&
6714		(memcmp(input_chars, output_chars, size * sizeof(pg_wchar)) == 0);
6715
6716	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
6717}
6718
6719/*
6720 * Check if first n chars are hexadecimal digits
6721 */
6722static bool
6723isxdigits_n(const char *instr, size_t n)
6724{
6725	for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i++)
6726		if (!isxdigit((unsigned char) instr[i]))
6727			return false;
6728
6729	return true;
6730}
6731
6732static unsigned int
6733hexval(unsigned char c)
6734{
6735	if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
6736		return c - '0';
6737	if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
6738		return c - 'a' + 0xA;
6739	if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
6740		return c - 'A' + 0xA;
6741	elog(ERROR, "invalid hexadecimal digit");
6742	return 0;					/* not reached */
6743}
6744
6745/*
6746 * Translate string with hexadecimal digits to number
6747 */
6748static unsigned int
6749hexval_n(const char *instr, size_t n)
6750{
6751	unsigned int result = 0;
6752
6753	for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i++)
6754		result += hexval(instr[i]) << (4 * (n - i - 1));
6755
6756	return result;
6757}
6758
6759/*
6760 * Replaces Unicode escape sequences by Unicode characters
6761 */
6762Datum
6763unistr(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6764{
6765	text	   *input_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
6766	char	   *instr;
6767	int			len;
6768	StringInfoData str;
6769	text	   *result;
6770	pg_wchar	pair_first = 0;
6771	char		cbuf[MAX_UNICODE_EQUIVALENT_STRING + 1];
6772
6773	instr = VARDATA_ANY(input_text);
6774	len = VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR(input_text);
6775
6776	initStringInfo(&str);
6777
6778	while (len > 0)
6779	{
6780		if (instr[0] == '\\')
6781		{
6782			if (len >= 2 &&
6783				instr[1] == '\\')
6784			{
6785				if (pair_first)
6786					goto invalid_pair;
6787				appendStringInfoChar(&str, '\\');
6788				instr += 2;
6789				len -= 2;
6790			}
6791			else if ((len >= 5 && isxdigits_n(instr + 1, 4)) ||
6792					 (len >= 6 && instr[1] == 'u' && isxdigits_n(instr + 2, 4)))
6793			{
6794				pg_wchar	unicode;
6795				int			offset = instr[1] == 'u' ? 2 : 1;
6796
6797				unicode = hexval_n(instr + offset, 4);
6798
6799				if (!is_valid_unicode_codepoint(unicode))
6800					ereport(ERROR,
6801							errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6802							errmsg("invalid Unicode code point: %04X", unicode));
6803
6804				if (pair_first)
6805				{
6806					if (is_utf16_surrogate_second(unicode))
6807					{
6808						unicode = surrogate_pair_to_codepoint(pair_first, unicode);
6809						pair_first = 0;
6810					}
6811					else
6812						goto invalid_pair;
6813				}
6814				else if (is_utf16_surrogate_second(unicode))
6815					goto invalid_pair;
6816
6817				if (is_utf16_surrogate_first(unicode))
6818					pair_first = unicode;
6819				else
6820				{
6821					pg_unicode_to_server(unicode, (unsigned char *) cbuf);
6822					appendStringInfoString(&str, cbuf);
6823				}
6824
6825				instr += 4 + offset;
6826				len -= 4 + offset;
6827			}
6828			else if (len >= 8 && instr[1] == '+' && isxdigits_n(instr + 2, 6))
6829			{
6830				pg_wchar	unicode;
6831
6832				unicode = hexval_n(instr + 2, 6);
6833
6834				if (!is_valid_unicode_codepoint(unicode))
6835					ereport(ERROR,
6836							errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6837							errmsg("invalid Unicode code point: %04X", unicode));
6838
6839				if (pair_first)
6840				{
6841					if (is_utf16_surrogate_second(unicode))
6842					{
6843						unicode = surrogate_pair_to_codepoint(pair_first, unicode);
6844						pair_first = 0;
6845					}
6846					else
6847						goto invalid_pair;
6848				}
6849				else if (is_utf16_surrogate_second(unicode))
6850					goto invalid_pair;
6851
6852				if (is_utf16_surrogate_first(unicode))
6853					pair_first = unicode;
6854				else
6855				{
6856					pg_unicode_to_server(unicode, (unsigned char *) cbuf);
6857					appendStringInfoString(&str, cbuf);
6858				}
6859
6860				instr += 8;
6861				len -= 8;
6862			}
6863			else if (len >= 10 && instr[1] == 'U' && isxdigits_n(instr + 2, 8))
6864			{
6865				pg_wchar	unicode;
6866
6867				unicode = hexval_n(instr + 2, 8);
6868
6869				if (!is_valid_unicode_codepoint(unicode))
6870					ereport(ERROR,
6871							errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
6872							errmsg("invalid Unicode code point: %04X", unicode));
6873
6874				if (pair_first)
6875				{
6876					if (is_utf16_surrogate_second(unicode))
6877					{
6878						unicode = surrogate_pair_to_codepoint(pair_first, unicode);
6879						pair_first = 0;
6880					}
6881					else
6882						goto invalid_pair;
6883				}
6884				else if (is_utf16_surrogate_second(unicode))
6885					goto invalid_pair;
6886
6887				if (is_utf16_surrogate_first(unicode))
6888					pair_first = unicode;
6889				else
6890				{
6891					pg_unicode_to_server(unicode, (unsigned char *) cbuf);
6892					appendStringInfoString(&str, cbuf);
6893				}
6894
6895				instr += 10;
6896				len -= 10;
6897			}
6898			else
6899				ereport(ERROR,
6900						(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
6901						 errmsg("invalid Unicode escape"),
6902						 errhint("Unicode escapes must be \\XXXX, \\+XXXXXX, \\uXXXX, or \\UXXXXXXXX.")));
6903		}
6904		else
6905		{
6906			if (pair_first)
6907				goto invalid_pair;
6908
6909			appendStringInfoChar(&str, *instr++);
6910			len--;
6911		}
6912	}
6913
6914	/* unfinished surrogate pair? */
6915	if (pair_first)
6916		goto invalid_pair;
6917
6918	result = cstring_to_text_with_len(str.data, str.len);
6919	pfree(str.data);
6920
6921	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(result);
6922
6923invalid_pair:
6924	ereport(ERROR,
6925			(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
6926			 errmsg("invalid Unicode surrogate pair")));
6927	PG_RETURN_NULL();			/* keep compiler quiet */
6928}
